data.text: I found the likely implementation areas. Next I’m reading the manifest and resolver code together so I can map the new field to actual serialization and the slice logic before editing anything.

data.output:
package registry

import (
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestLoadPackageManifest(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	manifest := `name: sample-plugin
version: 1.2.3
description: Sample plugin
type: plugin
source: https://example.com/sample-plugin
api_version: 1
install:
  root:
    source: .
    target: .ddx/plugins/sample-plugin
  skills:
    - source: skills/
      target: .agents/skills/
keywords:
  - sample
`
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, "package.yaml"), []byte(manifest), 0o644))

	pkg, issues, err := LoadPackageManifest(dir)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Empty(t, issues)
	require.NotNil(t, pkg)

	assert.Equal(t, "sample-plugin", pkg.Name)
	assert.Equal(t, "1.2.3", pkg.Version)
	assert.Equal(t, "Sample plugin", pkg.Description)
	assert.Equal(t, PackageTypePlugin, pkg.Type)
	assert.Equal(t, "https://example.com/sample-plugin", pkg.Source)
	assert.Equal(t, SupportedPackageAPIVersion, pkg.APIVersion)
	require.NotNil(t, pkg.Install.Root)
	assert.Equal(t, ".", pkg.Install.Root.Source)
	assert.Equal(t, ".ddx/plugins/sample-plugin", pkg.Install.Root.Target)
	require.Len(t, pkg.Install.Skills, 1)
	assert.Equal(t, "skills/", pkg.Install.Skills[0].Source)
	assert.Equal(t, ".agents/skills/", pkg.Install.Skills[0].Target)
}

func TestLoadPackageManifestReportsUnsupportedAPIVersion(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	manifest := `name: sample-plugin
version: 1.2.3
description: Sample plugin
type: plugin
source: https://example.com/sample-plugin
api_version: 2
`
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, "package.yaml"), []byte(manifest), 0o644))

	pkg, issues, err := LoadPackageManifest(dir)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotNil(t, pkg)
	require.NotEmpty(t, issues)
	assert.True(t, strings.Contains(issues[0].Error(), "unsupported `api_version`"))
}

func TestLoadPackageManifestWithFallbackUsesFallbackWhenManifestMissing(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	fallback := &Package{
		Name:        "sample-plugin",
		Version:     "1.2.3",
		Description: "Sample plugin",
		Type:        PackageTypePlugin,
		Source:      "https://example.com/sample-plugin",
	}

	pkg, missing, issues, err := LoadPackageManifestWithFallback(dir, fallback)
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(err))
	assert.True(t, missing)
	require.Empty(t, issues)
	assert.Same(t, fallback, pkg)
}

func TestLoadPackageManifestPreservesUnknownKeys(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	manifest := `name: future-plugin
version: 1.0.0
description: Manifest that uses keys introduced after this DDx build
type: plugin
source: https://example.com/future-plugin
api_version: 1
install:
  root:
    source: .
    target: .ddx/plugins/future-plugin
# Unknown top-level keys a future api_version might introduce.
hooks:
  pre-install: scripts/pre.sh
  post-install: scripts/post.sh
signatures:
  release:
    algo: ed25519
    key: AAA...ZZZ
future_flag: true
`
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, "package.yaml"), []byte(manifest), 0o644))

	pkg, issues, err := LoadPackageManifest(dir)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Empty(t, issues)
	require.NotNil(t, pkg)

	// Known keys parse as before.
	assert.Equal(t, "future-plugin", pkg.Name)
	assert.Equal(t, PackageTypePlugin, pkg.Type)

	// Unknown keys survive on Extra, keyed by their original YAML name.
	require.NotNil(t, pkg.Extra)
	assert.Contains(t, pkg.Extra, "hooks")
	assert.Contains(t, pkg.Extra, "signatures")
	assert.Contains(t, pkg.Extra, "future_flag")
	assert.Equal(t, true, pkg.Extra["future_flag"])
	if hooks, ok := pkg.Extra["hooks"].(map[string]any); ok {
		assert.Equal(t, "scripts/pre.sh", hooks["pre-install"])
		assert.Equal(t, "scripts/post.sh", hooks["post-install"])
	} else {
		t.Fatalf("expected hooks to be map[string]any, got %T", pkg.Extra["hooks"])
	}

}

func TestLoadPackageManifestNoUnknownKeysYieldsNilExtra(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	manifest := `name: plain-plugin
version: 1.0.0
description: No unknown top-level keys
type: plugin
source: https://example.com/plain
api_version: 1
`
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, "package.yaml"), []byte(manifest), 0o644))
	pkg, issues, err := LoadPackageManifest(dir)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Empty(t, issues)
	require.NotNil(t, pkg)
	assert.Nil(t, pkg.Extra, "no unknown keys should leave Extra nil")
}

./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:150:{"id":"corpus-a4c8352b5628","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"correctness: compaction can summarize away the active user prompt when prefix exhausts keep budget","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: compaction/compactor.go:48; compaction/config.go:74; loop.go:89\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The fix subtracts non-persisted system-prompt tokens directly from KeepRecentTokens and clamps the result to zero. When the system prompt is larger than the keep budget, FindCutPoint now returns len(messages), so CompactMessages summarizes the entire live history, including the current user prompt. A repro with ContextWindow=25, KeepRecentTokens=5, and an 80-character system prompt shows the final upstream-service call receives only the system prompt plus the generated summary; the explicit user prompt  is no longer present as a real user message. SD-006 says session prefix messages are excluded from preserved user messages and shows post-compaction ordering that retains recent user messages after the system prompt. Compacting away the active prompt lets the summarizer paraphrase or omit the instruction that the next route-target turn is supposed to answer.\nSuggested fix: Account for system-prompt tokens in the compaction trigger and post-compaction fit calculation without letting them reduce the preserved recent-conversation budget to zero. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:186:{"id":"corpus-cbc4ba9c4924","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed focused bead with small acceptance surface","title":"Prevent execute-loop from launching open epics by default","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\nAlpha validation run from /Users/erik/Projects/helix using `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json` against a live HELIX queue. Evidence includes the generated execution bundle `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7`, worktree `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-helix-fef22846-20260411T011535-bbda19d7`, and the current execute-loop implementation in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`.\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\n`ddx agent execute-loop` selected and launched an open `epic` bead from the HELIX execution queue instead of a bounded executable bead.\n\n## Reproduction\n1. In `/Users/erik/Projects/helix`, run `ddx bead ready --json --execution`.\n2. Observe the first candidate is `helix-fef22846`, `issue_type=epic`, title `Audit deleted HELIX artifact types for restoration or retirement`.\n3. Run `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json`.\n4. Observe DDx creates `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-helix-fef22846-20260411T011535-bbda19d7` and `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7/manifest.json` with `bead_id=helix-fef22846`.\n\n## Observed gap\n`ReadyExecution()` currently treats any open, unblocked bead as execution-ready unless `execution-eligible=false`, `superseded-by`, or cooldown metadata says otherwise. `execute-loop` then takes the first candidate without a stronger structural execution screen. That behavior is weaker than the API-001 execute-bead supervisor contract, which says the loop should run a generic execution-ready validator against ordered candidates before launch.\n\n## Why this matters\nOpen epics represent umbrella planning/decomposition work, not necessarily a bounded workspace transform. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:190:{"id":"corpus-d1868d0e5457","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"Update DDx to ddx-agent v0.1.1","description":"Upstream `DocumentDrivenDX/agent` now has point release `v0.1.1`. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:211:{"id":"corpus-e3d55d44b044","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"Add ddx agent workers command — native live worker status with current bead","description":"There is no CLI command to see which workers are running and which bead each is executing. The WorkerManager in cli/internal/server/workers.go already tracks this (WorkerRecord.CurrentBead at line 51, WorkerRecord.State, WorkerRecord.Runner, WorkerRecord.Route-target, WorkerRecord.Attempts/Successes/Failures) but it is only accessible through the server API. Local --local workers are not tracked by the server at all.\n\nThis bead adds ddx agent workers as a top-level CLI subcommand. It must not depend on worktrunk (wt) or any external tool.\n\n## Required changes\n\n### 1. New command: ddx agent workers (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go)\nRegister alongside the existing ddx agent log command. Flags:\n  --json         emit raw JSON array\n  --watch        re-render every 2s (like watch -n2) until Ctrl-C\n  --project      filter to a specific project root (default: CWD project)\n\n### 2. Data sources (two paths)\nA. Server-submitted workers: GET http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/agent/workers — deserialize []WorkerRecord from the server. Already implemented in cli/internal/server/workers.go List() / HTTP handler.\nB. Local --local workers: the server does not track these. Detect them by:\n   1. Reading .ddx/workers/*/status.json files in the project root (server writes these even for local workers when they self-register — confirm in workers.go; if not, fall back to scanning active execute-bead worktrees)\n   2. Scanning .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-* directories (from ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go line 403) and reading the manifest.json inside each to extract bead_id, attempt_id, runner, route-target, started_at\n\n### 3. Local worker detection via manifest scan\nFor each directory matching .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-*:\n  - Read .ddx/executions/{attemptID}/manifest.json (path encoded in worktree name suffix)\n  - Check if worktree is still active (git worktree list --porcelain)\n  - Read execute-loop-heartbeat-at from bead.Extra (requires ddx-7651506a) if available\n  - Derive synthetic WorkerRecord from manifest fields\n\n### 4. Table output (default)\nColumns: STATE | BEAD | TITLE (truncated 40 chars) | RUNNER | ROUTE-TARGET | ELAPSED | ATTEMPTS\n- STATE: running (green), exited (dim), failed (red)\n- ELAPSED: human-readable (e.g., 4m32s)\n- If current bead title unavailable (local worker pre-manifest), show bead ID\n\n### 5. Merge and deduplicate\nWhen both server and manifest sources are available, deduplicate by worker_id or attempt_id. Server record takes precedence over manifest-derived record for the same worker.\n\n## Key files\n- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go — add newAgentWorkersCommand(), register in newAgentCommand()\n- cli/internal/server/workers.go — WorkerRecord struct (lines 31-54), List API\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go — ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant (line 403), manifest shape (lines 726-771)\n- cli/internal/agent/types.go — SessionEntry Correlation map (bead_id field)","acceptance":"ddx agent workers prints a table with STATE, BEAD ID, TITLE, RUNNER, ROUTE-TARGET, ELAPSED, ATTEMPTS columns. Local --local workers are detected via manifest scan of .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-* directories. --json flag emits raw JSON. --watch re-renders every 2s. A worker executing bead axon-abc123 shows that bead ID and its title (truncated). An idle system (no active workers) prints 'no active workers' rather than an empty table. No dependency on worktrunk or any external binary beyond git.","labels":["ux","execute-loop","observability","cli"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-9060c8b2","title":"Add ddx agent workers command — native live worker status with current bead","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"There is no CLI command to see which workers are running and which bead each is executing. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:243:{"id":"corpus-2fb07647a39d","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"decomposable","rationale":"no_changes/review text says bead needs splitting","title":"exec: tier-based auto-escalation driven by resource discovery","description":"The CLI should not require the user to enumerate runner/route-target pairs. The tool already knows which tiers exist (route-target-catalog.yaml: cheap/standard/smart) and which runneres are configured. Given a live probe of each upstream-service's health at attempt time, the loop can auto-escalate on its own.\n\n## Principle\n\nDiscovery over configuration. The user shouldn't write a fallback chain — the tool should figure it out.\n\n## Default policy (no flags)\n\n- Start at **cheap** tier, execute the bead\n- If merge succeeds AND post-review (gap #1) APPROVES → close\n- If anything fails (execution_failed, no_changes after N retries, post_run_check_failed, REQUEST_CHANGES from review) → escalate one tier and retry\n- Chain: cheap → standard → smart → exhausted (preserve, flag for human)\n- Review agent runs at max(impl_tier+1, smart). Never review cheap with cheap.\n\n## Resource discovery at attempt time\n\n- Before picking a concrete runner/route-target for a tier, probe each candidate upstream-service (local inference host, API key validity, network reachability)\n- If upstream-service returns 502 / 500 / timeout / auth-rejected within a short budget, mark it unhealthy with a cooldown and try the next upstream-service in the tier\n- If every upstream-service in a tier is unhealthy, skip the tier and escalate\n- Per-upstream-service health state is process-local and visible via a new 'ddx agent upstream-services' sub-command (ddx-09ea1884 Visibility 3)\n\n## Rare overrides\n\n- --min-tier standard (skip cheap — useful for hard beads known to need it)\n- --max-tier standard (never reach smart — cost control)\n- --runner \u003cname\u003e / --route-target \u003cname\u003e (debug override; disables escalation)\n\n## Surface\n\nDefault invocation becomes:\n\n  ddx agent execute-loop --once\n\nEverything else is implicit. Review, escalation, and resource discovery are on-by-default.\n\n## Events\n\nEach attempt appends an execute-bead event: { runner, route-target, tier, upstream-service, probe_result, attempt_status, review_status }. The event stream shows exactly why a tier/upstream-service was picked or skipped.\n\n## Context\n\nObserved during axon ADR-018 loop:\n- bragi was down (502) — I manually probed with curl and switched to vidar-coder. Tool should do this.\n- qwen3-coder-next merged but opus review rejected — I manually re-invoked with sonnet. Tool should escalate automatically.\n- Resulting CLI invocation in my notes was too complex (—-fallback-chain a/b,c/d,e/f --post-review g/h). User feedback: too many knobs. Reach for a default that just works.\n\n## Acceptance criteria replaced with discovery-driven ones (see accept field)","acceptance":"- [ ] execute-loop with zero flags escalates across cheap → standard → smart on failure\n- [ ] Review agent auto-selects a tier \u003e= impl tier, defaulting to smart\n- [ ] Upstream-service health probe runs at attempt time; unhealthy upstream-services skip cleanly with cooldown\n- [ ] Each attempt records tier + upstream-service + probe_result + outcome on the bead\n- [ ] Integration test: cheap upstream-service 502 + standard succeeds → bead closes with escalation trail visible\n- [ ] --min-tier / --max-tier overrides work and are documented\n- [ ] --runner / --route-target debug overrides disable escalation\n- [ ] Zero-flag 'ddx agent execute-loop' is the documented happy path","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:feature","gap:review-loop"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-dc231144","title":"exec: tier-based auto-escalation driven by resource discovery","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"The CLI should not require the user to enumerate runner/route-target pairs. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:251:{"id":"corpus-926b27e7332a","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"decomposable","rationale":"repeated reviewer blocks cite decomposition scope","title":"Bead detail GraphQL resolver scans every project and every bead on each request","description":"## Observed\n\nOpening a bead in the sidepane on `/nodes/.../projects/.../beads?q=test` takes 5+ seconds. The beads list renders quickly; the delay is on single-bead lookup.\n\n## Root cause\n\n`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver.go:93-100` — the `Bead(id)` resolver calls `r.State.GetBeadSnapshots(\"\", \"\", \"\", \"\")` with no filters, which at `cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:49-105` walks **every project**, constructs a `bead.Store` per project, calls `ReadAll()` (full directory scan + JSONL parse of every bead file), then iterates the result in Go to find one matching ID.\n\nSo every sidepane open = `O(projects × beads_per_project)` disk reads + parses, for a lookup that should be `O(1)` or `O(log n)`.\n\n`bead.Store` already exposes `Get(id)` (`cli/internal/bead/store.go:450`), but the resolver doesn't use it — nor does it know which project the bead lives in, because the GraphQL schema exposes `bead(id:)` without a project scope.\n\n## Proposed direction (not prescriptive)\n\nAny of these would resolve it; pick based on measurements:\n\n1. **Project scope the query.** Change schema to `bead(projectId:, id:)` (or accept a composite global id) and call `Store.Get` directly.\n2. **Maintain an in-memory index** `beadID → projectID` refreshed on bead mutations and on a debounced filesystem watcher; resolver does one map lookup + one `Store.Get`.\n3. **Short-TTL snapshot cache** behind `GetBeadSnapshots`, invalidated on bead writes.\n\nBefore picking: add a baseline measurement runner (see acceptance) so we know the actual p50/p95 before and after, not just anecdote.","acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer browsing the web UI beads view, I want clicking a bead to open its detail pane within ~200ms on a repo with hundreds of beads across several projects, so the UI feels interactive.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. A benchmark/runner exists (Go benchmark or scripted GraphQL client) that measures end-to-end `bead(id:)` latency against a fixture repo with ≥ 5 projects and ≥ 500 beads total. Output is a p50/p95 number, not a pass/fail bool.\n2. Baseline is recorded in the bead notes (actual number measured on the current `main` binary at the time of fix).\n3. After the fix, p95 for `bead(id:)` on the fixture is ≤ 50ms in-process / ≤ 200ms over HTTP on a dev laptop.\n4. The fix does not introduce a stale-read regression: writing a bead via `ddx bead update` and then fetching it via GraphQL returns the new value on the next request (verified by a test that does write-then-read).\n5. Concurrency-safe: an integration test spawns N concurrent `bead(id:)` queries interleaved with `bead update` mutations and asserts no panics, no torn reads, and consistent responses.\n6. Playwright e2e: open `/nodes/.../beads`, click a bead row, assert the sidepane shows the bead title within 1s (generous budget; guards against regressions not the p95).","labels":["feat-008","feat-004","perf","graphql"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-ad0db8fd","title":"Bead detail GraphQL resolver scans every project and every bead on each request","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","description":"## Observed\n\nOpening a bead in the sidepane on `/nodes/.../projects/.../beads?q=test` takes 5+ seconds. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:259:{"id":"corpus-ef0f9a2702f5","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"decomposable","rationale":"no_changes/review text says bead needs splitting","title":"execute-bead: agents make edits but skip git commit; iteration commits land only execution evidence","description":"Observed today across 5 attempts on the axon repo (claude/sonnet, claude/opus, codex/gpt-5.4-mini): the agent does real work in the worktree (multiple Edit tool calls touching real source files) but never runs git commit. Result: iteration commit is built by ddx-land-coordinator with ONLY execution-evidence files (.ddx/executions/\u003cid\u003e/result.json, manifest.json, usage.json), no actual code or doc changes. The post-merge codex reviewer correctly flags BLOCK with rationale 'change set only adds execution metadata files'.\n\nConcrete evidence (axon repo, 2026-04-29):\n\n  Attempt: 20260429T235353-d14f6b91 (axon-80979cb8 / A1 sub-bead)\n  Runner: claude / route-target: sonnet\n  Duration: 817 sec, cost $3.61, 132 tool calls\n  Tool breakdown: 79 Bash, 34 Read, 11 Grep, 8 Edit\n  Edit targets: ui/src/lib/api.ts, ui/src/lib/policy-evaluator.ts,\n    ui/src/routes/.../policies/+page.svelte, ui/tests/e2e/link-policy-retry.spec.ts\n  Iteration commit (ddx-land-coordinator): contains exactly 2 files —\n    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/result.json (23 lines)\n    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/usage.json (9 lines)\n  Net: zero actual edits landed despite $3.61 of route-target time spent making them.\n\nThe same pattern appeared on:\n  20260429T235406-20eeaf2c (axon-db7a6d0a, claude/sonnet, $0.57, 4.2 min, BLOCK)\n  20260429T190657-fa91f49b (axon-ab2e52e0, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.08, 24s, stalled)\n  20260429T190634-579df880 (axon-80979cb8, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.12, 104s, BLOCK)\n  20260429T190646-5aa5b46e (axon-db7a6d0a, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.06, 25s, BLOCK)\n\nTotal burn today: ~$4.50 across 5 attempts on 3 beads, 0 closed.\n\nThe execute-bead prompt template tells the agent to commit:\n  '4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with git add \u003cspecific-paths\u003e...'\n\nSo agent self-commit is the contract. But:\n\n(a) When typecheck/lint/test fails, the agent is told to fix or write no_changes_rationale.txt — but apparently many agents are bailing silently without writing the rationale either, so the runner sees a clean worktree (no diff staged or committed) and treats it as 'agent made no commits'. The iteration then carries only the auto-generated execution evidence.\n\n(b) There may also be a path where the agent commits but a pre-commit hook silently rejects it. Have not confirmed; the worktree was reaped before I could inspect.\n\nThe issue spans multiple runneres (claude, codex via embedded openrouter), so it is not specific to one upstream-service's commit semantics. Either:\n  - The runner should reject 'agent made no commits AND no no_changes_rationale.txt' as a separate failure class instead of silently letting ddx-land-coordinator land an empty iteration\n  - The agent prompt should make rationale-writing on incomplete work non-optional with stronger language\n  - The runner should run a final-stage check 'did the agent produce ANY signal — commit OR rationale — and if not, treat as runner-execution-failed and surface to the operator'","acceptance":"AC1. When an execute-bead session exits without committing in the worktree AND without writing .ddx/executions/\u003cid\u003e/no_changes_rationale.txt, the runner records a NEW dedicated status (e.g. 'agent_silent_exit' or 'no_evidence_produced') instead of letting ddx-land-coordinator land an iteration containing only execution evidence.\n\nAC2. The codex reviewer's BLOCK rationale ('change set only adds execution metadata files') becomes redundant — the runner catches this case before the reviewer is even invoked, saving a redundant review pass and its cost.\n\nAC3. The execute-bead prompt template is updated so the rationale-on-incomplete-work instruction is harder to skip — ideally a tool the agent must invoke (e.g. SubmitNoChangesRationale tool) rather than 'write a file and exit'.\n\nAC4. When the agent did run Edit tool calls but the worktree has no commit, the runner logs a warning naming each file the agent edited that is now uncommitted, so operators can recover the work or diagnose the silent-commit-failure.\n\nAC5. Documentation in docs/ explains the agent-self-commit contract, the rationale-file fallback, and the new 'agent_silent_exit' classification.","labels":["area:agent","area:runner","area:execute-bead","kind:correctness"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-4e0a4a59","title":"execute-bead: agents make edits but skip git commit; iteration commits land only execution evidence","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","description":"Observed today across 5 attempts on the axon repo (claude/sonnet, claude/opus, codex/gpt-5.4-mini): the agent does real work in the worktree (multiple Edit tool calls touching real source files) but never runs git commit. WorkerManager runs a supervisor goroutine that, every minute, inspects each `workerHandle`.\n2. If `time.Since(record.StartedAt) \u003e WatchdogDeadline` (default e.g. 6h) AND `record.CurrentAttempt` has not transitioned in `StallDeadline` (default e.g. 1h), escalate:\n   - Send SIGTERM to the worker's process group (requires surfacing PID, aligned with ddx-b808df39).\n   - After 30s grace, send SIGKILL.\n   - Mark record.State = \"reaped\"; update claimed bead state so the claim is not leaked.\n3. Emit a `bead.reaped` event on the tracker with the full timeline so post-mortems are easy.\n\n## Non-goals\n\n- Per-bead runtime tuning; defaults are enough.\n- Replacing ddx-b808df39 — this is defense-in-depth, not duplication. ddx-b808df39 is operator-initiated; this bead is system-initiated.\n\n## Dependencies\n\n- ddx-b808df39 surfaces PID in `ddx agent workers --json` output; this bead ./.agents/skills/.ddx/beads.jsonl:3:{"acceptance":"- [ ] `RunOptions` accepts a caller ctx, or `Runner.Run`/`Runner.RunAgent` accept ctx explicitly\n- [ ] The internal cancel is derived from the caller ctx (no `context.Background()` seeds inside Run/RunAgent)\n- [ ] `agent.ExecuteBead` takes ctx and passes it down\n- [ ] `server.WorkerManager.Stop(id)` cancels the running agent provider call within 2s in a unit test (fake provider blocks on ctx)\n- [ ] Regression test: runner with a canceled ctx returns promptly (\u003c500ms) regardless of mocked provider latency","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T04:51:41Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"9b044bcebc981533d2d48d4ffd79a91743dee884","created_at":"2026-04-18T04:41:24.464808955Z","description":"## Root cause RC1 from ddx-0a651925\n\n`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:239` (Runner.Run) and `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:163` (Runner.RunAgent) both call `context.WithCancel(context.Background())`, discarding any context from the caller. `RunOptions` exposes no ctx field. `agent.ExecuteBead` likewise takes no ctx, so `server.WorkerManager.runWorker`'s ctx never reaches the agent call.\n\nAs a result, `WorkerManager.Stop(id)` (server/workers.go:648) cancels only the worker goroutine; the embedded `agentlib.Run` call (and any subprocess it launched) keeps running until SIGKILL. This is the structural defect behind 33–142h hung workers.\n\n## Scope\n\n1. Add `Context context.Context` to `RunOptions` (or change signatures to `Run(ctx, opts)`). Default to `context.Background()` when nil.\n2. Derive the internal ctx from the caller's rather than `context.Background()`.\n3. Thread ctx through `agent.ExecuteBead` → `runner.Run` → `agentlib.Run` → provider HTTP.\n4. Update `server.WorkerManager.runWorker`'s `singleTierAttempt` closure to pass `ctx` into `agent.ExecuteBead`.\n\n## Non-goals\n\n- New wall-clock timeout logic (covered by sibling RC2 bead)\n- Operator-facing stop command (covered by ./.agents/skills/.ddx/beads.jsonl:5:{"acceptance":"- [ ] New default wall-clock deadline (e.g. 3h) applied to both `OSExecutor.ExecuteInDir` and `Runner.RunAgent`\n- [ ] Wall-clock timeout fires even when stdout/stderr/agent events are flowing (unit test: fake provider emits 1 event/sec for \u003e deadline, expect cancel at deadline ± 1s)\n- [ ] Idle timeout behaviour preserved (unit test: no activity for idle timeout → fire)\n- [ ] Result.json distinguishes `timeout: wall-clock` from `timeout: idle`\n- [ ] Documented in types.go (DefaultWallClockMS constant with comment)","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T05:15:25Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"fff2c86240b98cd687a9dc9879926b17411db3f5","created_at":"2026-04-18T04:41:40.15942901Z","description":"## Root cause RC2 from ddx-0a651925\n\nThe `DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000` (2h) in `cli/internal/agent/types.go:363` is advertised as a hard cap but implemented as an *idle timeout*:\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/executor.go:154-178` resets `time.NewTimer(idleTimeout)` on every stdout/stderr byte.\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:264-285` resets on every non-compaction agentlib event.\n\nA provider that emits any heartbeat, retry line, or periodic event defeats the timer indefinitely — enabling the 33–142h hangs observed on agent projects.\n\nOnly the claude streaming path (`cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:373-384`) uses a true wall-clock deadline via `time.After(timeout)`. That path is why bundle `20260415T015231-c2ffa9ba` fired at exactly 2h 0m 0.098s — and nothing else does.\n\n## Scope\n\n1. Add an absolute wall-clock deadline in addition to (not replacing) the idle timeout.\n2. Default the wall-clock bound to something operators can trust — e.g. 3x `DefaultTimeoutMS` or a new `DefaultWallClockMS`.\n3. Apply in: `OSExecutor.ExecuteInDir` (subprocess path) and `Runner.RunAgent` (embedded agent path).\n4. Reflect the wall-clock error in result.json as a distinct `error: \"wall-clock deadline exceeded after Xh\"` so it is not confused with idle timeout.\n\n## Non-goals\n\n- Lowering or removing the existing idle timeout.\n- Per-provider tuning (can be follow-up).","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"claude\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-18T05:29:50.370737575Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent ./.agents/skills/.ddx/beads.jsonl:35:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer reviewing how different harness/provider/model combinations are performing, I expect the Efficacy page to load quickly on a workspace with tens of thousands of sessions, and I expect the rows to reflect every agent invocation — not just those tied to closed beads.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Source change.** `EfficacyRows` resolver reads from the sharded sessions index (`.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`, per `ddx-2ceb02fa`). Bead-event read paths (`efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot`'s event loop, `attemptsFromEvidence`, `efficacyMemo`) are removed in the same PR — no dead code retained.\n\n2. **Schema parity.** Row shape and field names stay as they are today (rowKey, harness, provider, model, attempts, successes, successRate, medianInputTokens, medianOutputTokens, medianDurationMs, medianCostUsd, warning). UI does not need to change. Optional new filters (`since`, `until`, `projectId`) are added as nullable args with sensible defaults.\n\n3. **Fixture + baseline recorded.** Fixture seeds ≥10,000 session index rows across 2+ monthly shards with ≥10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups. Baseline latencies (before any optimization beyond source change) written to bead notes with hardware + go-version footer.\n\n4. **First-pass perf targets.**\n   - All-time rollup on 10k-row, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.\n   - Last-30-days rollup on same fixture: p95 ≤ 60ms / ≤ 200ms HTTP.\n   - Stretch fixture (50k rows × 24 shards): all-time p95 ≤ 400ms in-process / ≤ 1000ms HTTP, or **user is consulted** before choosing a new substrate.\n\n5. **DB-decision protocol (per user directive 2026-04-22).**\n   - If targets in (4) cannot be met with in-place optimization (streaming aggregation, per-shard memoization with correct invalidation, selective field read), the PR author posts a short proposal in the bead notes: measured achievable numbers, proposed substrate from {axon, sqlite, postgres}, and migration cost. Implementation of that substrate requires explicit user approval before landing.\n\n6. **Completeness test.** A fixture seeds sessions that would not appear under the old bead-event path (open-bead sessions, `ddx agent run` sessions, benchmark sessions). The new resolver returns rows for each of them. A matching integration test on the old code path (kept as a golden-output comparison for one cycle) asserts the new output is a strict superset in the cases it should be.\n\n7. **Freshness.** A session written via the sessions index writer (from `ddx-2ceb02fa`) is visible in the Efficacy query within 2s. Integration test writes a row and queries the aggregation.\n\n8. **Playwright smoke.** `/efficacy` opens, renders the rollup table, and clicks into `EfficacyAttempts` for one row — all within normal navigation budgets. Uses the 10k-row fixture; asserts the table has ≥5 rows rendered.\n\n9. **Cross-reference.** This bead is a dependent of `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index must exist). If `ddx-2ceb02fa` is not yet landed when this work starts, bead notes record the stub / interim source chosen and the timeline for switching over.","claimed-at":"2026-04-23T05:29:15Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3704155","closing_commit_sha":"9ee64108c2ca79e70a9dd5971a72665865469f2a","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:47:04.443214848Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-0a33bc5f","depends_on_id":"ddx-2ceb02fa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-23T01:21:53Z"}],"description":"## Observed\n\nThe Efficacy page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../efficacy` loads very slowly. User feedback: efficacy should really be \"an aggregation of sessions grouped by provider / harness / model\" — which is not what the current implementation does.\n\n## Ground truth (current implementation)\n\n`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:676-833` —\n\n- `EfficacyRows` builds its rollup from **bead evidence events** (`kind:routing` and `kind:cost` on every closed bead), not from sessions.\n- Computation path: `store.ReadAll()` → for every closed bead, read its event file → filter for routing/cost → per-(harness, provider, model) aggregate.\n- There is a cache (`efficacyMemo`), keyed by a fingerprint that itself walks every event on every closed bead to compute event count + max timestamp. So cache validation costs almost as much as cache rebuild.\n- Cost shape: `O(closed_beads × events_per_bead)` on every request (cache miss) and even on cache hit the fingerprint walk happens.\n\n## Why this is wrong (per user feedback)\n\n- Efficacy is a sessions-view concept: \"how is harness/provider/model X doing?\" Sessions are the natural row. Bead events are a lossy, indirect proxy — they only exist for *closed* beads, they duplicate session state, and they force coupling between the efficacy feature and bead lifecycle.\n- The bead-event path misses: open beads with in-flight sessions, sessions that didn't close a bead, benchmark/quorum sessions, non-execute-bead invocations (e.g., `ddx agent run`).\n- The sessions feed is already being reworked into a sharded pointer index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`). Efficacy should read from that index — single source of truth.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\n### Part 1 — Reshape the data source\n\n- Rebuild `EfficacyRows` as an aggregation over `sessions.index` (the sharded index from `ddx-2ceb02fa`), not over bead events.\n- Grouping key: `(harness, provider, model)` — optionally filterable by project, bead, label, time range.\n- Metrics per row: attempts (count), successes (count + rate), median input/output tokens, median duration, median cost. Identical shape to today; only the source changes.\n- Shard awareness: time-range queries map to a shard set (same pattern as `ddx-2ceb02fa`); the default view reads current + previous shard.\n\n### Part 2 — Fixture + baseline (honor user's 1,000+ record floor)\n\n- Perf harness target: aggregate ≥**1,000 session rows** (≥ 10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups) within the budget below.\n- Stretch fixture: 50,000 session rows across 24 monthly shards. Measures steady-state behavior as the index grows.\n- Baseline captured in the bead notes before any optimization.\n\n### Part 3 — Perf targets (first pass, in-place)\n\n- Default Efficacy view (all-time) on a 10k-session, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.\n- Date-filtered view (last 30 days, 1 shard): p95 ≤ 60ms in-process / ≤ 200ms HTTP.\n- If in-place optimization (scope, pushdown, memoize-per-shard, streaming aggregation) **cannot hit these numbers** on the 50k stretch fixture, **do not silently change substrate** — raise the decision with the user. Candidate backends named by the user: axon, sqlite, postgres. Bring a recommendation + rationale, not a fait accompli.\n\n### Part 4 — Drop the bead-event read path\n\n- After the rewrite, remove `efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot` event iteration, `attemptsFromEvidence`, and `efficacyMemo`. They become dead weight once the source moves.\n- Evidence events on beads remain (they're bead audit trail, not efficacy data); nothing in bead lifecycle changes.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- The `comparisons` / \"compare arms\" feature on this page (tracked separately — see companion bead).\n- Project-scope filtering UI (the backend support can be added now; the UI widget is a follow-up).\n- Cross-machine / federated efficacy rollups.","efficacy_baseline":"recorded","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"omlx-vidar-1235\",\"resolved_model\":\"qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:59:51.254212382Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=omlx-vidar-1235 model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"tier=cheap harness=agent model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b probe=ok\nagent: provider error: openai: POST \"http://vidar:1235/v1/chat/completions\": 404 Not Found {\"message\":\"Model 'qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b' not found. The feature is a useful escape hatch, but shipping it without understanding WHY workers hang means we'll keep needing the escape hatch.\n\nOpus fresh-eyes review (2026-04-18) flagged: 'Hung workers for 33h–142h are an operational defect independent of root cause' — the feature bead is justified, but a SIBLING root-cause investigation is missing from the portfolio.\n\n## Observed\n\nThe referenced 33h and 142h hang durations in ddx-b808df39 imply multiple incidents where an execute-bead worker process remained in-progress past any reasonable upper bound for a single bead. Needs to investigate:\n\n1. Is the worker process alive but stuck in a tool call (bash subprocess that never returns)?\n2. Is it alive but mid-LLM-call with no timeout (provider hang + no client-side deadline)?\n3. Is it dead but the bead's claimed_at never cleared (tracker-side leak)?\n4. Is the worktree locked or fsync'd in a way that prevents cleanup?\n\n## Fix direction\n\nThis bead is an INVESTIGATION, not a fix. Deliverable is a short report that:\n\n1. Reproduces or observes at least one hang incident and captures:\n   - ps / pgrep state of the worker process tree\n   - stack traces via 'go tool pprof' against the worker (if alive)\n   - Timeline from .ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/embedded/*.jsonl (last event type + timestamp)\n   - tracker claimed_at and retry_after state\n\n2. Classifies the hang into one of the four categories above (or a new category).\n\n3. Files concrete follow-up beads for each identified root cause:\n   - Tool subprocess timeout if bash hangs\n   - LLM client deadline if provider hangs\n   - Claim expiry / heartbeat if tracker-side leak\n   - File-lock / worktree cleanup if fs-side\n\n4. Recommends whether ddx-b808df39's stop/kill mechanism is sufficient as mitigation or needs additional primitives.\n\n## Relation to ddx-b808df39\n\nddx-b808df39 ships the OPERATOR TOOL to stop a hung worker. This bead finds the BUG that causes hangs. Both are needed; ship ddx-b808df39 first if the operator-tool is more urgent, then this investigation.\n\n## Non-goals\n\n- Fixing the root cause in this bead. Scope is investigation + follow-up bead filing.\n- Preventing all possible hangs (impossible). Scope is the actually-observed hang Updated\\n    ddx-b808df39 notes to cross-reference this investigation so the\\n    operator stop/kill tool tracks the underlying fixes.\\n\\ndiff --git a/.ddx/executions/20260418T043148-1346e8a3/incident_report.md b/.ddx/executions/20260418T043148-1346e8a3/incident_report.md\\nnew file mode 100644\\nindex 00000000..5e8547cf\\n--- /dev/null\\n+++ b/.ddx/executions/20260418T043148-1346e8a3/incident_report.md\\n@@ -0,0 +1,193 @@\\n+# Investigation: root cause of 33–142h hung execute-bead workers\\n+\\n+**Bead:** ddx-0a651925 (investigates; sibling to ddx-b808df39)\\n+**Attempt:** 20260418T043148-1346e8a3\\n+**Scope:** Source-code + execution-bundle analysis. No live hung worker was available\\n+in this isolated worktree to capture live `ps`/`pprof`, so the incident evidence is\\n+drawn from `.ddx/executions/*` bundles and the worker/runner source path.\\n+\\n+---\\n+\\n+## 1. Observed evidence (from `.ddx/executions/`)\\n+\\n+| Metric                                               | Count / value                                        |\\n+| ---------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |\\n+| Total attempt bundles                                | 328                                                  |\\n+| Bundles with `result.json`                           | 291 (89%)                                            |\\n+| Bundles with `manifest.json` but **no** `result.json` | 37 (11%) — executions whose worker never finalized  |\\n+\\n+Top five longest *finalized* attempts:\\n+\\n+| `duration_ms` | Wall time    | `detail`                                                                    | Bundle                          |\\n+| ------------- | ------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------- |\\n+| 7,720,908     | **2h 8m**    | `agent: compaction stuck: 50 consecutive failed compaction attempts`        | 20260416T230316-efbf91c5        |\\n+| 7,200,098     | **2h 0m**    | `timeout after 2h0m0s` (claude streaming wall-clock fired)                  | 20260415T015231-c2ffa9ba        |\\n+| 6,764,927     | **1h 53m**   | success (agent harness, qwen)                                               | 20260416T230315-f9f46822        |\\n+| 5,649,987     | **1h 34m**   | compaction stuck                                                            | 20260416T230313-e193942b        |\\n+| 4,284,347     | **1h 11m**   | compaction stuck                                                            | 20260415T103422-bb3a45fa        |\\n+\\n+**Key observation:** the 33h / 142h hang durations cited in ddx-b808df39 are\\n+~15× and ~65× longer than the longest *finalized* attempt on record. This means\\n+the hung workers never reached the point of writing `result.json` — they were\\n+blocked inside `runner.Run` (or deeper) for days. Every code path that\\n+*should* have terminated them (idle timeout, compaction-stuck, stall detector,\\n+cancel propagation) failed to fire.\\n+\\n+The 37 manifest-only bundles are candidates for hung or externally-killed\\n+workers; e.g. `20260416T215953-6b515c05` has a manifest from 2026-04-16T21:59Z\\n+with no result. Those fit the profile of \\\"workers running 33h–142h with no bead\\n+assigned\\\" reported on agent projects.\\n+\\n+---\\n+\\n+## 2. Root-cause classification (source code evidence)\\n+\\n+The investigation checked the four hypotheses from the bead description.\\n+\\n+### RC1 (PRIMARY) — Caller context is discarded inside the Runner\\n+\\n+`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:239` (`Runner.Run`):\\n+```go\\n+ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\\n+```\\n+\\n+`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:163` (`Runner.RunAgent`):\\n+```go\\n+ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\\n+```\\n+\\n+`RunOptions` (types.go) takes no `context.Context` at all. `agent.ExecuteBead`\\n+likewise does not accept ctx, and `server.WorkerManager.runWorker` passes its\\n+own ctx into `worker.Run(ctx, …)` but that ctx **never reaches the agent\\n+invocation**.\\n+\\n+**Impact:** `WorkerManager.Stop(id)` (workers.go:648) calls `handle.cancel()`\\n+which cancels only the worker goroutine's ctx. The running agent provider call\\n+/ subprocess is detached. The only way to interrupt a stuck agent today is a\\n+SIGKILL from outside the process. This is the structural defect that enables\\n+33–142h hangs — once the agent is stuck, nothing inside DDx can stop it.\\n+\\n+### RC2 — Idle-reset timer, not wall-clock deadline\\n+\\n+`cli/internal/agent/executor.go:35-40` creates an *idle timeout* context value:\\n+```go\\n+func withExecutionTimeout(ctx context.Context, timeout time.Duration) context.Context {\\n+    return context.WithValue(ctx, executionTimeoutKey{}, timeout)\\n+}\\n+```\\n+\\n+`executor.go:154-178` consumes it and **resets the timer on every stdout/stderr\\n+byte**. `agent_runner.go:264-285` implements the same pattern against agentlib\\n+events.\\n+\\n+The default timeout is 2h (`types.go:363 DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000`). The\\n+comment claims \\\"2 hours — long enough for any task; supervisor/agent decides\\n+termination\\\" but the actual behaviour is **\\\"2 hours of silence required to fire\\\"**.\\n+A provider that emits a heartbeat, a ping, a retry line, or any byte resets the\\n+timer indefinitely.\\n+\\n+The `runClaudeStreaming` path (`claude_stream.go:373-384`) is the only path that\\n+uses a wall-clock deadline — and it is the only path we see actually hitting\\n+the timeout (bundle 20260415T015231 exactly 2h 0m 0.098s).\\n+\\n+**Impact:** for `harness=agent` (qwen, minimax, openrouter, etc.), no wall-clock\\n+bound exists. 33–142h hangs are consistent with a provider that dribbles events\\n+slowly enough to defeat the idle reset.\\n+\\n+### RC3 — Compaction-stuck breaker fires late\\n+\\n+`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:218-241` increments a counter on each no-op\\n+compaction event and trips at `stuckThreshold = maxConsecutiveCompactionFailures`\\n+(50 by default). On observed `efbf91c5` it took **2h 8m** to trip. If one\\n+iteration takes 3 minutes to produce a no-op compaction event, 50 events =\\n+~150 min — matches the observed tail.\\n+\\n+**Impact:** on its own, the breaker is too slow to be a useful hang-guard. It\\n+should be replaced or complemented by a wall-clock-bounded variant.\\n+\\n+### RC4 — Provider HTTP requests likely lack a per-request deadline\\n+\\n+`RunAgent` passes the internal `context.Background()`-derived ctx into\\n+`agentlib.Run(ctx, req)`. The agent library's providers (`openai.Client`,\\n+anthropic client) do not appear to install a per-request deadline themselves\\n+(grepping the v0.3.14 module cache shows short timeouts on discovery/probe\\n+only; the main call path has no WithTimeout). A stalled TCP socket after\\n+headers are received but before the response body arrives can hang a streaming\\n+read indefinitely.\\n+\\n+**Impact:** consistent with \\\"alive but mid-LLM-call with no client-side\\n+deadline\\\" — hypothesis (2) in the bead description. Cannot be fully confirmed\\n+from source alone; needs runtime `go tool pprof http://localhost:\u003cport\u003e/debug/pprof/goroutine`\\n+against a live hung worker.\\n+\\n+### RC-skipped — BashTool subprocess hang (hypothesis 1)\\n+\\n+Not the root cause. `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent@v0.3.14/tool/bash.go:15`\\n+installs a 120s default wall-clock timeout (`context.WithTimeout(ctx, timeout)`)\\n+and calls `exec.CommandContext`. Per-call bash hangs self-terminate at 2min.\\n+\\n+### RC-skipped — Tracker-side claim leak (hypothesis 3)\\n+\\n+Tracker state is incidental to the hang: the bead's own description notes the\\n+hung workers exist as processes (`ps aux | grep claude`). The worker process is\\n+alive; the claim is alive because the worker is alive. Fixing the claim alone\\n+does not kill the worker. This hypothesis is downstream, not upstream.\\n+\\n+### RC-skipped — Worktree file-lock (hypothesis 4)\\n+\\n+No evidence in bundles. Execute-bead worktrees are plain git worktrees at\\n+`/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/*`; no process-held file locks would survive across 33–142h\\n+without the owning process. If the process is alive, the lock is alive — same\\n+as RC3.\\n+\\n+---\\n+\\n+## 3. Relationship to ddx-b808df39\\n+\\n+ddx-b808df39 proposes an operator `ddx agent workers stop \u003cid\u003e` command that\\n+sends **SIGTERM at the OS level**, then SIGKILL after a grace period. That\\n+design **does** work around RC1 (process-level signal bypasses the broken ctx\\n+plumbing). So ddx-b808df39 is a *correct mitigation* — but only a mitigation.\\n+\\n+Without fixing RC1 (and at least RC2), operators will keep needing to invoke\\n+`workers stop` manually. The autonomous remediations every other system relies\\n+on (cancel ctx, idle timeout, circuit breaker) are each broken today in ways\\n+that require explicit code changes, not just a new operator tool.\\n+\\n+**Recommendation:** Ship ddx-b808df39 first (quick operational relief), then\\n+resolve the child beads filed below in order RC1 → RC2 → RC4 → RC3.\\n+\\n+---\\n+\\n+## 4. Follow-up beads filed\\n+\\n+Each addresses one root cause with a deterministic acceptance test. Priority\\n+and ID are shown; full details available via `ddx bead show \u003cid\u003e`.\\n+\\n+The IDs below carry an `.execute-bead-wt-…` prefix because `ddx bead create`\\n+was invoked from inside the isolated execute-bead worktree — this is a\\n+separate DDx bug worth noting but orthogonal to the hang investigation. They\\n+are parented to `ddx-0a651925` and resolvable by `ddx bead list --parent ddx-0a651925`.\\n+\\n+| RC  | Priority | Bead ID (suffix)                                                           | Title                                                              |\\n+| --- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |\\n+| RC1 | P0       | `…-526efaf1` | propagate caller context through Runner.Run and RunAgent           |\\n+| RC2 | P0       | `…-b203c889` | add wall-clock deadline alongside idle timeout for agent harness   |\\n+| RC3 | P2       | `…-b0562380` | bound compaction-stuck breaker by wall-clock time                  |\\n+| RC4 | P1       | `…-3e3913a0` | audit and enforce per-request HTTP deadlines on agent provider calls |\\n+| —   | P1       | `…-34d039b7` | autonomous worker watchdog with process-level reaper (complements ddx-b808df39) |\\n+\\n+**Suggested execution order:** RC1 → RC2 → RC4 → RC3 → watchdog. RC1 is a\\n+prerequisite for RC4 being meaningful.\\n+\\n+---\\n+\\n+## 5. What this investigation could NOT do\\n+\\n+- Capture live `ps`/`pprof` from a hung worker — none was accessible from the\\n+  isolated execute-bead worktree. RC4 in particular needs a live `go tool pprof`\\n+  goroutine dump to confirm that the stuck goroutine is in `net/http` streaming\\n+  read, not some other path.\\n+- Confirm whether the agent library's providers install any timeout that was\\n+  missed in grep. No live hung worker was available\\n+in this isolated worktree to capture live `ps`/`pprof`, so the incident evidence is\\n+drawn from `.ddx/executions/*` bundles and the worker/runner source path.\\n+\\n+---\\n+\\n+## 1. Observed evidence (from `.ddx/executions/`)\\n+\\n+| Metric                                               | Count / value                                        |\\n+| ---------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |\\n+| Total attempt bundles                                | 328                                                  |\\n+| Bundles with `result.json`                           | 291 (89%)                                            |\\n+| Bundles with `manifest.json` but **no** `result.json` | 37 (11%) — executions whose worker never finalized  |\\n+\\n+Top five longest *finalized* attempts:\\n+\\n+| `duration_ms` | Wall time    | `detail`                                                                    | Bundle                          |\\n+| ------------- | ------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------- |\\n+| 7,720,908     | **2h 8m**    | `agent: compaction stuck: 50 consecutive failed compaction attempts`        | 20260416T230316-efbf91c5        |\\n+| 7,200,098     | **2h 0m**    | `timeout after 2h0m0s` (claude streaming wall-clock fired)                  | 20260415T015231-c2ffa9ba        |\\n+| 6,764,927     | **1h 53m**   | success (agent harness, qwen)                                               | 20260416T230315-f9f46822        |\\n+| 5,649,987     | **1h 34m**   | compaction stuck                                                            | 20260416T230313-e193942b        |\\n+| 4,284,347     | **1h 11m**   | compaction stuck                                                            | 20260415T103422-bb3a45fa        |\\n+\\n+**Key observation:** the 33h / 142h hang durations cited in ddx-b808df39 are\\n+~15× and ~65× longer than the longest *finalized* attempt on record. This means\\n+the hung workers never reached the point of writing `result.json` — they were\\n+blocked inside `runner.Run` (or deeper) for days. Every code path that\\n+*should* have terminated them (idle timeout, compaction-stuck, stall detector,\\n+cancel propagation) failed to fire.\\n+\\n+The 37 manifest-only bundles are candidates for hung or externally-killed\\n+workers; e.g. `20260416T215953-6b515c05` has a manifest from 2026-04-16T21:59Z\\n+with no result. Those fit the profile of \\\"workers running 33h–142h with no bead\\n+assigned\\\" reported on agent projects.\\n+\\n+---\\n+\\n+## 2. Root-cause classification (source code evidence)\\n+\\n+The investigation checked the four hypotheses from the bead description.\\n+\\n+### RC1 (PRIMARY) — Caller context is discarded inside the Runner\\n+\\n+`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:239` (`Runner.Run`):\\n+```go\\n+ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\\n+```\\n+\\n+`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:163` (`Runner.RunAgent`):\\n+```go\\n+ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\\n+```\\n+\\n+`RunOptions` (types.go) takes no `context.Context` at all. `agent.ExecuteBead`\\n+likewise does not accept ctx, and `server.WorkerManager.runWorker` passes its\\n+own ctx into `worker.Run(ctx, …)` but that ctx **never reaches the agent\\n+invocation**.\\n+\\n+**Impact:** `WorkerManager.Stop(id)` (workers.go:648) calls `handle.cancel()`\\n+which cancels only the worker goroutine's ctx. The running agent provider call\\n+/ subprocess is detached. The only way to interrupt a stuck agent today is a\\n+SIGKILL from outside the process. This is the structural defect that enables\\n+33–142h hangs — once the agent is stuck, nothing inside DDx can stop it.\\n+\\n+### RC2 — Idle-reset timer, not wall-clock deadline\\n+\\n+`cli/internal/agent/executor.go:35-40` creates an *idle timeout* context value:\\n+```go\\n+func withExecutionTimeout(ctx context.Context, timeout time.Duration) context.Context {\\n+    return context.WithValue(ctx, executionTimeoutKey{}, timeout)\\n+}\\n+```\\n+\\n+`executor.go:154-178` consumes it and **resets the timer on every stdout/stderr\\n+byte**. `agent_runner.go:264-285` implements the same pattern against agentlib\\n+events.\\n+\\n+The default timeout is 2h (`types.go:363 DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000`). The\\n+comment claims \\\"2 hours — long enough for any task; supervisor/agent decides\\n+termination\\\" but the actual behaviour is **\\\"2 hours of silence required to fire\\\"**.\\n+A provider that emits a heartbeat, a ping, a retry line, or any byte resets the\\n+timer indefinitely.\\n+\\n+The `runClaudeStreaming` path (`claude_stream.go:373-384`) is the only path that\\n+uses a wall-clock deadline — and it is the only path we see actually hitting\\n+the timeout (bundle 20260415T015231 exactly 2h 0m 0.098s).\\n+\\n+**Impact:** for `harness=agent` (qwen, minimax, openrouter, etc.), no wall-clock\\n+bound exists. 33–142h hangs are consistent with a provider that dribbles events\\n+slowly enough to defeat the idle reset.\\n+\\n+### RC3 — Compaction-stuck breaker fires late\\n+\\n+`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:218-241` increments a counter on each no-op\\n+compaction event and trips at `stuckThreshold = maxConsecutiveCompactionFailures`\\n+(50 by default). On observed `efbf91c5` it took **2h 8m** to trip. If one\\n+iteration takes 3 minutes to produce a no-op compaction event, 50 events =\\n+~150 min — matches the observed tail.\\n+\\n+**Impact:** on its own, the breaker is too slow to be a useful hang-guard. It\\n+should be replaced or complemented by a wall-clock-bounded variant.\\n+\\n+### RC4 — Provider HTTP requests likely lack a per-request deadline\\n+\\n+`RunAgent` passes the internal `context.Background()`-derived ctx into\\n+`agentlib.Run(ctx, req)`. The agent library's providers (`openai.Client`,\\n+anthropic client) do not appear to install a per-request deadline themselves\\n+(grepping the v0.3.14 module cache shows short timeouts on discovery/probe\\n+only; the main call path has no WithTimeout). A stalled TCP socket after\\n+headers are received but before the response body arrives can hang a streaming\\n+read indefinitely.\\n+\\n+**Impact:** consistent with \\\"alive but mid-LLM-call with no client-side\\n+deadline\\\" — hypothesis (2) in the bead description. Cannot be fully confirmed\\n+from source alone; needs runtime `go tool pprof http://localhost:\u003cport\u003e/debug/pprof/goroutine`\\n+against a live hung worker.\\n+\\n+### RC-skipped — BashTool subprocess hang (hypothesis 1)\\n+\\n+Not the root cause. `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent@v0.3.14/tool/bash.go:15`\\n+installs a 120s default wall-clock timeout (`context.WithTimeout(ctx, timeout)`)\\n+and calls `exec.CommandContext`. Per-call bash hangs self-terminate at 2min.\\n+\\n+### RC-skipped — Tracker-side claim leak (hypothesis 3)\\n+\\n+Tracker state is incidental to the hang: the bead's own description notes the\\n+hung workers exist as processes (`ps aux | grep claude`). The worker process is\\n+alive; the claim is alive because the worker is alive. Fixing the claim alone\\n+does not kill the worker. This hypothesis is downstream, not upstream.\\n+\\n+### RC-skipped — Worktree file-lock (hypothesis 4)\\n+\\n+No evidence in bundles. Execute-bead worktrees are plain git worktrees at\\n+`/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/*`; no process-held file locks would survive across 33–142h\\n+without the owning process. If the process is alive, the lock is alive — same\\n+as RC3.\\n+\\n+---\\n+\\n+## 3. Relationship to ddx-b808df39\\n+\\n+ddx-b808df39 proposes an operator `ddx agent workers stop \u003cid\u003e` command that\\n+sends **SIGTERM at the OS level**, then SIGKILL after a grace period. That\\n+design **does** work around RC1 (process-level signal bypasses the broken ctx\\n+plumbing). So ddx-b808df39 is a *correct mitigation* — but only a mitigation.\\n+\\n+Without fixing RC1 (and at least RC2), operators will keep needing to invoke\\n+`workers stop` manually. The autonomous remediations every other system relies\\n+on (cancel ctx, idle timeout, circuit breaker) are each broken today in ways\\n+that require explicit code changes, not just a new operator tool.\\n+\\n+**Recommendation:** Ship ddx-b808df39 first (quick operational relief), then\\n+resolve the child beads filed below in order RC1 → RC2 → RC4 → RC3.\\n+\\n+---\\n+\\n+## 4. Follow-up beads filed\\n+\\n+Each addresses one root cause with a deterministic acceptance test. Priority\\n+and ID are shown; full details available via `ddx bead show \u003cid\u003e`.\\n+\\n+The IDs below carry an `.execute-bead-wt-…` prefix because `ddx bead create`\\n+was invoked from inside the isolated execute-bead worktree — this is a\\n+separate DDx bug worth noting but orthogonal to the hang investigation. RC1 is a\\n+prerequisite for RC4 being meaningful.\\n+\\n+---\\n+\\n+## 5. What this investigation could NOT do\\n+\\n+- Capture live `ps`/`pprof` from a hung worker — none was accessible from the\\n+  isolated execute-bead worktree. RC4 in particular needs a live `go tool pprof`\\n+  goroutine dump to confirm that the stuck goroutine is in `net/http` streaming\\n+  read, not some other path.\\n+- Confirm whether the agent library's providers install any timeout that was\\n+  missed in grep. E2e tests that mock the database or the HTTP layer are unit tests lying about their scope.\n- Stubs over mocks at the unit layer. Extract interfaces at domain boundaries; write in-memory stubs that return canned values set up by the test. Stubs do not record call sequences.\n- Contract tests between in-memory stubs and real backends — same suite runs against both, proving they are interchangeable. This is the discipline that keeps stubs honest.\n- Zero mocks in integration tests. Use real git (temp dir with scrubbed env), real filesystem (t.TempDir), real database (temp file / temp container), real HTTP (local test server). The only approved fake is at the AI-provider (or equivalent third-party-SDK) boundary.\n- Behavior over implementation — assert on observable state, not on call counts or invocation sequences.\n- Deterministic factory functions / fakers over static JSON fixtures.\n- Test names describe behavior (\"creates invoice when all line items valid\"), not positions (\"test_create_invoice_3\").\n- Testing pyramid as a *shape guide* (more unit than integration than e2e), not as mandated ratios.\n- **Performance measurements require a baseline and clear boundaries.** Any claim that something is \"fast\", \"scales\", or \"improved performance\" must include: (a) a numeric baseline to compare against (prior version, prior run, published SLO), (b) an explicit boundary — what unit of work is being measured, what environment, what is excluded (cold-start, warmup, network latency, etc.), (c) reproducible harness so the measurement can be re-run. \"It feels fast\" and \"benchmarks look good\" are not performance claims.\n\n**STRIP (cargo-cult testing dogma — do NOT ship):**\n- Hard coverage percentages as universal rules (\"\u003e= 80% overall\", \"100% on critical paths\"). Replace with: \"measure coverage where the project tracks it; coverage is a signal, not a target\".\n- Specific timing budgets as universal rules (\"unit \u003c 10ms\", \"integration \u003c 100ms\"). Replace with: \"tests should be fast enough that developers run them; the specific threshold is a project concern\".\n- Specific pyramid percentages (\"60% unit / 25% integration / 10% contract / 5% e2e\") — keep the shape, drop the numbers.\n- TDD as the only valid approach. Test-first is one workflow; test-alongside is another; test-after for a specific reason can be defensible. Do not call non-TDD \"worst practice\".\n- Tests-for-the-sake-of-tests (mirror-of-spec tests that add no signal, trivial getters/setters).\n- Mutation testing as a quality requirement. Mention as an optional signal, not a bar to clear.\n- Snapshot tests without a maintenance plan.\n- Retry loops around flaky tests as \"improvement\" — a retry loop hides a bug; fix the flake.\n- Branch coverage vs line coverage dogma — say \"coverage metrics matter less than whether tests catch real bugs\".\n\n**STRIP (DDx-internal specifics):**\n- References to cli/internal/agent/script.go (replace with generic \"a deterministic script-like fake at the AI-provider boundary\")\n- Named DDx legacy offenders (orchTestGitOps, fakeExecuteBeadGit, gateTestGitOps, mockExecutor) — these belong in a helix-test-engineer plugin persona, not this generic base\n- Authoritative-sources frontmatter section citing docs/helix/concerns.md and HELIX concern.md\n- Specific paths like cli/internal/bead, cli/internal/agent\n\nGround in external canon:\n- Kent Beck - Test-Driven Development: By Example\n- Martin Fowler - \"Mocks Arent Stubs\" (canonical reference for why call-sequence assertions are a code smell)\n- Brendan Gregg - Systems Performance methodology (for the baseline + boundary stance on perf measurement)\n- Anthropic Prompt Library (if a testing entry exists)\n- TDD Guard from anthropics/skills repo (if the automated-test-first-enforcement pattern is worth mentioning)\n\nBody structure: Philosophy / Approach / Anti-patterns / Sources.\n\nFrontmatter:\n---\nname: test-engineer\nroles: [test-engineer, quality-analyst]\ndescription: Testing specialist enforcing stubs-over-mocks, contract tests, real e2e, behavior-over-implementation, testing pyramid as shape not ratios, and baselined performance measurement\ntags: [testing, quality, performance]\n---\n\nIn-scope: library/personas/test-engineer.md (new)\nOut-of-scope: deletion of test-engineer-tdd.md (deprecation bead); helix-test-engineer plugin persona (HELIX concern)","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-17T22:37:54.294846139Z","id":"ddx-13ff86c6","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:personas"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-94c28517","priority":2,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"Generalize test-engineer persona — strip DDx-internal path references","updated_at":"2026-04-17T22:39:32.555980009Z"}

./.agents/skills/.ddx/beads.jsonl:83:{"claimed-at":"2026-04-15T01:13:31Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3766264","closing_commit_sha":"604151773f9b905346ee95b051e024a02f850cf0","created_at":"2026-04-15T01:11:17.497753676Z","description":"The embedded 'agent' harness can complete a task successfully (commit produced, tests pass) but then fail to terminate, getting stuck in a compaction retry loop with compaction always failing. The parent execute-loop never observes task_succeeded and hangs forever.\n\n## Observed\n\nRunning 'ddx agent execute-loop --once --local --harness agent --model qwen/qwen3-coder-next' against axon-43ed769b (axon auth-core-types bead):\n\n1. qwen3-coder-next ran the task correctly, producing commit 91d4bdb with 636 LOC of types + 109 green tests + clippy clean. The commit was present in the worktree at /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-43ed769b-20260415T005652-ebe7a16a.\n\n2. Instead of terminating, the agent kept generating events. After ~20 minutes the session log was 33 MB (8461+ events, 249 messages) and stuck in:\n\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.start\",...}\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.end\",...,\"data\":{\"messages_after\":249,\"messages_before\":249,\"no_compaction\":true,\"success\":false}}\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.start\",...}\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.end\",...,\"data\":{\"messages_after\":249,\"messages_before\":249,\"no_compaction\":true,\"success\":false}}\n    ...\n\n3. Parent ddx process in __futex_wait. Had to SIGTERM the parent (PID 4141811) and manually fast-forward-merge the worktree commit to land the work.\n\n4. A1 (previous bead) ran the same harness/model and terminated correctly in ~14 min with identical-looking commit shape. The difference was bead size: A2's output was a single 633-line Rust file, vs A1's 350-line SQL + 290-line test file. A2 didn't exceed what A1 did — so the context-limit explanation doesn't fully account for it.\n\n## Expected\n\n- After the agent commits and has nothing more to do, it should emit task_succeeded and terminate\n- Compaction should either succeed (messages_after \u003c messages_before) or bail out cleanly, not retry indefinitely\n- If the context genuinely cannot be compacted, the agent should report a structured failure and preserve the commits, not spin\n\n## Scope\n\n- Detect mid-turn compaction returning success=false repeatedly and bail with a structured 'compaction_stuck' status\n- Bound the total number of compaction attempts in a single turn\n- Ensure task termination happens immediately after a successful commit + no pending tool calls\n- Add a watchdog at the execute-loop level: kill the child if no commit+progress event is observed within a budget\n\n## Evidence\n\n- Session log: /Users/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/executions/20260415T005652-ebe7a16a/embedded/agent-1776214612561376672.jsonl (33 MB)\n- Rescued commit: 91d4bdb on axon master\n- Parent process: PID 4141811 (killed)\n\n## Acceptance\n\n- [ ] Compaction retry loop is bounded and fails structurally\n- [ ] Post-commit termination path is deterministic\n- [ ] Watchdog detects and terminates stuck sessions with a preserved-commit outcome\n- [ ] Regression test reproduces the stuck state (seeded session with large no-op tool calls)","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"merged onto current tip\nresult_rev=604151773f9b905346ee95b051e024a02f850cf0\nbase_rev=9943221220ec53cace570413e07dc6e809a5995a","created_at":"2026-04-15T01:39:52.60471501Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"success"},{"actor":"audit-reviewer","body":"The DDx-side circuit breaker in commit 604151773f9b905346ee95b051e024a02f850cf0 is a caller-layer workaround. The root cause (compaction driver producing 8461+ no-op events without a native bound) belongs upstream in github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent.\n\nFiled upstream bead agent-e5228424 in /home/erik/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl with:\n- The bug description\n- Pointer to DDx workaround commit as defense-in-depth\n- Requested native fix (bounded compaction attempts + structured ErrStuck)\n- Regression test requirement (seeded stuck-state reproduction)\n\nWhen the upstream fix lands and DDx bumps the agent library version, the caller-side circuit breaker can be removed.","created_at":"2026-04-15T03:46:00.187180985Z","kind":"note","source":"upstream companion bead filed 2026-04-15","summary":"Upstream native fix requested at github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent: bead agent-e5228424 (compaction loop native stuck detection). DDx circuit breaker is defense-in-depth; can be removed once upstream fix lands."}],"execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-15T01:39:31.55993889Z","id":"ddx-25a41f51","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:bug","gap:stability"],"owner":"ddx","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-e80e780a","status":"closed","title":"bug: embedded agent stalls in post-commit compaction loop","updated_at":"2026-04-15T03:46:00.206978601Z"}

./.agents/skills/.ddx/beads.jsonl:99:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As an operator, I expect the Sessions page to reflect reality — if I ran `ddx work` today, today's sessions appear within seconds. And I expect the feed not to silently break again the next time the agent execution path is refactored.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Session-index source of truth.** A single writer (invoked from `service.Execute` or a thin wrapper that every production agent-execution path calls) appends one line per completed agent invocation to `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl`. Fields are pointer-only: id, projectID, beadID, harness, surface, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, exitCode, bundlePath (relative), nativeLogRef (relative). No prompt or response bodies embedded.\n\n2. **Single-call-site guarantee.** Unit test enumerates every production code path that executes an agent (execute-bead, execute-loop, `ddx agent run`, quorum, replay if still present) and asserts each appends exactly one index row. If a new path is added later without wiring this up, the test fails.\n\n3. **Visibility restored.** GraphQL `sessions` query reads `sessions.index.jsonl`. Running `ddx work` (or any agent-execution path) against a fixture project causes the new session to appear in a GraphQL query within 2 seconds of completion. E2E test demonstrates this.\n\n4. **Body lazy-load.** A new resolver `session(id: ID!) { prompt response stderr }` loads the heavy fields on demand from the execution bundle (`.ddx/executions/\u003cts\u003e-\u003chash\u003e/`) or the agent-log path, without reading every session in the index.\n\n5. **Legacy migration.** A one-shot command (e.g. `ddx agent log reindex`) seeds the index from the existing `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (397 MB corpus on the current repo), preserving historical sessions. Integration test uses a fixture `sessions.jsonl` and asserts the index has one row per legacy entry with all pointer fields populated.\n\n6. **Deprecated-file behavior.** After migration, the legacy `sessions.jsonl` is not read by the server. `ddx agent usage` and related CLI commands (`cli/cmd/agent_usage.go:197,246,348`, `cli/cmd/status.go:179`) are updated to read from the index too, verified by their existing tests.\n\n7. **Perf sanity.** The GraphQL `sessions` list resolves in ≤100ms p95 on a fixture with 10,000 index entries; current behavior times out or OOMs. Benchmark captured in bead notes as baseline + after-fix.\n\n8. **UI note.** If there is a gap in the index (e.g. the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap on the current repo, assuming backfill from executions/agent-logs is not feasible), the Sessions page surfaces a single-line banner \"No sessions recorded between X and Y\" so the gap is not silently invisible. If backfill is feasible, do it instead and skip the banner.\n\n9. **Playwright e2e.**\n   - Seeds a fixture project with `.ddx/executions/` bundles from both before and after a simulated gap.\n   - Runs the migration command.\n   - Opens `/sessions`, asserts the most recent session timestamp matches the latest bundle, asserts a clicked row lazy-loads prompt/response from the bundle rather than from an embedded payload.\n   - Asserts the feed updates live (within 2s) after a new simulated session is written.","closing_commit_sha":"d733f7f20828708c5179f0ceff9462eaa34654eb","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:22:41.990684692Z","description":"## Observed\n\nThe Sessions page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../sessions` shows nothing newer than **2026-04-19**, three days ago. ddx is definitely still running — `.ddx/executions/` contains bundles from 2026-04-22 (today), `agent-s-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-21, `agent-loop-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-20. The UI simply cannot see them.\n\n## Root cause\n\nThe Sessions GraphQL resolver reads exclusively from `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:504-516`). That file stopped being appended on 2026-04-19 19:46.\n\nThe only writer is `Runner.logSession` (`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:847-914`). It is called from four sites today:\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:229` (Runner.Run — legacy)\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:216`\n- `cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:516`\n- `cli/internal/agent/virtual.go:206`\n\nProduction execute-bead / execute-loop no longer goes through any of them. The Runner-to-service refactor series (`5b933103`, `280f58e0`, `74eceda9`, `7cc1eb58`, `e05c785e`, `9f79f037`) routed the common path through `service.Execute` directly and did not carry forward the sessions.jsonl append. The last sessions.jsonl write lines up with the day that refactor set became default.\n\n## Secondary issue (same fix should address)\n\n`sessions.jsonl` is 397 MB for only 895 entries because each entry embeds the full `result.Output` (see `SessionEntry.Response` at `runner.go:879`). Growth is unbounded, there is no rotation, and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-490`) reads the whole file, parses every entry, and sorts in memory on every query. Even if we just restore the writer, the feed is fragile and will become a perf problem again quickly — relevant to `ddx-ad0db8fd` (bead detail was also O(N) scan).\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nDo not simply paper over by re-plumbing `logSession` into `service.Execute`. The right fix is:\n\n1. **Decide the source of truth for \"a session\".** Current candidates on disk:\n   - `.ddx/executions/\u003cts\u003e-\u003chash\u003e/{manifest.json,prompt.md,result.json}` — already written per execute-bead attempt.\n   - `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` and `agent-s-*.jsonl` — per-run streams from the agent harness.\n   - Bead evidence events (kind:cost already written per attempt, see `execute_bead.go:492`).\n   A session record should be derivable from a combination of execution bundles + agent-log streams, not from a separately-appended aggregate. The aggregate should be an **index**, not a **copy**.\n\n2. **Introduce a lightweight index.** A thin `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl` containing `{id, projectID, beadID, harness, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, bundlePath}` — pointer fields only, no embedded response bodies. Existing `sessions.jsonl` is deprecated to a read-only legacy file during a grace period.\n\n3. **Write the index from `service.Execute`** (or a wrapper the execute-bead / run / execute-loop paths all share). One place to touch. Unit test guards that every production code path that executes an agent appends exactly one index row.\n\n4. **GraphQL reads the index, streams bodies on demand.** `sessions { edges { node { ... } } }` reads the index. A new `session(id: ID!) { prompt, response, stderr }` lazy-loads the heavy fields from the execution bundle or agent-log file when the user expands a row.\n\n5. **Legacy sessions.jsonl compatibility.** The index can be seeded once from the existing 397 MB file so historical data is not lost, then the old file is not read again. One-shot migration command (`ddx agent log reindex` or similar).\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Replaying or regenerating sessions for the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap. If the evidence exists in executions/agent-logs we can backfill the index; if not, accept the gap and cap it with a dated marker in the UI.\n- A full telemetry rework. This bead just restores visibility and fixes the shape.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"omlx-vidar-1235\",\"resolved_model\":\"qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:59:17.183631171Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=omlx-vidar-1235 model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"tier=cheap harness=agent model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b probe=ok\nagent: provider error: openai: POST \"http://vidar:1235/v1/chat/completions\": 404 Not Found {\"message\":\"Model 'qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b' not found.
