The aggregate needs to be split.\n\n### Revised storage shape\n\n- Directory: `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/` (per-project, under each registered project's `.ddx`).\n- Inside: **monthly shards** named `sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`.\n- Each shard is pointer-only, same fields as the previously-proposed index (id, projectID, beadID, harness, surface, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, exitCode, bundlePath, nativeLogRef).\n- Writer resolves the target shard from `time.Now().UTC()` at append time and creates the shard file lazily on first write of the month.\n- Heavy bodies (prompt, response, stderr) stay where they already live — `.ddx/executions/\u003cts\u003e-\u003chash\u003e/` and agent-log streams — and are lazy-loaded by the `session(id:)` resolver.\n\n### Why monthly, not daily / size-based / SQLite\n\n- **Daily** — produces tens of thousands of files over the project lifetime; ReadDir becomes the cost.\n- **Size-based** — requires coordinating rotation with the append; race-prone and hard to test.\n- **SQLite** — overkill for first pass; revisit if a single monthly shard grows past tens of MB.\n- **Monthly** — ≤12 shards/year, human-readable filenames, date-range queries map trivially to shard selection, bounded shard size in practice.\n\n### Reader behavior\n\n- Default Sessions query loads the **current + previous shard** (covers \"recent activity\" for any current date).\n- Date-range queries map the range to a shard set and read only those.\n- Pagination beyond the default window triggers older-shard loads on demand.\n- No shard-coordination lock needed: each month's file is append-only and only the current-month shard is actively written.\n\n### Legacy migration\n\n- `ddx agent log reindex` reads the existing `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` once and splits by each entry's `timestamp` field into the appropriate monthly shards under `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/`.\n- After migration, the legacy file is renamed `sessions.jsonl.legacy` (not deleted) and never read again by the server. Hand-removal is the user's call.\n- Idempotent: re-running the command merges without duplicating (index row dedup by `id`).\n\n### Revised acceptance criteria (supersedes criteria 1, 5, 6, 7 on original description)\n\n1. **Sharded index writer.** Single shared call site (from `service.Execute` or equivalent wrapper) appends one line to `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`, computing the shard from `time.Now().UTC()`. Test: write across a simulated month boundary and assert two shards exist with the correct split.\n\n5. **Legacy migration shards too.** `ddx agent log reindex` splits `sessions.jsonl` into monthly shards by entry timestamp. Integration test uses a fixture with entries across 3 distinct months and asserts 3 shards are produced with correct contents and no duplicates on re-run.\n\n6. **Server reads the shard directory.** `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` reads `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/*.jsonl`, respects any date-range arg, and loads only the shards that intersect the range. Unit test seeds 6 monthly shards and asserts a 2-month query reads exactly 2 files.\n\n7. **Perf on sharded index.** Default Sessions view (current + previous shard) resolves in ≤50ms p95 on a fixture with 12 monthly shards × 10k entries each (120k total). Capture baseline (single-file read of same corpus) vs sharded in bead notes.\n\n(Criteria 2, 3, 4, 8, 9 from the original acceptance list remain as written.)\n\n### Scope clarifier\n\nThis amendment does not change the single-call-site writer guarantee or the lazy-body resolver — those remain the load-bearing ideas. The amendment is specifically about **how the pointer index is physically stored**.\n## 2026-04-23 — Implementation benchmark and evidence\n\nBaseline: `go test ./internal/agent -run '^$' -bench 'Benchmark(ReadSessionIndexDefaultWindow|LegacySessionsJSONLFullScan)$' -benchtime=1x` measured legacy single-file full scan/sort of 120k legacy-shaped rows with 2KB embedded responses at `BenchmarkLegacySessionsJSONLFullScan-12 1 1731705957 ns/op`.\n\nAfter fix: the same command measured the sharded default-window reader at `BenchmarkReadSessionIndexDefaultWindow-12 1 8103661 ns/op` for 12 monthly shards x 10k rows each (120k total), with the default query reading only the current + previous shard window. Environment footer: linux/arm64, Go go1.26.2.\n\nAcceptance evidence: `TestSessionIndexWritesMonthlyShards`, `TestSessionIndexShardFilesSelectsOnlyIntersectingDateRange`, `TestReindexLegacySessionsSplitsShardsAndDedupes`, `TestRunViaServiceWithAppendsOneSessionIndexRow`, `TestProductionAgentExecutionPathsUseIndexedServiceWriter`, `TestGraphQLAgentSessions`, `TestAgentLogReindexMigratesLegacyFile`, existing agent usage/status/processmetrics tests, and `e2e/sessions.spec.ts` cover writer sharding, bounded reads, migration, lazy body loading, deprecated-file read removal, UI gap banner, and live refresh.\n\nREVIEW:BLOCK\n\nMissing session index writer: no changes to `service.Execute`, shared execution wrappers, or agent execution paths to append `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl`.\nMissing GraphQL changes: no update to `cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go` for index-backed `sessions` or lazy `session(id:)` body loading.\nMissing migration command: no `ddx agent log reindex` or equivalent CLI implementation and no fixture-based migration test.\nMissing CLI usage/status updates: no changes to `cli/cmd/agent_usage.go` or `cli/cmd/status.go` to stop reading legacy `sessions.jsonl`.\nMissing performance validation: no benchmark or fixture proving 10,000 index entries resolve within the required threshold.\nMissing UI gap/banner behavior and Playwright coverage for `/sessions`, lazy loading, migration, and live feed updates.\n\nREVIEW:BLOCK\n\n**AC 1–3**: No `sessions.index.jsonl` schema, writer in `service.Execute`, or updated GraphQL resolver in `cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go`.\n**AC 4**: No `session(id: ID!)` resolver for lazy-loading prompt/response/stderr from execution bundles.\n**AC 5**: No `ddx agent log reindex` command or migration logic.\n**AC 6**: `cli/cmd/agent_usage.go` and `cli/cmd/status.go` are untouched.\n**AC 7**: No benchmark captured.\n**AC 8–9**: No frontend changes or Playwright tests.\n\nREVIEW:BLOCK\n\nMissing `service.Execute` or shared agent-execution wrapper change to append one pointer-only row to `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl`.\nMissing unit test proving execute-bead, execute-loop, `ddx agent run`, quorum, and replay paths append exactly one index row.\nMissing `cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go` update so GraphQL `sessions` reads `sessions.index.jsonl`.\nMissing new GraphQL `session(id: ID!)` resolver for lazy-loading prompt, response, and stderr from execution bundles or native logs.\nMissing `ddx agent log reindex` or equivalent migration command, plus integration test for legacy `sessions.jsonl`.\nMissing updates to `cli/cmd/agent_usage.go` and `cli/cmd/status.go` to stop reading legacy `sessions.jsonl`.\nMissing 10,000-entry GraphQL sessions benchmark and recorded baseline/after-fix bead notes.\nMissing Sessions UI gap banner or feasible backfill behavior.\nMissing Playwright e2e covering migration, latest timestamp visibility, lazy body load, and live feed Verified by reading the updated implementations in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:gitShow` and `cli/cmd/bead_review.go:beadReviewGitShow` — both invoke `git show \u003crev\u003e -- . ':(exclude)...'` (or functional equivalent) with the documented exclusion set.\n\n2. Existing execution evidence continues to satisfy auditability: either still tracked but excluded from review diffs, or moved to a dedicated ref / ignored store per the chosen fix. `ddx agent metrics` and `ddx bead metrics` still surface per-attempt usage/tokens/cost from the evidence bundle after the fix.","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T00:13:24Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1551897","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:08:23.667089738Z","description":"## Summary\n\nOn retry, DDx synthesizes a \u003cbead-review\u003e prompt whose \u003cdiff\u003e section is the output of `git show \u003cresultRev\u003e`. When the prior attempt's landing sequence appended an evidence commit of the form `chore: add execution evidence [\u003cattempt_id\u003e]` that added `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt_id\u003e/embedded/agent-*.jsonl` (full per-event NDJSON session log) as a tracked file, `resultRev` is that evidence commit's SHA (because `landEvidence` sets `result.NewTip = sha` and the orchestrator copies `land.NewTip` into `res.ResultRev`). `git show` of the evidence commit emits the entire session log diff with a `+` prefix per line. Session logs are 5-50k lines; the retry prompt exceeds every public context window.\n\n## Category / severity / surface\n\n- Category: correctness / resource-exhaustion\n- Severity: high\n- Surface: `ddx agent execute-bead` retry path triggered by `kind:review-malfunction` (and any other flow that re-synthesizes a \u003cbead-review\u003e prompt from a git diff/show since base_rev).\n\n## Full bug description (verbatim)\n\nOn retry, DDx synthesizes a \u003cbead-review\u003e prompt whose \u003cdiff\u003e section is the raw `git diff \u003cbase_rev\u003e..\u003cresult_rev\u003e` of the prior attempt. The prior attempt's landing sequence includes an evidence commit of the form:\n\n    chore: add execution evidence [\u003cattempt_id\u003e]\n\nthat adds `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt_id\u003e/embedded/agent-*.jsonl` — the full per-event NDJSON session log of the prior attempt — as a tracked file. Because that file is *added* in the diff range, the diff includes it in full with a `+` prefix on every line. Session logs are routinely 5-50k lines. One reasonable fix iteration is enough to push the retry prompt past 1-2 million tokens, which no currently supported model can accept — the provider streams back an n_keep \u003e n_ctx error and the retry crashes without a request round-trip.\n\nThis is a self-poisoning loop: the \"evidence\" DDx commits to make attempts auditable is the exact artifact that makes the next attempt undeliverable.\n\n## Reproduction (observed live 2026-04-17 in nexiq)\n\n1. Bead `hx-dd4ab35f` claimed by execute-loop worker (harness=agent, model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b, provider=vidar).\n2. Attempt `20260417T211600-d6a7dee6` succeeded and landed two commits: `cb5e733` (implementation + regression test [hx-dd4ab35f]) and `9a006dd` (chore: add execution evidence [20260417T211600-], adds `.ddx/executions/20260417T211600-d6a7dee6/embedded/agent-1776460560227794836.jsonl`, +20,623 lines).\n3. Post-merge review emitted `BLOCK` without a rationale; DDx recorded `kind:review-malfunction` and reopened the bead for retry.\n4. Retry `20260417T212928-27f9054a` synthesized `.ddx/executions/20260417T212928-27f9054a/prompt.md` of 6,307,997 bytes; `\u003cbead-review\u003e` block = 6,307,969 bytes; 99.9998% is the diff of the prior evidence commit, 99%+ of that diff is the session log file. Rest of \u003cbead-review\u003e (title/description/acceptance/labels/governing) is ~28 bytes.\n5. lmstudio responded: \"n_keep: 2,274,074 \u003e= n_ctx: 196,608\". Worker recorded failed attempt with zero usage. Same pattern on vidar (n_keep: 1,169,147) and bragi (n_keep: 2,274,074), all running qwen3.6-35b-a3b at max_context_length 262144. Provider/model is irrelevant — prompt exceeds every public context window.\n\n## DDx code-path verification (Claude investigation 2026-04-17)\n\nRelevant files and line ranges in /Users/erik/Projects/ddx:\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:553-561` — `DefaultBeadReviewer.gitShow(rev)` runs `git -C \u003cprojectRoot\u003e show \u003crev\u003e` with no pathspec. Output is injected raw into `\u003cdiff\u003e` by `BuildReviewPrompt` at `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:383-384`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:427-431` — reviewer fetches `diff := r.gitShow(resultRev)` then builds the prompt.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:540-555` (`landEvidence`) — creates `chore: add execution evidence [\u003cshort\u003e]` and sets `result.NewTip = sha`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:428-434` — `RealLandingGitOps.StageDir` runs `git add -- \u003crelPath\u003e` on the entire `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/` directory with no exclusions for `embedded/`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:250-251` — after Land, `res.ResultRev = land.NewTip`, so `ResultRev` ends up pointing at the evidence commit. The reviewer then runs `git show \u003cevidence-commit\u003e`, which is literally the dump of the session log.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1135-1164` (`VerifyCleanWorktree`) — also stages the evidence dir via `git add -- \u003cevidenceDir\u003e` and commits `chore: add execution evidence [\u003cshort\u003e]`; same no-exclusion pattern.\n- `cli/cmd/bead_review.go:162-171` (`beadReviewGitShow`) — mirror of the same bug in the standalone `ddx bead review` command.\n\nConfirmation the bug reporter's claim about `:(exclude).ddx/executions/*/embedded`:\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:269-283` (`SynthesizeCommit`) — the exclusion pathspec IS present here; the bug is that the evidence-landing commit path (`StageDir` + `VerifyCleanWorktree`) and the review path (`git show \u003crev\u003e`) do not share this exclusion.\n- `cli/cmd/init.go:19-46` — the gitignore rules DO include `.ddx/executions/*/embedded/`, but `git add -- \u003cdir\u003e` on pre-existing or explicitly-added paths can still stage, and older workspaces may pre-date this gitignore (matches nexiq's scenario where the evidence commit landed the session log).\n\n## Deviation from bug description (minor)\n\nThe bug text describes the diff as `git diff \u003cbase_rev\u003e..\u003cresult_rev\u003e`. The code actually does `git show \u003cresultRev\u003e` (single-rev show). The self-poisoning mechanism is functionally identical: when `resultRev` points at the evidence commit (`landEvidence` + `res.ResultRev = land.NewTip`), `git show` emits the entire `+`-prefixed session log. All suggested fixes still apply.\n\n## Suggested fixes (ascending scope)\n\n1. Exclude execution-evidence paths from bead-review diffs by passing a pathspec to the `git show`/`git diff` call in the prompt builder: `-- . ':(exclude).ddx/executions/*/embedded/*' ':(exclude).ddx/executions/*/prompt.md' ':(exclude).ddx/executions/*/usage.json'`. The same exclusion already exists at `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:269-283` for `SynthesizeCommit`; replicate it in `DefaultBeadReviewer.gitShow` (`execute_bead_review.go:553-561`), `beadReviewGitShow` (`cli/cmd/bead_review.go:162-171`), `landEvidence` → `StageDir` (`execute_bead_land.go:428-434,540-555`), and `VerifyCleanWorktree` (`execute_bead.go:1135-1164`).\n2. Substitute, don't reproduce. When a tracked-file addition exceeds a byte/line budget in the diff, replace it with a one-line stub such as: `+++ b/.ddx/executions/.../agent-*.jsonl  (+20,623 lines of session evidence — omitted from review prompt)`.\n3. Stop committing raw session logs as tracked evidence. Move `.ddx/executions/*/embedded/*.jsonl` to a gitignored store, or a separate `refs/ddx/evidence/*` ref analogous to `refs/ddx/iterations/*`.\n4. Progress is measured by: (a) count of child beads closed that concretely improved the ladder, reviewed-rejection rate, or measurement granularity; (b) observable reduction in cost-per-closed-bead over time once the metric exists; (c) observable reduction in opus-review rejection rate as the cheap-tier work improves.","created_at":"2026-04-15T03:47:42.980708133Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-4b59b816","depends_on_id":"ddx-e7254128","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-20T13:58:09Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-4b59b816","depends_on_id":"ddx-7bc0c8d5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-20T14:36:21Z"}],"description":"Standing optimization goal captured 2026-04-15: get the most work done for the least cost, and continuously improve our ability to measure and tune that trade-off.\n\n## Operating principles\n\n1. Cheap models do the work. Local (vidar-coder / qwen3-coder-next / other LM Studio hosts), then budget-tier cloud (claude haiku / gpt-5.4-mini), then standard (claude sonnet / gpt-5.4), then smart (claude opus / gpt-5.4 high-effort). Start cheap; escalate on failure.\n2. Strong models review. Every closed bead gets an opus review against its acceptance. Rejected reviews reopen with specific fixes.\n3. Deterministic fixes are the top rung of the ladder. When LLMs keep failing on a mechanical task, apply the fix manually; capture as input for routing policy.\n4. Measurement feeds optimization. Track per-bead which model attempted, how long, whether it passed review, what failure mode if any.\n\n## Existing bead tie-ins (helix review-loop epic from parallel agent)\n\n- ddx-a317208c exec: post-merge review agent on-by-default in execute-loop\n- ddx-dc231144 exec: tier-based auto-escalation driven by resource discovery\n- ddx-fdf031c9 exec: ddx bead review \u003cid\u003e — template a review prompt\n- ddx-44084b3e exec: ddx bead reopen \u003cid\u003e — atomic review-driven reopen\n- ddx-518b2690 exec: ddx agent run --output text — plain result extraction\n\n## Existing bead tie-ins (mine)\n\n- ddx-bd0691fe Epic: surface ddx-agent routing visibility (providers/models/check/route-status commands — prereq for routing policy)\n- ddx-bfc1aeaf Visibility 8: capture routing decisions in bead evidence (kind:routing) — feeds the measurement database\n\n## New child beads (to be filed under this epic)\n\nThe beads below are scope for this epic. File as children when we are ready to work each one. NOT pre-filed to avoid blocking the current Svelte+GraphQL migration.\n\n1. Per-bead cost aggregation: extend bead evidence schema to record tokens-in/tokens-out/cost-usd per attempt; add ddx bead metrics aggregate command that rolls up per-bead totals.\n2. Ladder success-rate analytics: ddx agent metrics tier-success — query last N closed beads, report success rate at each tier (vidar-coder, claude-sonnet, claude-opus, deterministic-fix).\n3. Prompt-context pre-filter: shrink the prompt for cheap-tier attempts — omit the full plan doc and include only the bead description + acceptance + 2 most relevant files.\n4. Failure-mode taxonomy: document the known failure modes (vidar long-context hang, read-loop stall, ship-unbuilt-code, opus-shape-miss, etc.) as bead evidence kinds so measurement can aggregate by mode.\n5. Review-rejection telemetry: aggregate opus-review outcomes (APPROVE / APPROVE_WITH_EDITS / REJECT) per originating tier so we know where the shape gaps are.\n6. Dashboard page for the metrics (requires Svelte migration Stage 4 to land first).\n\n## Why this is captured as an epic not a memory-only goal\n\nMemory is read; epics are tracked. Future sessions should see this in ddx bead list and be able to file child beads against it as we discover new measurement needs or optimization opportunities. The memory file is the principle; this epic is the backlog.","id":"ddx-4b59b816","issue_type":"epic","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:optimization","area:agen: ".ddx/executions/20260428T024807-b88003fb/manifest.json",

./.ddx/executions/20260422T205914-9451fa0a/manifest.json:14:    "description": "## Observed\n\nThe Sessions page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../sessions` shows nothing newer than **2026-04-19**, three days ago. ddx is definitely still running — `.ddx/executions/` contains bundles from 2026-04-22 (today), `agent-s-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-21, `agent-loop-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-20. The UI simply cannot see them.\n\n## Root cause\n\nThe Sessions GraphQL resolver reads exclusively from `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:504-516`). That file stopped being appended on 2026-04-19 19:46.\n\nThe only writer is `Runner.logSession` (`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:847-914`). It is called from four sites today:\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:229` (Runner.Run — legacy)\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:216`\n- `cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:516`\n- `cli/internal/agent/virtual.go:206`\n\nProduction execute-bead / execute-loop no longer goes through any of them. The Runner-to-service refactor series (`5b933103`, `280f58e0`, `74eceda9`, `7cc1eb58`, `e05c785e`, `9f79f037`) routed the common path through `service.Execute` directly and did not carry forward the sessions.jsonl append. The last sessions.jsonl write lines up with the day that refactor set became default.\n\n## Secondary issue (same fix should address)\n\n`sessions.jsonl` is 397 MB for only 895 entries because each entry embeds the full `result.Output` (see `SessionEntry.Response` at `runner.go:879`). Growth is unbounded, there is no rotation, and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-490`) reads the whole file, parses every entry, and sorts in memory on every query. Even if we just restore the writer, the feed is fragile and will become a perf problem again quickly — relevant to `ddx-ad0db8fd` (bead detail was also O(N) scan).\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nDo not simply paper over by re-plumbing `logSession` into `service.Execute`. The right fix is:\n\n1. **Decide the source of truth for \"a session\".** Current candidates on disk:\n   - `.ddx/executions/\u003cts\u003e-\u003chash\u003e/{manifest.json,prompt.md,result.json}` — already written per execute-bead attempt.\n   - `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` and `agent-s-*.jsonl` — per-run streams from the agent harness.\n   - Bead evidence events (kind:cost already written per attempt, see `execute_bead.go:492`).\n   A session record should be derivable from a combination of execution bundles + agent-log streams, not from a separately-appended aggregate. ./.ddx/executions/20260422T205914-9451fa0a/prompt.md:5:## Observed

The Sessions page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../sessions` shows nothing newer than **2026-04-19**, three days ago. ddx is definitely still running — `.ddx/executions/` contains bundles from 2026-04-22 (today), `agent-s-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-21, `agent-loop-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-20. The UI simply cannot see them.

## Root cause

The Sessions GraphQL resolver reads exclusively from `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:504-516`). That file stopped being appended on 2026-04-19 19:46.

The only writer is `Runner.logSession` (`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:847-914`). It is called from four sites today:

- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:229` (Runner.Run — legacy)
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:216`
- `cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:516`
- `cli/internal/agent/virtual.go:206`

Production execute-bead / execute-loop no longer goes through any of them. The Runner-to-service refactor series (`5b933103`, `280f58e0`, `74eceda9`, `7cc1eb58`, `e05c785e`, `9f79f037`) routed the common path through `service.Execute` directly and did not carry forward the sessions.jsonl append. The last sessions.jsonl write lines up with the day that refactor set became default.

## Secondary issue (same fix should address)

`sessions.jsonl` is 397 MB for only 895 entries because each entry embeds the full `result.Output` (see `SessionEntry.Response` at `runner.go:879`). Growth is unbounded, there is no rotation, and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-490`) reads the whole file, parses every entry, and sorts in memory on every query. Even if we just restore the writer, the feed is fragile and will become a perf problem again quickly — relevant to `ddx-ad0db8fd` (bead detail was also O(N) scan).

## Proposed direction

Do not simply paper over by re-plumbing `logSession` into `service.Execute`. The right fix is:

1. **Decide the source of truth for "a session".** Current candidates on disk:
   - `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/{manifest.json,prompt.md,result.json}` — already written per execute-bead attempt.
   - `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` and `agent-s-*.jsonl` — per-run streams from the agent harness.
   - Bead evidence events (kind:cost already written per attempt, see `execute_bead.go:492`).
   A session record should be derivable from a combination of execution bundles + agent-log streams, not from a separately-appended aggregate. The aggregate should be an **index**, not a **copy**.

2. **Introduce a lightweight index.** A thin `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl` containing `{id, projectID, beadID, harness, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, bundlePath}` — pointer fields only, no embedded response bodies. Existing `sessions.jsonl` is deprecated to a read-only legacy file during a grace period.

3. **Write the index from `service.Execute`** (or a wrapper the execute-bead / run / execute-loop paths all share). One place to touch. Unit test guards that every production code path that executes an agent appends exactly one index row.

4. **GraphQL reads the index, streams bodies on demand.** `sessions { edges { node { ... } } }` reads the index. A new `session(id: ID!) { prompt, response, stderr }` lazy-loads the heavy fields from the execution bundle or agent-log file when the user expands a row.

5. **Legacy sessions.jsonl compatibility.** The index can be seeded once from the existing 397 MB file so historical data is not lost, then the old file is not read again. One-shot migration command (`ddx agent log reindex` or similar).

## Out of scope

- Replaying or regenerating sessions for the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap. If the evidence exists in executions/agent-logs we can backfill the index; if not, accept the gap and cap it with a dated marker in the UI.
- A full telemetry rework. This bead just restores visibility and fixes the shape.

Do not simply paper over by re-plumbing `logSession` into `service.Execute`. The right fix is:

1. **Decide the source of truth for "a session".** Current candidates on disk:
   - `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/{manifest.json,prompt.md,result.json}` — already written per execute-bead attempt.
   - `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` and `agent-s-*.jsonl` — per-run streams from the agent harness.
   - Bead evidence events (kind:cost already written per attempt, see `execute_bead.go:492`).
   A session record should be derivable from a combination of execution bundles + agent-log streams, not from a separately-appended aggregate. The aggregate should be an **index**, not a **copy**.

2. **Introduce a lightweight index.** A thin `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl` containing `{id, projectID, beadID, harness, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, bundlePath}` — pointer fields only, no embedded response bodies. Existing `sessions.jsonl` is deprecated to a read-only legacy file during a grace period.

3. **Write the index from `service.Execute`** (or a wrapper the execute-bead / run / execute-loop paths all share). One place to touch. Unit test guards that every production code path that executes an agent appends exactly one index row.

4. **GraphQL reads the index, streams bodies on demand.** `sessions { edges { node { ... } } }` reads the index. A new `session(id: ID!) { prompt, response, stderr }` lazy-loads the heavy fields from the execution bundle or agent-log file when the user expands a row.

5. **Legacy sessions.jsonl compatibility.** The index can be seeded once from the existing 397 MB file so historical data is not lost, then the old file is not read again. One-shot migration command (`ddx agent log reindex` or similar).

## Out of scope

- Replaying or regenerating sessions for the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap. If the evidence exists in executions/agent-logs we can backfill the index; if not, accept the gap and cap it with a dated marker in the UI.
- A full telemetry rework. This bead just restores visibility and fixes the shape.

./.ddx/executions/20260422T205920-c98900f5/prompt.md:5:## Observed

The Sessions page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../sessions` shows nothing newer than **2026-04-19**, three days ago. ddx is definitely still running — `.ddx/executions/` contains bundles from 2026-04-22 (today), `agent-s-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-21, `agent-loop-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-20. The UI simply cannot see them.

## Root cause

The Sessions GraphQL resolver reads exclusively from `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:504-516`). That file stopped being appended on 2026-04-19 19:46.

The only writer is `Runner.logSession` (`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:847-914`). It is called from four sites today:

- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:229` (Runner.Run — legacy)
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:216`
- `cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:516`
- `cli/internal/agent/virtual.go:206`

Production execute-bead / execute-loop no longer goes through any of them. The Runner-to-service refactor series (`5b933103`, `280f58e0`, `74eceda9`, `7cc1eb58`, `e05c785e`, `9f79f037`) routed the common path through `service.Execute` directly and did not carry forward the sessions.jsonl append. The last sessions.jsonl write lines up with the day that refactor set became default.

## Secondary issue (same fix should address)

`sessions.jsonl` is 397 MB for only 895 entries because each entry embeds the full `result.Output` (see `SessionEntry.Response` at `runner.go:879`). Growth is unbounded, there is no rotation, and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-490`) reads the whole file, parses every entry, and sorts in memory on every query. Even if we just restore the writer, the feed is fragile and will become a perf problem again quickly — relevant to `ddx-ad0db8fd` (bead detail was also O(N) scan).

## Proposed direction

Do not simply paper over by re-plumbing `logSession` into `service.Execute`. The right fix is:

1. **Decide the source of truth for "a session".** Current candidates on disk:
   - `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/{manifest.json,prompt.md,result.json}` — already written per execute-bead attempt.
   - `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` and `agent-s-*.jsonl` — per-run streams from the agent harness.
   - Bead evidence events (kind:cost already written per attempt, see `execute_bead.go:492`).
   A session record should be derivable from a combination of execution bundles + agent-log streams, not from a separately-appended aggregate. The aggregate should be an **index**, not a **copy**.

2. **Introduce a lightweight index.** A thin `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl` containing `{id, projectID, beadID, harness, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, bundlePath}` — pointer fields only, no embedded response bodies. Existing `sessions.jsonl` is deprecated to a read-only legacy file during a grace period.

3. **Write the index from `service.Execute`** (or a wrapper the execute-bead / run / execute-loop paths all share). One place to touch. Unit test guards that every production code path that executes an agent appends exactly one index row.

4. **GraphQL reads the index, streams bodies on demand.** `sessions { edges { node { ... } } }` reads the index. A new `session(id: ID!) { prompt, response, stderr }` lazy-loads the heavy fields from the execution bundle or agent-log file when the user expands a row.

5. **Legacy sessions.jsonl compatibility.** The index can be seeded once from the existing 397 MB file so historical data is not lost, then the old file is not read again. One-shot migration command (`ddx agent log reindex` or similar).

## Out of scope

- Replaying or regenerating sessions for the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap. If the evidence exists in executions/agent-logs we can backfill the index; if not, accept the gap and cap it with a dated marker in the UI.
- A full telemetry rework. This bead just restores visibility and fixes the shape.

Closure was fake\n   (already_satisfied or premature). The retirement was NOT actually\n   performed — only ExecuteBeadLoopOptions (B16) and ExecuteBeadOptions\n   (B28) shipped real type-deletion commits.\"\n\nSo B17-B21 (the per-caller Stage 2 migration beads) were never done.\nToday's measured surface, from this worktree:\n\n  rg \"RunOptions|CompareOptions|QuorumOptions\" cli/  →  188 references\n                                                        across 48 files\n  rg \"RunOptions\\{|CompareOptions\\{|QuorumOptions\\{\" cli/\n                                                     →  ~50 composite literals\n                                                        (~13 production,\n                                                         ~31 in tests,\n                                                         ~5 in lint testdata,\n                                                         rest in agent\n                                                         package internals)\n\nProduction constructor sites:\n  cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1462\n  cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go:61, 261, 414, 685\n  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:647\n  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:531\n  cli/internal/agent/grade.go:169\n  cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:82\n  cli/internal/exec/store.go:201\n  cli/internal/server/server.go:1913, 3786\n\nInternal-implementation surface (beyond the two files the bead names as\n\"in-scope\"): every dispatch helper in the agent package threads\nRunOptions through its signature —\n\n  agent_runner.go, agent_runner_service.go, claude_stream.go,\n  compare_adapter.go, execute_bead.go, routing_metrics.go, runner.go,\n  script.go, service_run.go, session_index.go, virtual.go\n\nplus BuildArgs, RunAgent, runVirtualFn, runScriptFn,\nrunClaudeWithFallbackFn, finalizeClaudeResult, processResult, and\nTestProviderConnectivity all take RunOptions or fields from it.\nCompareOptions and QuorumOptions both EMBED RunOptions, so deleting\nRunOptions also forces a redesign of compare_adapter.go's option flow.\n\n## What is blocking\n\nThe bead is framed as a one-shot type-deletion (\"delete the structs,\nrename RunWithConfig → Run\"), but the AC requires `go build` and\n`go test` to pass tree-wide. To pass those gates the bead has to\nalso:\n\n1. Replace every internal RunOptions parameter (agent package\n   helpers) with an AgentRunRuntime + ResolvedConfig pair, OR keep\n   them on a non-RunOptions internal type.\n2. Migrate all ~13 production caller sites to RunWithConfig\n   (renamed Run), including building a config.ResolvedConfig at each\n   site (CLI, server, exec store, grade, compare_adapter,\n   execute_bead, execute_bead_review, service_run).\n3. Migrate or delete the CompareOptions/QuorumOptions surface\n   (compare_adapter.go is heavily built around it; this is essentially\n   a rewrite of the comparison/quorum dispatcher, not a rename).\n4. Migrate ~31 test composite literals across agent_test.go,\n   claude_stream_test.go, prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go,\n   session_index_test.go, models_test.go, virtual_test.go,\n   plus runtime tests.\n5. Update or remove evidencelint analyzer entries for RunOptions\n   and the runtimelint testdata (which deliberately uses the legacy\n   types as fixtures).\n\nThis is the SD-024 §\"Migrate one production caller per bead\" / §\"Migrate\ntest files in groups\" work — the ~5 beads (B17–B21) the bead description\npoints at as completed dependencies. Doing all of that here in a single\nexecute-bead window violates the SD's own per-bead size budget\n(\"\u003c500 LoC of net change, \u003c20 minutes of agent time, under $10 of model\nspend\") by an order of magnitude. The realistic LoC delta is in the\n1500–2500 range across 30+ files.\n\n## What a follow-up attempt would need\n\nReopen B17–B21 (or their successor beads) with concrete per-file scopes\nbefore re-queuing this bead. Suggested split:\n\n- B17: migrate cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go RunViaService caller +\n       cli/internal/server/server.go (2 sites). Add a\n       config.ResolvedConfig handoff at each site.\n- B18: migrate cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go +\n       execute_bead_review.go + service_run.go (3 sites; agent-package\n       internal callers).\n- B19: migrate cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go and\n       cli/internal/agent/grade.go — biggest surface; redesigns\n       CompareOptions/QuorumOptions on top of AgentRunRuntime\n       (likely promotes Harnesses/Strategy/Threshold/ArmModels/\n       ArmLabels/Sandbox/KeepSandbox/PostRun onto a new\n       CompareRuntime/QuorumRuntime).\n- B20: migrate cli/internal/exec/store.go + types.go.\n- B21: migrate the ~31 test composite literals; update\n       evidencelint analyzer + testdata.\n\nOnce those land green, this bead (B22) becomes the trivially-correct\nfinal cleanup — delete the three types, rename RunWithConfig → Run,\nupdate the runtimelint testdata that intentionally uses the legacy\nnames, and confirm runtimelint reports zero violations on the live\ntree.\n\nRecommendation: do NOT escalate this bead to a stronger model yet.\nThe blocker is the missing prior beads, not model capability. Request re-scoping into sub-beads (see below)\nbefore another execution attempt.\n\n================================================================\nWhat is done\n================================================================\n\nStage 2 dispatch-site migrations (beads c0ff5d3e, 4c31e465, 21f9e321,\nee2cd434) landed RunWithConfig / RunCompareWithConfigViaService /\nRunQuorumWithConfigViaService alongside the legacy Run /\nRunCompareViaService / RunQuorumViaService entry points. The CLI\ndispatch sites (`ddx agent run`, `ddx agent compare`, `ddx agent\nquorum`) now construct rcfg + AgentRunRuntime / CompareRuntime /\nQuorumRuntime and dispatch through the new `*WithConfig*` paths.\nA subset of agent test files were migrated by bead ee2cd434.\n\nStage 1 cleanup (bead edbfa046) removed ExecuteBeadLoopOptions and\nthe legacy `ExecuteBeadWorker.Run(opts)` method, renamed\n`RunWithConfig` → `Run`. That cleanup touched 18 files and ~123\nnet-changed lines.\n\n================================================================\nWhy this bead cannot land in one pass as currently scoped\n================================================================\n\nThe bead description in-scopes only two files:\n\n  - cli/internal/agent/types.go (delete RunOptions / CompareOptions /\n    QuorumOptions structs)\n  - cli/internal/agent/runner.go (delete legacy Run, rename\n    RunWithConfig → Run)\n\nThe bead acceptance criteria, however, require `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go build\n./...` and `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./...` to pass. After the structs and\nthe legacy method are deleted, the tree does not compile. A grep\nover the post-Stage-2 tree shows the deleted symbols are still\nreferenced in:\n\n  RunOptions: 137 occurrences across 42 files\n  CompareOptions: ~15 occurrences across compare_adapter.go,\n    models.go, agent_compare_config_test.go, plus 4 testdata files\n  QuorumOptions: ~5 occurrences across compare_adapter.go,\n    agent_quorum_config_test.go, plus testdata\n\nStage 2's dispatch-site migrations only retired *construction* of\nthese structs at the four CLI entry points and a portion of\nagent-package tests. The structs are still load-bearing for:\n\n  - Internal helpers in cli/internal/agent: BuildArgs, resolveHarness,\n    resolvePrompt, resolveModel, resolveTimeout, resolveWallClock,\n    runVirtualFn, RunAgent, runScriptFn, runClaudeWithFallbackFn,\n    SessionIndexEntryFromResult, BenchmarkArmsToCompare, and the\n    entire compare_adapter.go internal pipeline (CompareOptions /\n    QuorumOptions are used as the working struct shape inside\n    RunCompare / RunQuorum, not just at the top of the public API).\n  - The `runner.Run(RunOptions{...})` self-call inside\n    runner.go::TestProviderConnectivity (line 976).\n  - The `r.Run(RunOptions{...})` call inside grade.go::169.\n  - The `agent.RunViaService` exported function and its internal\n    callers (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1462, cli/internal/server/server.go\n    at lines 1913 and 3786, cli/internal/exec/store.go:201).\n  - The `Run(opts agent.RunOptions)` interface contract on\n    cli/internal/exec/types.go:11 plus its three implementers\n    (cmd/exec.go, internal/exec/store.go, exec_acceptance_test.go,\n    workers_stop_propagation_test.go).\n  - cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go's two test runners\n    (fakeAgentRunner, modelPassthroughCapture).\n  - cli/internal/agent/prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go's\n    resolvePrompt / defaultResolvePromptForCompare /\n    RunViaServiceWith calls (the bead 21 commit explicitly noted\n    these were left out of scope).\n  - cli/tools/lint/evidencelint/* — the testdata uses a *separate*\n    stub `agent.RunOptions` in testdata/src/agent/agent.go that is\n    independent of the production type, so testdata compiles fine,\n    but analyzer.go's package-level doc comments and\n    runOptionsTypeNames list reference the production type's name.\n\n================================================================\nEstimated true cost\n================================================================\n\nStage 1's analogous cleanup (ExecuteBeadLoopOptions) was 18 files /\n~123 net lines. Stage 2's RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions\ncleanup is ~3-4× larger by raw reference count, and ~2× larger\nstructurally because (a) RunOptions is used as the central internal\ndata carrier inside compare_adapter.go's full Run / RunCompare /\nRunQuorum pipelines (not just at the dispatch entry), and (b)\nexternal callers in three different packages (cmd/, internal/exec/,\ninternal/server/) each need their own rcfg + AgentRunRuntime\nconstruction wired in, which means the `agent.RunViaService` /\n`agent.RunCompareViaService` / `agent.RunQuorumViaService`\nback-compat shims must stay or be replaced.\n\nConservative estimate: ~40 files / ~1500–2500 net lines / 3-5×\nthe per-bead size budget defined in SD-024 §\"Per-bead size budget\"\n(target \u003c500 LoC, \u003c20 minutes, \u003c$10 spend).\n\nA no-change here is preferable to a half-finished commit that\nleaves the tree red or smuggles a partial migration past review.\n\n================================================================\nWhat a follow-up attempt would need\n================================================================\n\nRecommend splitting into a fresh stage-2-cleanup sub-stage with\nbeads sized to the SD-024 budget. Suggested decomposition:\n\n  1. Migrate cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go internals to\n     drive RunCompare / RunQuorum through AgentRunRuntime + rcfg\n     end-to-end; retire CompareOptions and QuorumOptions struct\n     usage *inside* the agent package.\n\n  2. Migrate cli/internal/agent helpers (BuildArgs, resolveHarness,\n     resolvePrompt, resolveModel, resolveTimeout, resolveWallClock,\n     runVirtual, RunAgent, runScript, runClaudeWithFallback,\n     SessionIndexEntryFromResult, BenchmarkArmsToCompare) so they\n     take rcfg + AgentRunRuntime (or per-field args) instead of\n     RunOptions. Includes runner.go::TestProviderConnectivity and\n     grade.go's `r.Run` self-call.\n\n  3. Migrate cli/internal/agent/service_run.go (RunViaService et\n     al.) to take rcfg + AgentRunRuntime and update all three\n     callers: cmd/agent_cmd.go:1462, server.go:1913+3786,\n     internal/exec/store.go:201.\n\n  4. Migrate cli/internal/exec (types.go interface +\n     store.go construction + store_test.go mock) and\n     cmd/exec.go's serviceExecAgentRunner +\n     exec_acceptance_test.go mock onto the rcfg-based interface.\n\n  5. Migrate the remaining agent-package test sites that bead\n     ee2cd434 left out of scope (prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go,\n     execute_bead_runtime_test.go, models_test.go,\n     session_index_test.go, virtual_test.go, claude_stream_test.go,\n     and the BuildArgs tests in agent_test.go that still construct\n     RunOptions literals as inputs to the unit under test).\n\n  6. Migrate cli/cmd test sites (agent_execute_bead_test.go's two\n     fake runners, agent_run_config_test.go remnants).\n\n  7. Migrate cli/internal/server (server.go RunOptions\n     construction + workers_stop_propagation_test.go mock).\n\n  8. THEN run this cleanup bead as the no-op final-deletion\n     bead it was designed to be: delete RunOptions /\n     CompareOptions / QuorumOptions structs from types.go,\n     delete the legacy Run method on Runner, rename\n     RunWithConfig → Run. At that point the AC's `go build`\n     and `go test` will pass on the existing tree.\n\nAfter step 7, the tree should grep-clean for the three struct\nnames except in cli/tools/lint/evidencelint/testdata/src/agent/\n(self-contained stub package, leave alone) and in analyzer.go's\npackage doc comment + runOptionsTypeNames list (which can be\nupdated cosmetically in step 8 alongside the deletion).\n\n================================================================\nVerification\n================================================================\n\n  cd cli \u0026\u0026 go build ./...   # green at base rev (b199cf3c)\n  rg 'type (RunOptions|CompareOptions|QuorumOptions) ' cli/\n    # 3 matches in cli/internal/agent/types.go, all current\n\nNo code changes were made in this worktree.\nrationale: Bead ddx-ade5ebb3 — agent: retire RunOptions + CompareOptions + QuorumOptions + legacy Run\n\nSTATUS: not attempted; scope-vs-budget mismatch between bead description\nand actual codebase state. ./.ddx/executions/20260426T181711-4cdff442/result.json:9:  "no_changes_rationale": "Bead: ddx-ade5ebb3 — agent: retire RunOptions + CompareOptions + QuorumOptions + legacy Run\n\nNO CHANGES — bead preconditions not satisfied; scope exceeds single-window budget.\n\n## What is done\n\n- Read the bead, SD-024, TD-024, runtimelint analyzer, and current state of\n  cli/internal/agent/types.go and runner.go.\n- Surveyed actual caller surface in the worktree (HEAD = 562f293c).\n\n## Why no commit was made\n\nThe bead description states explicit dependencies:\n\n  \"Depends on bead 16 (Stage 1 cleanup) AND beads 17-21 (every Stage 2\n   caller migrated).\"\n\nThe bead's own reopen note (2026-04-26) confirms those dependencies are\nNOT satisfied:\n\n  \"type RunOptions still exists at cli/internal/agent/types.go:36;\n   QuorumOptions at :81; CompareOptions at :171. runtimelint reports\n   ~80 violations across the tree using these types. Closure was fake\n   (already_satisfied or premature). The retirement was NOT actually\n   performed — only ExecuteBeadLoopOptions (B16) and ExecuteBeadOptions\n   (B28) shipped real type-deletion commits.\"\n\nSo B17-B21 (the per-caller Stage 2 migration beads) were never done.\nToday's measured surface, from this worktree:\n\n  rg \"RunOptions|CompareOptions|QuorumOptions\" cli/  →  188 references\n                                                        across 48 files\n  rg \"RunOptions\\{|CompareOptions\\{|QuorumOptions\\{\" cli/\n                                                     →  ~50 composite literals\n                                                        (~13 production,\n                                                         ~31 in tests,\n                                                         ~5 in lint testdata,\n                                                         rest in agent\n                                                         package internals)\n\nProduction constructor sites:\n  cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1462\n  cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go:61, 261, 414, 685\n  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:647\n  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:531\n  cli/internal/agent/grade.go:169\n  cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:82\n  cli/internal/exec/store.go:201\n  cli/internal/server/server.go:1913, 3786\n\nInternal-implementation surface (beyond the two files the bead names as\n\"in-scope\"): every dispatch helper in the agent package threads\nRunOptions through its signature —\n\n  agent_runner.go, agent_runner_service.go, claude_stream.go,\n  compare_adapter.go, execute_bead.go, routing_metrics.go, runner.go,\n  script.go, service_run.go, session_index.go, virtual.go\n\nplus BuildArgs, RunAgent, runVirtualFn, runScriptFn,\nrunClaudeWithFallbackFn, finalizeClaudeResult, processResult, and\nTestProviderConnectivity all take RunOptions or fields from it.\nCompareOptions and QuorumOptions both EMBED RunOptions, so deleting\nRunOptions also forces a redesign of compare_adapter.go's option flow.\n\n## What is blocking\n\nThe bead is framed as a one-shot type-deletion (\"delete the structs,\nrename RunWithConfig → Run\"), but the AC requires `go build` and\n`go test` to pass tree-wide. To pass those gates the bead has to\nalso:\n\n1. Replace every internal RunOptions parameter (agent package\n   helpers) with an AgentRunRuntime + ResolvedConfig pair, OR keep\n   them on a non-RunOptions internal type.\n2. ./.ddx/executions/20260422T205948-1f58b550/manifest.json:14:    "description": "## Observed\n\nThe Efficacy page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../efficacy` loads very slowly. User feedback: efficacy should really be \"an aggregation of sessions grouped by provider / harness / model\" — which is not what the current implementation does.\n\n## Ground truth (current implementation)\n\n`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:676-833` —\n\n- `EfficacyRows` builds its rollup from **bead evidence events** (`kind:routing` and `kind:cost` on every closed bead), not from sessions.\n- Computation path: `store.ReadAll()` → for every closed bead, read its event file → filter for routing/cost → per-(harness, provider, model) aggregate.\n- There is a cache (`efficacyMemo`), keyed by a fingerprint that itself walks every event on every closed bead to compute event count + max timestamp. ./.ddx/executions/20260422T205948-1f58b550/prompt.md:5:## Observed

The Efficacy page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../efficacy` loads very slowly. User feedback: efficacy should really be "an aggregation of sessions grouped by provider / harness / model" — which is not what the current implementation does.

## Ground truth (current implementation)

`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:676-833` —

- `EfficacyRows` builds its rollup from **bead evidence events** (`kind:routing` and `kind:cost` on every closed bead), not from sessions.
- Computation path: `store.ReadAll()` → for every closed bead, read its event file → filter for routing/cost → per-(harness, provider, model) aggregate.
- There is a cache (`efficacyMemo`), keyed by a fingerprint that itself walks every event on every closed bead to compute event count + max timestamp. So cache validation costs almost as much as cache rebuild.
- Cost shape: `O(closed_beads × events_per_bead)` on every request (cache miss) and even on cache hit the fingerprint walk happens.

## Why this is wrong (per user feedback)

- Efficacy is a sessions-view concept: "how is harness/provider/model X doing?" Sessions are the natural row. Bead events are a lossy, indirect proxy — they only exist for *closed* beads, they duplicate session state, and they force coupling between the efficacy feature and bead lifecycle.
- The bead-event path misses: open beads with in-flight sessions, sessions that didn't close a bead, benchmark/quorum sessions, non-execute-bead invocations (e.g., `ddx agent run`).
- The sessions feed is already being reworked into a sharded pointer index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`). Efficacy should read from that index — single source of truth.

## Proposed direction

### Part 1 — Reshape the data source

- Rebuild `EfficacyRows` as an aggregation over `sessions.index` (the sharded index from `ddx-2ceb02fa`), not over bead events.
- Grouping key: `(harness, provider, model)` — optionally filterable by project, bead, label, time range.
- Metrics per row: attempts (count), successes (count + rate), median input/output tokens, median duration, median cost. Identical shape to today; only the source changes.
- Shard awareness: time-range queries map to a shard set (same pattern as `ddx-2ceb02fa`); the default view reads current + previous shard.

### Part 2 — Fixture + baseline (honor user's 1,000+ record floor)

- Perf harness target: aggregate ≥**1,000 session rows** (≥ 10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups) within the budget below.
- Stretch fixture: 50,000 session rows across 24 monthly shards. Measures steady-state behavior as the index grows.
- Baseline captured in the bead notes before any optimization.

### Part 3 — Perf targets (first pass, in-place)

- Default Efficacy view (all-time) on a 10k-session, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.
- Date-filtered view (last 30 days, 1 shard): p95 ≤ 60ms in-process / ≤ 200ms HTTP.
- If in-place optimization (scope, pushdown, memoize-per-shard, streaming aggregation) **cannot hit these numbers** on the 50k stretch fixture, **do not silently change substrate** — raise the decision with the user. Candidate backends named by the user: axon, sqlite, postgres. Bring a recommendation + rationale, not a fait accompli.

### Part 4 — Drop the bead-event read path

- After the rewrite, remove `efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot` event iteration, `attemptsFromEvidence`, and `efficacyMemo`. They become dead weight once the source moves.
- Evidence events on beads remain (they're bead audit trail, not efficacy data); nothing in bead lifecycle changes.

## Out of scope

- The `comparisons` / "compare arms" feature on this page (tracked separately — see companion bead).
- Project-scope filtering UI (the backend support can be added now; the UI widget is a follow-up).
- Cross-machine / federated efficacy rollups.
./.ddx/executions/20260422T205948-1f58b550/prompt.md:8:**User story:** As a developer reviewing how different harness/provider/model combinations are performing, I expect the Efficacy page to load quickly on a workspace with tens of thousands of sessions, and I expect the rows to reflect every agent invocation — not just those tied to closed beads.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Source change.** `EfficacyRows` resolver reads from the sharded sessions index (`.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`, per `ddx-2ceb02fa`). Bead-event read paths (`efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot`'s event loop, `attemptsFromEvidence`, `efficacyMemo`) are removed in the same PR — no dead code retained.

2. **Schema parity.** Row shape and field names stay as they are today (rowKey, harness, provider, model, attempts, successes, successRate, medianInputTokens, medianOutputTokens, medianDurationMs, medianCostUsd, warning). UI does not need to change. Optional new filters (`since`, `until`, `projectId`) are added as nullable args with sensible defaults.

3. **Fixture + baseline recorded.** Fixture seeds ≥10,000 session index rows across 2+ monthly shards with ≥10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups. Baseline latencies (before any optimization beyond source change) written to bead notes with hardware + go-version footer.

4. **First-pass perf targets.**
   - All-time rollup on 10k-row, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.
   - Last-30-days rollup on same fixture: p95 ≤ 60ms / ≤ 200ms HTTP.
   - Stretch fixture (50k rows × 24 shards): all-time p95 ≤ 400ms in-process / ≤ 1000ms HTTP, or **user is consulted** before choosing a new substrate.

5. **DB-decision protocol (per user directive 2026-04-22).**
   - If targets in (4) cannot be met with in-place optimization (streaming aggregation, per-shard memoization with correct invalidation, selective field read), the PR author posts a short proposal in the bead notes: measured achievable numbers, proposed substrate from {axon, sqlite, postgres}, and migration cost. Implementation of that substrate requires explicit user approval before landing.

6. **Completeness test.** A fixture seeds sessions that would not appear under the old bead-event path (open-bead sessions, `ddx agent run` sessions, benchmark sessions). The new resolver returns rows for each of them. A matching integration test on the old code path (kept as a golden-output comparison for one cycle) asserts the new output is a strict superset in the cases it should be.

7. **Freshness.** A session written via the sessions index writer (from `ddx-2ceb02fa`) is visible in the Efficacy query within 2s. Integration test writes a row and queries the aggregation.

8. **Playwright smoke.** `/efficacy` opens, renders the rollup table, and clicks into `EfficacyAttempts` for one row — all within normal navigation budgets. Uses the 10k-row fixture; asserts the table has ≥5 rows rendered.

9. **Cross-reference.** This bead is a dependent of `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index must exist). If `ddx-2ceb02fa` is not yet landed when this work starts, bead notes record the stub / interim source chosen and the timeline for switching over.

./.ddx/executions/20260422T205954-bc8b12f1/prompt.md:5:## Observed

The Efficacy page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../efficacy` loads very slowly. User feedback: efficacy should really be "an aggregation of sessions grouped by provider / harness / model" — which is not what the current implementation does.

## Ground truth (current implementation)

`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:676-833` —

- `EfficacyRows` builds its rollup from **bead evidence events** (`kind:routing` and `kind:cost` on every closed bead), not from sessions.
- Computation path: `store.ReadAll()` → for every closed bead, read its event file → filter for routing/cost → per-(harness, provider, model) aggregate.
- There is a cache (`efficacyMemo`), keyed by a fingerprint that itself walks every event on every closed bead to compute event count + max timestamp. So cache validation costs almost as much as cache rebuild.
- Cost shape: `O(closed_beads × events_per_bead)` on every request (cache miss) and even on cache hit the fingerprint walk happens.

## Why this is wrong (per user feedback)

- Efficacy is a sessions-view concept: "how is harness/provider/model X doing?" Sessions are the natural row. Bead events are a lossy, indirect proxy — they only exist for *closed* beads, they duplicate session state, and they force coupling between the efficacy feature and bead lifecycle.
- The bead-event path misses: open beads with in-flight sessions, sessions that didn't close a bead, benchmark/quorum sessions, non-execute-bead invocations (e.g., `ddx agent run`).
- The sessions feed is already being reworked into a sharded pointer index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`). Efficacy should read from that index — single source of truth.

## Proposed direction

### Part 1 — Reshape the data source

- Rebuild `EfficacyRows` as an aggregation over `sessions.index` (the sharded index from `ddx-2ceb02fa`), not over bead events.
- Grouping key: `(harness, provider, model)` — optionally filterable by project, bead, label, time range.
- Metrics per row: attempts (count), successes (count + rate), median input/output tokens, median duration, median cost. Identical shape to today; only the source changes.
- Shard awareness: time-range queries map to a shard set (same pattern as `ddx-2ceb02fa`); the default view reads current + previous shard.

### Part 2 — Fixture + baseline (honor user's 1,000+ record floor)

- Perf harness target: aggregate ≥**1,000 session rows** (≥ 10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups) within the budget below.
- Stretch fixture: 50,000 session rows across 24 monthly shards. Measures steady-state behavior as the index grows.
- Baseline captured in the bead notes before any optimization.

### Part 3 — Perf targets (first pass, in-place)

- Default Efficacy view (all-time) on a 10k-session, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.
- Date-filtered view (last 30 days, 1 shard): p95 ≤ 60ms in-process / ≤ 200ms HTTP.
- If in-place optimization (scope, pushdown, memoize-per-shard, streaming aggregation) **cannot hit these numbers** on the 50k stretch fixture, **do not silently change substrate** — raise the decision with the user. Candidate backends named by the user: axon, sqlite, postgres. Bring a recommendation + rationale, not a fait accompli.

### Part 4 — Drop the bead-event read path

- After the rewrite, remove `efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot` event iteration, `attemptsFromEvidence`, and `efficacyMemo`. They become dead weight once the source moves.
- Evidence events on beads remain (they're bead audit trail, not efficacy data); nothing in bead lifecycle changes.

## Out of scope

- The `comparisons` / "compare arms" feature on this page (tracked separately — see companion bead).
- Project-scope filtering UI (the backend support can be added now; the UI widget is a follow-up).
- Cross-machine / federated efficacy rollups.
./.ddx/executions/20260422T205954-bc8b12f1/prompt.md:8:**User story:** As a developer reviewing how different harness/provider/model combinations are performing, I expect the Efficacy page to load quickly on a workspace with tens of thousands of sessions, and I expect the rows to reflect every agent invocation — not just those tied to closed beads.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Source change.** `EfficacyRows` resolver reads from the sharded sessions index (`.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`, per `ddx-2ceb02fa`). Bead-event read paths (`efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot`'s event loop, `attemptsFromEvidence`, `efficacyMemo`) are removed in the same PR — no dead code retained.

2. **Schema parity.** Row shape and field names stay as they are today (rowKey, harness, provider, model, attempts, successes, successRate, medianInputTokens, medianOutputTokens, medianDurationMs, medianCostUsd, warning). UI does not need to change. Optional new filters (`since`, `until`, `projectId`) are added as nullable args with sensible defaults.

3. **Fixture + baseline recorded.** Fixture seeds ≥10,000 session index rows across 2+ monthly shards with ≥10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups. Baseline latencies (before any optimization beyond source change) written to bead notes with hardware + go-version footer.

4. **First-pass perf targets.**
   - All-time rollup on 10k-row, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.
   - Last-30-days rollup on same fixture: p95 ≤ 60ms / ≤ 200ms HTTP.
   - Stretch fixture (50k rows × 24 shards): all-time p95 ≤ 400ms in-process / ≤ 1000ms HTTP, or **user is consulted** before choosing a new substrate.

5. **DB-decision protocol (per user directive 2026-04-22).**
   - If targets in (4) cannot be met with in-place optimization (streaming aggregation, per-shard memoization with correct invalidation, selective field read), the PR author posts a short proposal in the bead notes: measured achievable numbers, proposed substrate from {axon, sqlite, postgres}, and migration cost. Implementation of that substrate requires explicit user approval before landing.

6. **Completeness test.** A fixture seeds sessions that would not appear under the old bead-event path (open-bead sessions, `ddx agent run` sessions, benchmark sessions). The new resolver returns rows for each of them. A matching integration test on the old code path (kept as a golden-output comparison for one cycle) asserts the new output is a strict superset in the cases it should be.

7. **Freshness.** A session written via the sessions index writer (from `ddx-2ceb02fa`) is visible in the Efficacy query within 2s. Integration test writes a row and queries the aggregation.

8. **Playwright smoke.** `/efficacy` opens, renders the rollup table, and clicks into `EfficacyAttempts` for one row — all within normal navigation budgets. Uses the 10k-row fixture; asserts the table has ≥5 rows rendered.

9. **Cross-reference.** This bead is a dependent of `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index must exist). If `ddx-2ceb02fa` is not yet landed when this work starts, bead notes record the stub / interim source chosen and the timeline for switching over.

Satisfies US-222.\n\nDepends on Stage C1 (ddx-12934d66) — the context_overflow class is\nemitted by Stage C1's pre-dispatch short-circuit; this bead formalizes\nthe full taxonomy and the retry counter logic.\n\nIn-scope files:\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (classify errors returned\n  from the review dispatch into the four taxonomy classes per §12:\n  context_overflow, provider_empty, unparseable, transport)\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (count review attempts per\n  result_rev via existing bead events; on Nth failure emit terminal\n  review-manual-required event and do NOT re-execute primary; a new\n  result_rev resets the counter; iterations without a result_rev\n  (--no-merge, execution-failed) do NOT consume the budget per FEAT-022\n  §14)\n- cli/internal/config or per-project config (add a review_max_retries\n  field — default 3)\n- Tests: each of the four error classes triggers from its distinct\n  root cause (empty output, unparseable output, context overflow from\n  Stage C1, transport failure); retry counter scoping (result_rev\n  change resets, --no-merge does not consume budget); terminal\n  review-manual-required event after N failures; bead is NOT closed by\n  the reviewer failure path (invariant from\n  execute_bead_review_failure_modes_test.go:13 stays green).\n\nOut-of-scope:\n- Any change that could auto-close a bead on reviewer failure (§13\n  invariant is non-negotiable)\n- UI for surfacing review-manual-required beads (separate work)\n- Context-overflow escalation to a smaller reviewer model or tighter\n  caps on retry (noted as follow-up in FEAT-022, not in scope here)\n\nThe terminal 'review-manual-required' event body should include the\nfailure class, attempt count, and the result_rev being blocked, so\noperators can triage without opening multiple bundles.",

./.ddx/executions/20260425T143440-73ecde86/prompt.md:5:Stage G of FEAT-022. Split review-error into four explicit outcome
classes. Add a bounded retry policy per result_rev with terminal
manual-escalation event. Preserve the invariant that reviewer failure
does NOT close the bead.

Implements FEAT-022 requirements §12, §13, §14. Satisfies US-222.

Depends on Stage C1 (ddx-12934d66) — the context_overflow class is
emitted by Stage C1's pre-dispatch short-circuit; this bead formalizes
the full taxonomy and the retry counter logic.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (classify errors returned
  from the review dispatch into the four taxonomy classes per §12:
  context_overflow, provider_empty, unparseable, transport)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (count review attempts per
  result_rev via existing bead events; on Nth failure emit terminal
  review-manual-required event and do NOT re-execute primary; a new
  result_rev resets the counter; iterations without a result_rev
  (--no-merge, execution-failed) do NOT consume the budget per FEAT-022
  §14)
- cli/internal/config or per-project config (add a review_max_retries
  field — default 3)
- Tests: each of the four error classes triggers from its distinct
  root cause (empty output, unparseable output, context overflow from
  Stage C1, transport failure); retry counter scoping (result_rev
  change resets, --no-merge does not consume budget); terminal
  review-manual-required event after N failures; bead is NOT closed by
  the reviewer failure path (invariant from
  execute_bead_review_failure_modes_test.go:13 stays green).

Out-of-scope:
- Any change that could auto-close a bead on reviewer failure (§13
  invariant is non-negotiable)
- UI for surfacing review-manual-required beads (separate work)
- Context-overflow escalation to a smaller reviewer model or tighter
  caps on retry (noted as follow-up in FEAT-022, not in scope here)

The terminal 'review-manual-required' event body should include the
failure class, attempt count, and the result_rev being blocked, so
operators can triage without opening multiple bundles.

./.ddx/executions/20260425T105546-e5825bd5/prompt.md:5:Stage F1 of FEAT-022. Emit the evidence-assembly telemetry block onto
attempt bundles for review and grading attempts. Add a compact summary
to bead event bodies for review, review-error, and compare-result.

Implements FEAT-022 requirements §15 and §16. Satisfies the event-body
criterion of US-220 and the per-field criterion of US-221.

Depends on Stage C1 (ddx-12934d66) and Stage C2 (ddx-0bbf8563) —
F1 serializes the EvidenceAssemblySection struct pinned in Stage A1,
which C1 and C2 populate during assembly.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go: during and after
  assembly, collect per-section EvidenceAssemblySection records
  (struct shape pinned in Stage A1). Write them into result.json /
  manifest.json under an evidence_assembly key (additive to FEAT-014
  §19/§20 runtime-metrics block; does not replace).
- cli/internal/agent/grade.go: same for grading attempts.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: on the review and
  review-error events emitted by the loop, populate event body fields
  input_bytes, output_bytes, elapsed_ms, harness, model. Respect the
  existing event-body size cap at cli/internal/bead/store.go:747;
  never include section detail in the event body (that lives only on
  the artifact).
- Similar event-body update for compare-result events emitted by the
  grading flow.

Out-of-scope:
- CLI metrics surface extension — separate Stage F2 bead.
- Any UI or dashboard consumer of the new fields.
- Primary execute-bead event telemetry mirroring (explicitly OOS per
  FEAT-022 Out of Scope).

Rollback: revert telemetry writes; attempt bundles and event bodies
return to prior shape. Artifact-schema compatibility: new key on
result.json is additive; consumers that don't know about it continue
to work.

./.ddx/executions/20260425T073541-4f46941d/prompt.md:11:## 2026-04-22 — User directives on fixture size and storage strategy

Two standing instructions from the user that apply to this bead and to all other perf beads (`ddx-ad0db8fd`, `ddx-2ceb02fa`, and any spawned from efficacy/providers work):

1. **Minimum fixture size: 1,000s of records.** The original acceptance criterion (10 projects × 500 beads = 5,000) already meets this for beads, but restate the floor explicitly: **no perf work lands against a fixture smaller than 1,000 records per query shape**. Sessions fixtures, efficacy fixtures, provider/quota history fixtures — all ≥1k rows. Beads fixture stays at 5k; sessions fixture for `ddx-2ceb02fa` should be ≥10k index entries (already specified); efficacy and any new surfaces ≥1k attempts/rows each.

2. **DB backend is on the table.** If in-place optimization (scope, pushdown, lightweight index) cannot hit the target latencies on the fixture sizes above, the user is open to introducing a real data store — candidates named: **axon**, **sqlite**, **postgres**. Do not silently pick one: **raise the decision with the user before choosing a path.** Capture the baseline, capture what in-place optimization achieved, then bring the choice (and the rationale) to the user.

These directives supersede any acceptance criterion that implies "optimize in place no matter what." If the numbers say storage needs to change, that's the outcome — but the user decides the substrate.

**AC §8 — Playwright cross-project test routes wrong path**: `beads-smoke.spec.ts:107` navigates to `/nodes/${NODE_INFO.id}/projects/${PROJECT_ID}/beads` for the "cross-project" test, which is the same per-project URL as the 1s test. A cross-project smoke test should hit the all-beads route (e.g., `/nodes/${NODE_INFO.id}/beads` or whatever the actual cross-project URL is) and mock the `beads` query, not `beadsByProject`. The mock for `beads` exists (lines 76-89) but is never exercised because both tests navigate to the project-scoped path.
**AC §9 — Bead notes cross-reference**: The acceptance criteria require this bead's notes to list `ddx-ad0db8fd` and `ddx-2ceb02fa`. The baseline `.md` and code comments mention them, but no `ddx bead update` call or notes update appears in the diff. If the notes were updated via a separate mechanism not captured in this branch, this finding can be dismissed.

## 2026-04-23 — Baseline and perf-thread cross-reference

Baseline recorded in `docs/helix/06-iterate/perf/2026-04-22-baseline.md` and JSON sibling from the 10 projects x 500 beads fixture (5,000 beads total, 1,000 session rows). Environment: `go=go1.26.2 goos=linux goarch=arm64 cpu-cores=12`.

Key p95 results: `bead` 6.72ms in-process / 6.80ms HTTP; `beadsByProject` 8.84ms in-process / 9.29ms HTTP; `beads_crossProject` 62.16ms in-process / 75.80ms HTTP; `beadsByProject_statusOpen` 6.45ms in-process / 6.87ms HTTP.

Cross-reference for inherited harness work: `ddx-ad0db8fd` covers single-bead detail lookup; `ddx-2ceb02fa` covers sessions shard/index work. Both should use/extend this GraphQL perf harness.

Review follow-up: AC §8 cross-project Playwright smoke now navigates to `/nodes/{nodeId}/beads` and exercises the `beads` GraphQL query instead of the project-scoped `beadsByProject` path. Proposed CI posture remains a follow-up +25% p95 regression gate, not implemented in this bead.

## 2026-04-23 — Baseline refresh after session-index fixture alignment

Re-ran `BENCH_REPORT_DATE=2026-04-22 make bench-graphql` after updating the perf fixture to seed current pointer-only session-index shards instead of legacy `sessions.jsonl`. Refreshed artifacts: `docs/helix/06-iterate/perf/2026-04-22-baseline.md` and `.json`.

Refreshed p95 results: `bead` 7.23ms in-process / 7.29ms HTTP; `beadsByProject` 7.18ms in-process / 7.83ms HTTP; `beads_crossProject` 62.49ms in-process / 67.29ms HTTP; `beadsByProject_statusOpen` 6.74ms in-process / 7.73ms HTTP; `agentSessions` 13.45ms in-process / 14.19ms HTTP.

## 2026-04-23 — Final `make bench-graphql` verification

After fixing `cli/Makefile` to pass an absolute `DDX_BENCH_REPORT_DIR`, `BENCH_REPORT_DATE=2026-04-22 make bench-graphql` writes to the intended repo docs path. Final p95 results from that run: `bead` 6.76ms in-process / 7.20ms HTTP; `beadsByProject` 6.88ms in-process / 7.85ms HTTP; `beads_crossProject` 63.22ms in-process / 71.82ms HTTP; `beadsByProject_statusOpen` 6.75ms in-process / 7.56ms HTTP; `agentSessions` 13.24ms in-process / 13.64ms HTTP.

./.ddx/executions/20260425T093913-f16121f6/prompt.md:79:+    "description": "Upstream report (\"ddx execute-loop: post-merge reviewer silently fails on large prompts\", suggested fix #5) flags that even when the reviewer returns content, the markdown verdict extractor mis-parses it: when Claude responds with '### Verdict: APPROVE' the extractor pulls 'BLOCK' from the options header line in the prompt template that the model echoes back, logging '{\"verdict\":\"BLOCK\"}' for an actual APPROVE. This silently lets regressions land.\n\nFEAT-022 (parent ddx-0c35470e) is scoped to prompt assembly and size invariants; response parsing is out of scope there. File this as a sibling.\n\nSwitch the reviewer contract to a machine-readable JSON object emitted as the model's final response:\n\n  {\n    \"verdict\": \"APPROVE\" | \"REQUEST_CHANGES\" | \"BLOCK\",\n    \"findings\": [\n      { \"severity\": \"info\"|\"warn\"|\"block\", \"summary\": \"…\", \"location\": \"path:line?\" }\n    ],\n    \"summary\": \"≤300 char human-readable verdict justification\"\n  }\n\nReplace the existing markdown extractor with a strict JSON parser:\n1. Update the reviewer prompt template (post-collapse: agent.BuildReviewPrompt) to instruct the model to emit ONLY a JSON object matching the schema, fenced as ```json … ``` for robustness against models that won't emit raw JSON.\n2. Add internal/agent/review_verdict.go with a typed ReviewVerdict struct and a ParseReviewVerdict([]byte) (ReviewVerdict, error) that strips an optional ```json fence and decodes; reject on unknown verdict values.\n3. Replace the regex-based extractor call site in execute_bead_review.go with ParseReviewVerdict; on parse error, emit the existing 'unparseable' review-error class (introduced by ddx-70c1d2e2) — do NOT close the bead.\n4. Schema-version the contract: include schema_version: 1 in the prompt; the parser tolerates unknown fields but rejects unknown verdict values.\n\nIn-scope files:\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (prompt template, extractor call site)\n- cli/internal/agent/review_verdict.go (new)\n- cli/internal/agent/review_verdict_test.go (new)\n- prompt template asset under cli/internal/agent/assets/ if separated\n\nOut of scope:\n- Bounded retry / error-class taxonomy (ddx-70c1d2e2 owns this; this bead emits 'unparseable' into that taxonomy)\n- Prompt size invariants (FEAT-022)\n- Grading verdicts (separate bead if needed; this is review-only)\n\nCross-reference: This bead's `unparseable` emission feeds into ddx-70c1d2e2's bounded-retry logic. ddx-70c1d2e2 should be reviewed once both land to confirm the unparseable retry budget is sane (the upstream report observed real APPROVE verdicts being lost, so unparseable should retry, not terminal-escalate immediately).",

./.ddx/executions/20260425T092410-e9991cad/prompt.md:5:Upstream report ("ddx execute-loop: post-merge reviewer silently fails on large prompts", suggested fix #5) flags that even when the reviewer returns content, the markdown verdict extractor mis-parses it: when Claude responds with '### Verdict: APPROVE' the extractor pulls 'BLOCK' from the options header line in the prompt template that the model echoes back, logging '{"verdict":"BLOCK"}' for an actual APPROVE. This silently lets regressions land.

FEAT-022 (parent ddx-0c35470e) is scoped to prompt assembly and size invariants; response parsing is out of scope there. File this as a sibling.

Switch the reviewer contract to a machine-readable JSON object emitted as the model's final response:

  {
    "verdict": "APPROVE" | "REQUEST_CHANGES" | "BLOCK",
    "findings": [
      { "severity": "info"|"warn"|"block", "summary": "…", "location": "path:line?" }
    ],
    "summary": "≤300 char human-readable verdict justification"
  }

Replace the existing markdown extractor with a strict JSON parser:
1. Update the reviewer prompt template (post-collapse: agent.BuildReviewPrompt) to instruct the model to emit ONLY a JSON object matching the schema, fenced as ```json … ``` for robustness against models that won't emit raw JSON.
2. Add internal/agent/review_verdict.go with a typed ReviewVerdict struct and a ParseReviewVerdict([]byte) (ReviewVerdict, error) that strips an optional ```json fence and decodes; reject on unknown verdict values.
3. Replace the regex-based extractor call site in execute_bead_review.go with ParseReviewVerdict; on parse error, emit the existing 'unparseable' review-error class (introduced by ddx-70c1d2e2) — do NOT close the bead.
4. Schema-version the contract: include schema_version: 1 in the prompt; the parser tolerates unknown fields but rejects unknown verdict values.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (prompt template, extractor call site)
- cli/internal/agent/review_verdict.go (new)
- cli/internal/agent/review_verdict_test.go (new)
- prompt template asset under cli/internal/agent/assets/ if separated

Out of scope:
- Bounded retry / error-class taxonomy (ddx-70c1d2e2 owns this; this bead emits 'unparseable' into that taxonomy)
- Prompt size invariants (FEAT-022)
- Grading verdicts (separate bead if needed; this is review-only)

Cross-reference: This bead's `unparseable` emission feeds into ddx-70c1d2e2's bounded-retry logic. ddx-70c1d2e2 should be reviewed once both land to confirm the unparseable retry budget is sane (the upstream report observed real APPROVE verdicts being lost, so unparseable should retry, not terminal-escalate immediately).

Add a bounded retry policy per result_rev with terminal\nmanual-escalation event.
