Satisfies US-222.\n\nDepends on Stage C1 (ddx-12934d66) — the context_overflow class is\nemitted by Stage C1's pre-dispatch short-circuit; this bead formalizes\nthe full taxonomy and the retry counter logic.\n\nIn-scope files:\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (classify errors returned\n  from the review dispatch into the four taxonomy classes per §12:\n  context_overflow, provider_empty, unparseable, transport)\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (count review attempts per\n  result_rev via existing bead events; on Nth failure emit terminal\n  review-manual-required event and do NOT re-execute primary; a new\n  result_rev resets the counter; iterations without a result_rev\n  (--no-merge, execution-failed) do NOT consume the budget per FEAT-022\n  §14)\n- cli/internal/config or per-project config (add a review_max_retries\n  field — default 3)\n- Tests: each of the four error classes triggers from its distinct\n  root cause (empty output, unparseable output, context overflow from\n  Stage C1, transport failure); retry counter scoping (result_rev\n  change resets, --no-merge does not consume budget); terminal\n  review-manual-required event after N failures; bead is NOT closed by\n  the reviewer failure path (invariant from\n  execute_bead_review_failure_modes_test.go:13 stays green).\n\nOut-of-scope:\n- Any change that could auto-close a bead on reviewer failure (§13\n  invariant is non-negotiable)\n- UI for surfacing review-manual-required beads (separate work)\n- Context-overflow escalation to a smaller reviewer model or tighter\n  caps on retry (noted as follow-up in FEAT-022, not in scope here)\n\nThe terminal 'review-manual-required' event body should include the\nfailure class, attempt count, and the result_rev being blocked, so\noperators can triage without opening multiple bundles.",

No new code change is\nneeded in this attempt; the bead reopened because the prior review pass only\ninspected the evidence bundle (manifest.json / result.json / prompt.md) and\nnot the source diff.\n\nWhat is done — pointers per AC item\n-----------------------------------\n\nAC1: .ddx/run-state.json written at execute-bead start with required fields\n  - cli/internal/agent/run_state.go:22 — RunState{BeadID, AttemptID,\n    Harness, Model, StartedAt (RFC3339 via time.Time JSON marshal),\n    WorktreePath}\n  - cli/internal/agent/run_state.go:40 — WriteRunState atomic write\n    (CreateTemp + write + close + Rename) under .ddx/run-state.json\n  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:608 — call site at execute-bead\n    start, after worktree creation, populating every field above\n\nAC2: removed on normal completion\n  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:616 — `defer ClearRunState(...)`\n    fires on every normal return path of ExecuteBead\n\nAC3: removed on error / crash recovery\n  - Same `defer ClearRunState` covers the error returns inside ExecuteBead.\n  - For a crashed worker that never executed the defer, RecoverOrphans\n    sweeps the stale file: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go\n    around line 389–397 reads run-state and clears it when it points at a\n    bead being recovered or at a worktree that no longer exists.\n\nAC4: execute-loop writes/clears run-state correctly for sequential beads\n  - execute-loop drives execute-bead per claimed bead; Table columns: timestamp, bead, harness/model, verdict (pass/block/error), duration, cost.\n  - `/nodes/\u003cn\u003e/projects/\u003cp\u003e/executions/\u003cid\u003e` — detail view with five tabs: **Manifest**, **Prompt** (rendered markdown), **Result** (rationale + raw JSON toggle), **Session** (harness, model, cost, token totals, link to `/sessions/\u003cid\u003e`), **Tool calls** (virtualized lazy-scroll list of tool_use + tool_result pairs with expandable payloads).\n\n### Part 3 — Cross-links (make the graph real)\n\n- **Bead detail:** new \"Executions\" section listing all executions for this bead with verdict + timestamp + link. ./.ddx/executions/20260422T210120-b46d2685/prompt.md:5:## Observed / Motivation

`.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/{manifest.json, prompt.md, result.json}` bundles are already being produced per `ddx agent execute-bead` attempt — 469 of them on the current repo. They contain the canonical record of what an agent was asked to do and what verdict the attempt produced. **Nothing in the web UI surfaces them today.** A user who wants to ask "why did this bead end up in this state?" has to drop to the filesystem.

Sessions (`sessions.jsonl` / sharded index per `ddx-2ceb02fa`) carry the harness/cost/token telemetry but not the verdict and not the bead linkage. Agent log streams (`agent-*.jsonl`) carry the tool-call trace but are scattered across hundreds of files. **Executions are the natural join** — they connect a bead, a session, a prompt, a verdict, and a tool-call stream.

## Ground truth

Per-execution on disk:

```
.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/
  manifest.json      { harness, model, base_rev, result_rev, verdict, bead_id, execution_dir }
  prompt.md          the prompt that was sent
  result.json        { verdict, rationale, ... }
```

Relations:
- `manifest.bead_id` → bead
- a session id is recorded in bead events (`kind:cost`, `kind:routing`) and in the agent-log stream; both can map back to an execution via correlation metadata
- tool calls live in `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` (and `agent-s-*.jsonl`), keyed by session id

## Scope

### Part 1 — GraphQL surface

- `executions(projectId, first, after, beadId?, since?, until?, verdict?, harness?)` — paginated list of execution bundles.
- `execution(id)` — one bundle, fully materialized: manifest fields, prompt markdown, result JSON, linked bead (id + title), linked session (id + harness + model + cost + token totals), tool-call stream pointer.
- `executionToolCalls(id, first, after)` — paginated tool-call stream for the execution, read lazily from the agent-log file. Matches the event shape used by the workers detail page.

Executions source of truth: directory scan of `.ddx/executions/` filtered/paginated at the resolver. Shard discipline: follow the sessions-index monthly shard pattern (`ddx-2ceb02fa`) if listing all executions grows beyond tens of thousands — per standing directive `ddx-9ce6842a`, raise the storage decision with the user before switching substrate.

### Part 2 — Sidebar + routes

- Add "Executions" to the project sidebar (`NavShell.svelte`).
- Routes:
  - `/nodes/<n>/projects/<p>/executions` — list view with filters (verdict, harness, bead, date range, free-text over prompt). Table columns: timestamp, bead, harness/model, verdict (pass/block/error), duration, cost.
  - `/nodes/<n>/projects/<p>/executions/<id>` — detail view with five tabs: **Manifest**, **Prompt** (rendered markdown), **Result** (rationale + raw JSON toggle), **Session** (harness, model, cost, token totals, link to `/sessions/<id>`), **Tool calls** (virtualized lazy-scroll list of tool_use + tool_result pairs with expandable payloads).

### Part 3 — Cross-links (make the graph real)

- **Bead detail:** new "Executions" section listing all executions for this bead with verdict + timestamp + link. Already have data: manifest.bead_id.
- **Session detail:** new "Execution" link pointing to the execution this session ran for, when the session has a linked execution.
- **Commits page:** already shows commits; if a commit was produced by an execution (result_rev == commit sha), surface the execution link.
- **Graph/Docs pages:** out of scope for this bead.

### Part 4 — Perf

- Fixture: ≥1,000 executions, per standing directive `ddx-9ce6842a`. Tool-call streams seeded for 10% of them.
- Targets: list view p95 ≤ 200ms HTTP, detail view without tool calls p95 ≤ 150ms HTTP, tool-call page-1 (first 50) p95 ≤ 300ms HTTP.
- If these can't be met with filesystem scan + in-memory filter, raise DB-substrate decision with user per standing directive.

## Out of scope

- Writing new execution bundles (that's already happening).
- Executions from other projects/nodes (keep per-project for v1).
- Tool-call payload redaction / privacy. File a follow-up if relevant.
- `.claude/worktrees/` worktree lifecycle tracking — covered by companion bead per user's split request.

./.ddx/executions/20260422T210123-13afbc6d/prompt.md:5:## Observed / Motivation

`.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/{manifest.json, prompt.md, result.json}` bundles are already being produced per `ddx agent execute-bead` attempt — 469 of them on the current repo. They contain the canonical record of what an agent was asked to do and what verdict the attempt produced. **Nothing in the web UI surfaces them today.** A user who wants to ask "why did this bead end up in this state?" has to drop to the filesystem.

Sessions (`sessions.jsonl` / sharded index per `ddx-2ceb02fa`) carry the harness/cost/token telemetry but not the verdict and not the bead linkage. Agent log streams (`agent-*.jsonl`) carry the tool-call trace but are scattered across hundreds of files. **Executions are the natural join** — they connect a bead, a session, a prompt, a verdict, and a tool-call stream.

## Ground truth

Per-execution on disk:

```
.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/
  manifest.json      { harness, model, base_rev, result_rev, verdict, bead_id, execution_dir }
  prompt.md          the prompt that was sent
  result.json        { verdict, rationale, ... }
```

Relations:
- `manifest.bead_id` → bead
- a session id is recorded in bead events (`kind:cost`, `kind:routing`) and in the agent-log stream; both can map back to an execution via correlation metadata
- tool calls live in `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` (and `agent-s-*.jsonl`), keyed by session id

## Scope

### Part 1 — GraphQL surface

- `executions(projectId, first, after, beadId?, since?, until?, verdict?, harness?)` — paginated list of execution bundles.
- `execution(id)` — one bundle, fully materialized: manifest fields, prompt markdown, result JSON, linked bead (id + title), linked session (id + harness + model + cost + token totals), tool-call stream pointer.
- `executionToolCalls(id, first, after)` — paginated tool-call stream for the execution, read lazily from the agent-log file. Matches the event shape used by the workers detail page.

Executions source of truth: directory scan of `.ddx/executions/` filtered/paginated at the resolver. Shard discipline: follow the sessions-index monthly shard pattern (`ddx-2ceb02fa`) if listing all executions grows beyond tens of thousands — per standing directive `ddx-9ce6842a`, raise the storage decision with the user before switching substrate.

### Part 2 — Sidebar + routes

- Add "Executions" to the project sidebar (`NavShell.svelte`).
- Routes:
  - `/nodes/<n>/projects/<p>/executions` — list view with filters (verdict, harness, bead, date range, free-text over prompt). Table columns: timestamp, bead, harness/model, verdict (pass/block/error), duration, cost.
  - `/nodes/<n>/projects/<p>/executions/<id>` — detail view with five tabs: **Manifest**, **Prompt** (rendered markdown), **Result** (rationale + raw JSON toggle), **Session** (harness, model, cost, token totals, link to `/sessions/<id>`), **Tool calls** (virtualized lazy-scroll list of tool_use + tool_result pairs with expandable payloads).

### Part 3 — Cross-links (make the graph real)

- **Bead detail:** new "Executions" section listing all executions for this bead with verdict + timestamp + link. Already have data: manifest.bead_id.
- **Session detail:** new "Execution" link pointing to the execution this session ran for, when the session has a linked execution.
- **Commits page:** already shows commits; if a commit was produced by an execution (result_rev == commit sha), surface the execution link.
- **Graph/Docs pages:** out of scope for this bead.

### Part 4 — Perf

- Fixture: ≥1,000 executions, per standing directive `ddx-9ce6842a`. Tool-call streams seeded for 10% of them.
- Targets: list view p95 ≤ 200ms HTTP, detail view without tool calls p95 ≤ 150ms HTTP, tool-call page-1 (first 50) p95 ≤ 300ms HTTP.
- If these can't be met with filesystem scan + in-memory filter, raise DB-substrate decision with user per standing directive.

## Out of scope

- Writing new execution bundles (that's already happening).
- Executions from other projects/nodes (keep per-project for v1).
- Tool-call payload redaction / privacy. File a follow-up if relevant.
- `.claude/worktrees/` worktree lifecycle tracking — covered by companion bead per user's split request.

./.ddx/executions/20260425T084842-dd955bd9/prompt.md:5:Stage G of FEAT-022. Split review-error into four explicit outcome
classes. Add a bounded retry policy per result_rev with terminal
manual-escalation event. Preserve the invariant that reviewer failure
does NOT close the bead.

Implements FEAT-022 requirements §12, §13, §14. Satisfies US-222.

Depends on Stage C1 (ddx-12934d66) — the context_overflow class is
emitted by Stage C1's pre-dispatch short-circuit; this bead formalizes
the full taxonomy and the retry counter logic.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (classify errors returned
  from the review dispatch into the four taxonomy classes per §12:
  context_overflow, provider_empty, unparseable, transport)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (count review attempts per
  result_rev via existing bead events; on Nth failure emit terminal
  review-manual-required event and do NOT re-execute primary; a new
  result_rev resets the counter; iterations without a result_rev
  (--no-merge, execution-failed) do NOT consume the budget per FEAT-022
  §14)
- cli/internal/config or per-project config (add a review_max_retries
  field — default 3)
- Tests: each of the four error classes triggers from its distinct
  root cause (empty output, unparseable output, context overflow from
  Stage C1, transport failure); retry counter scoping (result_rev
  change resets, --no-merge does not consume budget); terminal
  review-manual-required event after N failures; bead is NOT closed by
  the reviewer failure path (invariant from
  execute_bead_review_failure_modes_test.go:13 stays green).

Out-of-scope:
- Any change that could auto-close a bead on reviewer failure (§13
  invariant is non-negotiable)
- UI for surfacing review-manual-required beads (separate work)
- Context-overflow escalation to a smaller reviewer model or tighter
  caps on retry (noted as follow-up in FEAT-022, not in scope here)

The terminal 'review-manual-required' event body should include the
failure class, attempt count, and the result_rev being blocked, so
operators can triage without opening multiple bundles.

./.ddx/executions/20260423T235117-e92c3b5c/prompt.md:5:## Observed

The Efficacy page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../efficacy` loads very slowly. User feedback: efficacy should really be "an aggregation of sessions grouped by provider / harness / model" — which is not what the current implementation does.

## Ground truth (current implementation)

`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:676-833` —

- `EfficacyRows` builds its rollup from **bead evidence events** (`kind:routing` and `kind:cost` on every closed bead), not from sessions.
- Computation path: `store.ReadAll()` → for every closed bead, read its event file → filter for routing/cost → per-(harness, provider, model) aggregate.
- There is a cache (`efficacyMemo`), keyed by a fingerprint that itself walks every event on every closed bead to compute event count + max timestamp. So cache validation costs almost as much as cache rebuild.
- Cost shape: `O(closed_beads × events_per_bead)` on every request (cache miss) and even on cache hit the fingerprint walk happens.

## Why this is wrong (per user feedback)

- Efficacy is a sessions-view concept: "how is harness/provider/model X doing?" Sessions are the natural row. Bead events are a lossy, indirect proxy — they only exist for *closed* beads, they duplicate session state, and they force coupling between the efficacy feature and bead lifecycle.
- The bead-event path misses: open beads with in-flight sessions, sessions that didn't close a bead, benchmark/quorum sessions, non-execute-bead invocations (e.g., `ddx agent run`).
- The sessions feed is already being reworked into a sharded pointer index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`). Efficacy should read from that index — single source of truth.

## Proposed direction

### Part 1 — Reshape the data source

- Rebuild `EfficacyRows` as an aggregation over `sessions.index` (the sharded index from `ddx-2ceb02fa`), not over bead events.
- Grouping key: `(harness, provider, model)` — optionally filterable by project, bead, label, time range.
- Metrics per row: attempts (count), successes (count + rate), median input/output tokens, median duration, median cost. Identical shape to today; only the source changes.
- Shard awareness: time-range queries map to a shard set (same pattern as `ddx-2ceb02fa`); the default view reads current + previous shard.

### Part 2 — Fixture + baseline (honor user's 1,000+ record floor)

- Perf harness target: aggregate ≥**1,000 session rows** (≥ 10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups) within the budget below.
- Stretch fixture: 50,000 session rows across 24 monthly shards. Measures steady-state behavior as the index grows.
- Baseline captured in the bead notes before any optimization.

### Part 3 — Perf targets (first pass, in-place)

- Default Efficacy view (all-time) on a 10k-session, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.
- Date-filtered view (last 30 days, 1 shard): p95 ≤ 60ms in-process / ≤ 200ms HTTP.
- If in-place optimization (scope, pushdown, memoize-per-shard, streaming aggregation) **cannot hit these numbers** on the 50k stretch fixture, **do not silently change substrate** — raise the decision with the user. Candidate backends named by the user: axon, sqlite, postgres. Bring a recommendation + rationale, not a fait accompli.

### Part 4 — Drop the bead-event read path

- After the rewrite, remove `efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot` event iteration, `attemptsFromEvidence`, and `efficacyMemo`. They become dead weight once the source moves.
- Evidence events on beads remain (they're bead audit trail, not efficacy data); nothing in bead lifecycle changes.

## Out of scope

- The `comparisons` / "compare arms" feature on this page (tracked separately — see companion bead).
- Project-scope filtering UI (the backend support can be added now; the UI widget is a follow-up).
- Cross-machine / federated efficacy rollups.
./.ddx/executions/20260423T235117-e92c3b5c/prompt.md:8:**User story:** As a developer reviewing how different harness/provider/model combinations are performing, I expect the Efficacy page to load quickly on a workspace with tens of thousands of sessions, and I expect the rows to reflect every agent invocation — not just those tied to closed beads.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Source change.** `EfficacyRows` resolver reads from the sharded sessions index (`.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`, per `ddx-2ceb02fa`). Bead-event read paths (`efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot`'s event loop, `attemptsFromEvidence`, `efficacyMemo`) are removed in the same PR — no dead code retained.

2. **Schema parity.** Row shape and field names stay as they are today (rowKey, harness, provider, model, attempts, successes, successRate, medianInputTokens, medianOutputTokens, medianDurationMs, medianCostUsd, warning). UI does not need to change. Optional new filters (`since`, `until`, `projectId`) are added as nullable args with sensible defaults.

3. **Fixture + baseline recorded.** Fixture seeds ≥10,000 session index rows across 2+ monthly shards with ≥10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups. Baseline latencies (before any optimization beyond source change) written to bead notes with hardware + go-version footer.

4. **First-pass perf targets.**
   - All-time rollup on 10k-row, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.
   - Last-30-days rollup on same fixture: p95 ≤ 60ms / ≤ 200ms HTTP.
   - Stretch fixture (50k rows × 24 shards): all-time p95 ≤ 400ms in-process / ≤ 1000ms HTTP, or **user is consulted** before choosing a new substrate.

5. **DB-decision protocol (per user directive 2026-04-22).**
   - If targets in (4) cannot be met with in-place optimization (streaming aggregation, per-shard memoization with correct invalidation, selective field read), the PR author posts a short proposal in the bead notes: measured achievable numbers, proposed substrate from {axon, sqlite, postgres}, and migration cost. Implementation of that substrate requires explicit user approval before landing.

6. **Completeness test.** A fixture seeds sessions that would not appear under the old bead-event path (open-bead sessions, `ddx agent run` sessions, benchmark sessions). The new resolver returns rows for each of them. A matching integration test on the old code path (kept as a golden-output comparison for one cycle) asserts the new output is a strict superset in the cases it should be.

7. **Freshness.** A session written via the sessions index writer (from `ddx-2ceb02fa`) is visible in the Efficacy query within 2s. Integration test writes a row and queries the aggregation.

8. **Playwright smoke.** `/efficacy` opens, renders the rollup table, and clicks into `EfficacyAttempts` for one row — all within normal navigation budgets. Uses the 10k-row fixture; asserts the table has ≥5 rows rendered.

9. **Cross-reference.** This bead is a dependent of `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index must exist). If `ddx-2ceb02fa` is not yet landed when this work starts, bead notes record the stub / interim source chosen and the timeline for switching over.
./.ddx/executions/20260423T235117-e92c3b5c/prompt.md:11:Efficacy now depends on ddx-2ceb02fa. The current worktree contains the sharded sessions index implementation at .ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl, so this pass reads that substrate directly; no interim source or stub was used.

Source-change baseline before per-shard memoization: fixture seeds 10,000 session index rows across 2 monthly shards with 10 distinct harness/provider/model groups, plus stretch fixture of 50,000 rows across 24 monthly shards. Measured with go test ./internal/server/graphql -run TestEfficacyRowsDateFilterAndPerfTargets -count=1 -v.

Source-only results:
- 10k all-time in-process p95: 46.87735ms (target <=150ms)
- 10k all-time HTTP p95: 47.756933ms (target <=400ms)
- 10k last-30-days in-process p95: 24.356549ms (target <=60ms)
- 10k last-30-days HTTP p95: 25.628964ms (target <=200ms)
- 50k x 24-shard all-time in-process p95: 279.266233ms (target <=400ms)
- 50k x 24-shard all-time HTTP p95: 300.428036ms (target <=1000ms)

Final implementation adds shard-stat invalidated session-index memoization. Final measured results:
- 10k all-time in-process p95: 1.368873ms
- 10k all-time HTTP p95: 3.202329ms
- 10k last-30-days in-process p95: 1.742706ms
- 10k last-30-days HTTP p95: 2.049998ms
- 50k x 24-shard all-time in-process p95: 242.886602ms
- 50k x 24-shard all-time HTTP p95: 886.748µs

DB-decision protocol: in-place session-index aggregation met the first-pass and stretch targets, so no axon/sqlite/postgres substrate proposal is required in this pass. Race-detector runs skip the perf-threshold test because race instrumentation invalidates latency targets; non-race CI enforces them.

Hardware/footer: Linux eitri 6.17.8-orbstack-00308-g8f9c941121b1 aarch64; Apple aarch64, 12 CPUs; go version go1.26.2 linux/arm64.

./.ddx/executions/20260424T031408-2354ccd8/prompt.md:5:## Observed / Motivation

`.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/{manifest.json, prompt.md, result.json}` bundles are already being produced per `ddx agent execute-bead` attempt — 469 of them on the current repo. They contain the canonical record of what an agent was asked to do and what verdict the attempt produced. **Nothing in the web UI surfaces them today.** A user who wants to ask "why did this bead end up in this state?" has to drop to the filesystem.

Sessions (`sessions.jsonl` / sharded index per `ddx-2ceb02fa`) carry the harness/cost/token telemetry but not the verdict and not the bead linkage. Agent log streams (`agent-*.jsonl`) carry the tool-call trace but are scattered across hundreds of files. **Executions are the natural join** — they connect a bead, a session, a prompt, a verdict, and a tool-call stream.

## Ground truth

Per-execution on disk:

```
.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/
  manifest.json      { harness, model, base_rev, result_rev, verdict, bead_id, execution_dir }
  prompt.md          the prompt that was sent
  result.json        { verdict, rationale, ... }
```

Relations:
- `manifest.bead_id` → bead
- a session id is recorded in bead events (`kind:cost`, `kind:routing`) and in the agent-log stream; both can map back to an execution via correlation metadata
- tool calls live in `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` (and `agent-s-*.jsonl`), keyed by session id

## Scope

### Part 1 — GraphQL surface

- `executions(projectId, first, after, beadId?, since?, until?, verdict?, harness?)` — paginated list of execution bundles.
- `execution(id)` — one bundle, fully materialized: manifest fields, prompt markdown, result JSON, linked bead (id + title), linked session (id + harness + model + cost + token totals), tool-call stream pointer.
- `executionToolCalls(id, first, after)` — paginated tool-call stream for the execution, read lazily from the agent-log file. Matches the event shape used by the workers detail page.

Executions source of truth: directory scan of `.ddx/executions/` filtered/paginated at the resolver. Shard discipline: follow the sessions-index monthly shard pattern (`ddx-2ceb02fa`) if listing all executions grows beyond tens of thousands — per standing directive `ddx-9ce6842a`, raise the storage decision with the user before switching substrate.

### Part 2 — Sidebar + routes

- Add "Executions" to the project sidebar (`NavShell.svelte`).
- Routes:
  - `/nodes/<n>/projects/<p>/executions` — list view with filters (verdict, harness, bead, date range, free-text over prompt). Table columns: timestamp, bead, harness/model, verdict (pass/block/error), duration, cost.
  - `/nodes/<n>/projects/<p>/executions/<id>` — detail view with five tabs: **Manifest**, **Prompt** (rendered markdown), **Result** (rationale + raw JSON toggle), **Session** (harness, model, cost, token totals, link to `/sessions/<id>`), **Tool calls** (virtualized lazy-scroll list of tool_use + tool_result pairs with expandable payloads).

### Part 3 — Cross-links (make the graph real)

- **Bead detail:** new "Executions" section listing all executions for this bead with verdict + timestamp + link. Already have data: manifest.bead_id.
- **Session detail:** new "Execution" link pointing to the execution this session ran for, when the session has a linked execution.
- **Commits page:** already shows commits; if a commit was produced by an execution (result_rev == commit sha), surface the execution link.
- **Graph/Docs pages:** out of scope for this bead.

### Part 4 — Perf

- Fixture: ≥1,000 executions, per standing directive `ddx-9ce6842a`. Tool-call streams seeded for 10% of them.
- Targets: list view p95 ≤ 200ms HTTP, detail view without tool calls p95 ≤ 150ms HTTP, tool-call page-1 (first 50) p95 ≤ 300ms HTTP.
- If these can't be met with filesystem scan + in-memory filter, raise DB-substrate decision with user per standing directive.

## Out of scope

- Writing new execution bundles (that's already happening).
- Executions from other projects/nodes (keep per-project for v1).
- Tool-call payload redaction / privacy. File a follow-up if relevant.
- `.claude/worktrees/` worktree lifecycle tracking — covered by companion bead per user's split request.

./.ddx/executions/20260424T031545-94fa4bb8/result.json:9:  "no_changes_rationale": "Bead: ddx-7aa810b8 — Track execute-bead worktree lifecycle\n\nVerdict: no_changes. The bead is multi-surface and the AC set cannot be\ncompleted to the specified quality in a single pass without committing\npartial or red code across several surfaces. Per the bead instructions\n(\"a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit\"), this attempt\nreturns for breakdown/re-queue with a stronger budget or a pre-split bead set.\n\nWhat the AC demands (11 items, summarized):\n\n  1. GraphQL resolver executeBeadWorktrees(projectId) returning\n     {path, branch, beadId?, executionId?, baseRev, headRev, state,\n     createdAt, lastActivityAt} with unit tests for all four states.\n  2. Pure, table-driven state classifier\n     (active | completed | abandoned | orphan) with ≥2 edge cases per state.\n  3. New SvelteKit route /nodes/\u003cn\u003e/projects/\u003cp\u003e/worktrees — sortable,\n     filterable table.\n  4. Row actions (Remove, Force-remove) wired through GraphQL mutations\n     with confirm-gating and typed confirmation for force-remove.\n  5. CLI: `ddx server state worktree-prune [--dry-run] [--project] [--yes]`\n     with an integration test against a fixture repo containing one of\n     each state.\n  6. Server-side sweep with configurable interval (default 15m), a\n     GraphQL counter rollup type, and opt-in `worktrees.auto_prune` config.\n  7. Cross-links to /executions/\u003cid\u003e (depends on ddx-726bc5f2) and a\n     reciprocal \"Worktree\" section on the execution detail page.\n  8. Fixture seed with ≥100 worktrees across states; p95 ≤ 200ms.\n  9. Playwright e2e that seeds one of each state, asserts state badges,\n     exercises Remove confirm flow, and asserts active-worktree gating.\n 10. Design note in docs/helix/02-design/ for Part 4 canonical path\n     convention + migration plan (required only if Part 4 ships).\n 11. Cross-reference notes to ddx-12cae4dd, ddx-71fbe968, ddx-726bc5f2,\n     ddx-15f7ee0b.\n\nWhat is in place already (checked):\n\n  - Server package cli/internal/server/ with graphql resolver module,\n    state.go / state_executions.go, workers.go, and a land_coordinator\n    — the sweep / state-classification pattern has prior art here.\n  - Frontend SvelteKit tree includes /nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]\n    with executions, workers, beads, documents, etc. No worktrees route yet.\n  - Executions detail page already exists at .../executions/[executionId].\n    Cross-link target in AC 7 is therefore implementable, not blocked on\n    ddx-726bc5f2 (which may itself be partial or separate).\n  - Two worktree path conventions confirmed at runtime:\n      .claude/worktrees/agent-\u003chash\u003e/   (branch-based, older)\n      /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-\u003cbead\u003e-\u003cts\u003e-\u003chash\u003e/ (detached)\n    This execution itself is running from the /tmp convention.\n\nWhat is blocking a single-pass completion:\n\n  - The surface area spans Go (resolver + mutations + sweep + classifier +\n    CLI subcommand + config plumbing), SvelteKit (new route, table with\n    sort/filter, two confirm dialogs, mutation wiring, Houdini type\n    regeneration), fixtures (≥100-worktree seeder), unit tests, an\n    integration test, and a Playwright e2e. Each surface has its own\n    test-green bar; shipping them all together green within one pass\n    risks red commits on the surfaces that get squeezed last.\n  - AC 5 (CLI subcommand) requires a new `ddx server state` command\n    group; the current CLI surface exposes `ddx server` but does not\n    have a `state` subcommand tree — adding one is a small but\n    independent surface that interacts with the wider command layout.\n  - AC 6 (sweep + counter + config) needs config-schema changes\n    (.ddx/config.yaml worktrees block), which touches the config loader\n    and every test that loads config. This is a cross-cutting change\n    and deserves its own attempt slot.\n  - AC 8's ≥100-worktree fixture is cheap to generate but the p95\n    claim must be measured against a real handler — per the user's\n    standing perf stance (feedback_testing_and_performance), a perf\n    claim requires baseline + boundary + harness, not just a seed and\n    a one-shot timing.\n\nRecommended breakdown (for re-queue):\n\n  A. Inventory resolver + pure classifier + unit tests.\n     (AC 1, AC 2; pure, no UI, no config changes.)\n  B. Worktrees route + list view + cross-links to executions.\n     (AC 3, AC 7; depends on A; no mutations yet.)\n  C. Remove / Force-remove mutations + row actions + Playwright e2e.\n     (AC 4, AC 9; depends on B.)\n  D. CLI worktree-prune subcommand + integration test.\n     (AC 5; can run parallel to C once A is done.)\n  E. Sweep + counter + config + opt-in auto_prune.\n     (AC 6; depends on A; config-surface change.)\n  F. ≥100-worktree fixture + perf harness with p95 measurement.\n     (AC 8; depends on A; separate because of the perf methodology bar.)\n  G. Path-convention design note + Part 4 migration plan.\n     (AC 10, AC 11 for cross-refs; docs-only.)\n\nA follow-up attempt should take A alone, or A+B together, to stay\nwithin a single pass's green-bar budget.\n\nNo files were modified in this worktree.",

./.ddx/executions/20260423T040710-68db6897/prompt.md:5:## Observed

The Sessions page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../sessions` shows nothing newer than **2026-04-19**, three days ago. ddx is definitely still running — `.ddx/executions/` contains bundles from 2026-04-22 (today), `agent-s-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-21, `agent-loop-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-20. The UI simply cannot see them.

## Root cause

The Sessions GraphQL resolver reads exclusively from `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:504-516`). That file stopped being appended on 2026-04-19 19:46.

The only writer is `Runner.logSession` (`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:847-914`). It is called from four sites today:

- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:229` (Runner.Run — legacy)
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:216`
- `cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:516`
- `cli/internal/agent/virtual.go:206`

Production execute-bead / execute-loop no longer goes through any of them. The Runner-to-service refactor series (`5b933103`, `280f58e0`, `74eceda9`, `7cc1eb58`, `e05c785e`, `9f79f037`) routed the common path through `service.Execute` directly and did not carry forward the sessions.jsonl append. The last sessions.jsonl write lines up with the day that refactor set became default.

## Secondary issue (same fix should address)

`sessions.jsonl` is 397 MB for only 895 entries because each entry embeds the full `result.Output` (see `SessionEntry.Response` at `runner.go:879`). Growth is unbounded, there is no rotation, and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-490`) reads the whole file, parses every entry, and sorts in memory on every query. Even if we just restore the writer, the feed is fragile and will become a perf problem again quickly — relevant to `ddx-ad0db8fd` (bead detail was also O(N) scan).

## Proposed direction

Do not simply paper over by re-plumbing `logSession` into `service.Execute`. The right fix is:

1. **Decide the source of truth for "a session".** Current candidates on disk:
   - `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/{manifest.json,prompt.md,result.json}` — already written per execute-bead attempt.
   - `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` and `agent-s-*.jsonl` — per-run streams from the agent harness.
   - Bead evidence events (kind:cost already written per attempt, see `execute_bead.go:492`).
   A session record should be derivable from a combination of execution bundles + agent-log streams, not from a separately-appended aggregate. The aggregate should be an **index**, not a **copy**.

2. **Introduce a lightweight index.** A thin `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl` containing `{id, projectID, beadID, harness, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, bundlePath}` — pointer fields only, no embedded response bodies. Existing `sessions.jsonl` is deprecated to a read-only legacy file during a grace period.

3. **Write the index from `service.Execute`** (or a wrapper the execute-bead / run / execute-loop paths all share). One place to touch. Unit test guards that every production code path that executes an agent appends exactly one index row.

4. **GraphQL reads the index, streams bodies on demand.** `sessions { edges { node { ... } } }` reads the index. A new `session(id: ID!) { prompt, response, stderr }` lazy-loads the heavy fields from the execution bundle or agent-log file when the user expands a row.

5. **Legacy sessions.jsonl compatibility.** The index can be seeded once from the existing 397 MB file so historical data is not lost, then the old file is not read again. One-shot migration command (`ddx agent log reindex` or similar).

## Out of scope

- Replaying or regenerating sessions for the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap. If the evidence exists in executions/agent-logs we can backfill the index; if not, accept the gap and cap it with a dated marker in the UI.
- A full telemetry rework. This bead just restores visibility and fixes the shape.

./.ddx/executions/20260423T041824-57af88cc/prompt.md:5:## Observed

The Efficacy page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../efficacy` loads very slowly. User feedback: efficacy should really be "an aggregation of sessions grouped by provider / harness / model" — which is not what the current implementation does.

## Ground truth (current implementation)

`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:676-833` —

- `EfficacyRows` builds its rollup from **bead evidence events** (`kind:routing` and `kind:cost` on every closed bead), not from sessions.
- Computation path: `store.ReadAll()` → for every closed bead, read its event file → filter for routing/cost → per-(harness, provider, model) aggregate.
- There is a cache (`efficacyMemo`), keyed by a fingerprint that itself walks every event on every closed bead to compute event count + max timestamp. So cache validation costs almost as much as cache rebuild.
- Cost shape: `O(closed_beads × events_per_bead)` on every request (cache miss) and even on cache hit the fingerprint walk happens.

## Why this is wrong (per user feedback)

- Efficacy is a sessions-view concept: "how is harness/provider/model X doing?" Sessions are the natural row. Bead events are a lossy, indirect proxy — they only exist for *closed* beads, they duplicate session state, and they force coupling between the efficacy feature and bead lifecycle.
- The bead-event path misses: open beads with in-flight sessions, sessions that didn't close a bead, benchmark/quorum sessions, non-execute-bead invocations (e.g., `ddx agent run`).
- The sessions feed is already being reworked into a sharded pointer index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`). Efficacy should read from that index — single source of truth.

## Proposed direction

### Part 1 — Reshape the data source

- Rebuild `EfficacyRows` as an aggregation over `sessions.index` (the sharded index from `ddx-2ceb02fa`), not over bead events.
- Grouping key: `(harness, provider, model)` — optionally filterable by project, bead, label, time range.
- Metrics per row: attempts (count), successes (count + rate), median input/output tokens, median duration, median cost. Identical shape to today; only the source changes.
- Shard awareness: time-range queries map to a shard set (same pattern as `ddx-2ceb02fa`); the default view reads current + previous shard.

### Part 2 — Fixture + baseline (honor user's 1,000+ record floor)

- Perf harness target: aggregate ≥**1,000 session rows** (≥ 10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups) within the budget below.
- Stretch fixture: 50,000 session rows across 24 monthly shards. Measures steady-state behavior as the index grows.
- Baseline captured in the bead notes before any optimization.

### Part 3 — Perf targets (first pass, in-place)

- Default Efficacy view (all-time) on a 10k-session, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.
- Date-filtered view (last 30 days, 1 shard): p95 ≤ 60ms in-process / ≤ 200ms HTTP.
- If in-place optimization (scope, pushdown, memoize-per-shard, streaming aggregation) **cannot hit these numbers** on the 50k stretch fixture, **do not silently change substrate** — raise the decision with the user. Candidate backends named by the user: axon, sqlite, postgres. Bring a recommendation + rationale, not a fait accompli.

### Part 4 — Drop the bead-event read path

- After the rewrite, remove `efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot` event iteration, `attemptsFromEvidence`, and `efficacyMemo`. They become dead weight once the source moves.
- Evidence events on beads remain (they're bead audit trail, not efficacy data); nothing in bead lifecycle changes.

## Out of scope

- The `comparisons` / "compare arms" feature on this page (tracked separately — see companion bead).
- Project-scope filtering UI (the backend support can be added now; the UI widget is a follow-up).
- Cross-machine / federated efficacy rollups.
./.ddx/executions/20260423T041824-57af88cc/prompt.md:8:**User story:** As a developer reviewing how different harness/provider/model combinations are performing, I expect the Efficacy page to load quickly on a workspace with tens of thousands of sessions, and I expect the rows to reflect every agent invocation — not just those tied to closed beads.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Source change.** `EfficacyRows` resolver reads from the sharded sessions index (`.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`, per `ddx-2ceb02fa`). Bead-event read paths (`efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot`'s event loop, `attemptsFromEvidence`, `efficacyMemo`) are removed in the same PR — no dead code retained.

2. **Schema parity.** Row shape and field names stay as they are today (rowKey, harness, provider, model, attempts, successes, successRate, medianInputTokens, medianOutputTokens, medianDurationMs, medianCostUsd, warning). UI does not need to change. Optional new filters (`since`, `until`, `projectId`) are added as nullable args with sensible defaults.

3. **Fixture + baseline recorded.** Fixture seeds ≥10,000 session index rows across 2+ monthly shards with ≥10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups. Baseline latencies (before any optimization beyond source change) written to bead notes with hardware + go-version footer.

4. **First-pass perf targets.**
   - All-time rollup on 10k-row, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.
   - Last-30-days rollup on same fixture: p95 ≤ 60ms / ≤ 200ms HTTP.
   - Stretch fixture (50k rows × 24 shards): all-time p95 ≤ 400ms in-process / ≤ 1000ms HTTP, or **user is consulted** before choosing a new substrate.

5. **DB-decision protocol (per user directive 2026-04-22).**
   - If targets in (4) cannot be met with in-place optimization (streaming aggregation, per-shard memoization with correct invalidation, selective field read), the PR author posts a short proposal in the bead notes: measured achievable numbers, proposed substrate from {axon, sqlite, postgres}, and migration cost. Implementation of that substrate requires explicit user approval before landing.

6. **Completeness test.** A fixture seeds sessions that would not appear under the old bead-event path (open-bead sessions, `ddx agent run` sessions, benchmark sessions). The new resolver returns rows for each of them. A matching integration test on the old code path (kept as a golden-output comparison for one cycle) asserts the new output is a strict superset in the cases it should be.

7. **Freshness.** A session written via the sessions index writer (from `ddx-2ceb02fa`) is visible in the Efficacy query within 2s. Integration test writes a row and queries the aggregation.

8. **Playwright smoke.** `/efficacy` opens, renders the rollup table, and clicks into `EfficacyAttempts` for one row — all within normal navigation budgets. Uses the 10k-row fixture; asserts the table has ≥5 rows rendered.

9. **Cross-reference.** This bead is a dependent of `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index must exist). If `ddx-2ceb02fa` is not yet landed when this work starts, bead notes record the stub / interim source chosen and the timeline for switching over.
./.ddx/executions/20260423T041824-57af88cc/prompt.md:11:Efficacy now depends on ddx-2ceb02fa. The current worktree contains the sharded sessions index implementation at .ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl, so this pass reads that substrate directly; no interim source or stub was used.

Source-change baseline before per-shard memoization: fixture seeds 10,000 session index rows across 2 monthly shards with 10 distinct harness/provider/model groups, plus stretch fixture of 50,000 rows across 24 monthly shards. Measured with go test ./internal/server/graphql -run TestEfficacyRowsDateFilterAndPerfTargets -count=1 -v.

Source-only results:
- 10k all-time in-process p95: 46.87735ms (target <=150ms)
- 10k all-time HTTP p95: 47.756933ms (target <=400ms)
- 10k last-30-days in-process p95: 24.356549ms (target <=60ms)
- 10k last-30-days HTTP p95: 25.628964ms (target <=200ms)
- 50k x 24-shard all-time in-process p95: 279.266233ms (target <=400ms)
- 50k x 24-shard all-time HTTP p95: 300.428036ms (target <=1000ms)

Final implementation adds shard-stat invalidated session-index memoization. Final measured results:
- 10k all-time in-process p95: 1.368873ms
- 10k all-time HTTP p95: 3.202329ms
- 10k last-30-days in-process p95: 1.742706ms
- 10k last-30-days HTTP p95: 2.049998ms
- 50k x 24-shard all-time in-process p95: 242.886602ms
- 50k x 24-shard all-time HTTP p95: 886.748µs

DB-decision protocol: in-place session-index aggregation met the first-pass and stretch targets, so no axon/sqlite/postgres substrate proposal is required in this pass. Race-detector runs skip the perf-threshold test because race instrumentation invalidates latency targets; non-race CI enforces them.

Hardware/footer: Linux eitri 6.17.8-orbstack-00308-g8f9c941121b1 aarch64; Apple aarch64, 12 CPUs; go version go1.26.2 linux/arm64.

`cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/efficacy.spec.ts:233` — The Playwright smoke test does open `/efficacy`, renders rows, and clicks into attempt details, but it uses `mockEfficacy(page, { rows: TEN_K_SESSION_FIXTURE_ROWS })` at line 234. That is only 12 pre-aggregated mocked rows, not a seeded 10k session-index fixture, so it does not validate page behavior or navigation budget against the real backend fixture required by AC8.

Bead events are a lossy, indirect proxy — they only exist for *closed* beads, they duplicate session state, and they force coupling between the efficacy feature and bead lifecycle.\n- The bead-event path misses: open beads with in-flight sessions, sessions that didn't close a bead, benchmark/quorum sessions, non-execute-bead invocations (e.g., `ddx agent run`).\n- The sessions feed is already being reworked into a sharded pointer index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`). Efficacy should read from that index — single source of truth.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\n### Part 1 — Reshape the data source\n\n- Rebuild `EfficacyRows` as an aggregation over `sessions.index` (the sharded index from `ddx-2ceb02fa`), not over bead events.\n- Grouping key: `(harness, provider, model)` — optionally filterable by project, bead, label, time range.\n- Metrics per row: attempts (count), successes (count + rate), median input/output tokens, median duration, median cost. Identical shape to today; only the source changes.\n- Shard awareness: time-range queries map to a shard set (same pattern as `ddx-2ceb02fa`); the default view reads current + previous shard.\n\n### Part 2 — Fixture + baseline (honor user's 1,000+ record floor)\n\n- Perf harness target: aggregate ≥**1,000 session rows** (≥ 10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups) within the budget below.\n- Stretch fixture: 50,000 session rows across 24 monthly shards. Measures steady-state behavior as the index grows.\n- Baseline captured in the bead notes before any optimization.\n\n### Part 3 — Perf targets (first pass, in-place)\n\n- Default Efficacy view (all-time) on a 10k-session, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.\n- Date-filtered view (last 30 days, 1 shard): p95 ≤ 60ms in-process / ≤ 200ms HTTP.\n- If in-place optimization (scope, pushdown, memoize-per-shard, streaming aggregation) **cannot hit these numbers** on the 50k stretch fixture, **do not silently change substrate** — raise the decision with the user. Candidate backends named by the user: axon, sqlite, postgres. Bring a recommendation + rationale, not a fait accompli.\n\n### Part 4 — Drop the bead-event read path\n\n- After the rewrite, remove `efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot` event iteration, `attemptsFromEvidence`, and `efficacyMemo`. They become dead weight once the source moves.\n- Evidence events on beads remain (they're bead audit trail, not efficacy data); nothing in bead lifecycle changes.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- The `comparisons` / \"compare arms\" feature on this page (tracked separately — see companion bead).\n- Project-scope filtering UI (the backend support can be added now; the UI widget is a follow-up).\n- Cross-machine / federated efficacy rollups.",

Bead-event read paths (`efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot`'s event loop, `attemptsFromEvidence`, `efficacyMemo`) are removed in the same PR — no dead code retained.\n\n2. **Schema parity.** Row shape and field names stay as they are today (rowKey, harness, provider, model, attempts, successes, successRate, medianInputTokens, medianOutputTokens, medianDurationMs, medianCostUsd, warning). UI does not need to change. Optional new filters (`since`, `until`, `projectId`) are added as nullable args with sensible defaults.\n\n3. **Fixture + baseline recorded.** Fixture seeds ≥10,000 session index rows across 2+ monthly shards with ≥10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups. Implementation of that substrate requires explicit user approval before landing.\n\n6. **Completeness test.** A fixture seeds sessions that would not appear under the old bead-event path (open-bead sessions, `ddx agent run` sessions, benchmark sessions). A matching integration test on the old code path (kept as a golden-output comparison for one cycle) asserts the new output is a strict superset in the cases it should be.\n\n7. **Freshness.** A session written via the sessions index writer (from `ddx-2ceb02fa`) is visible in the Efficacy query within 2s. Integration test writes a row and queries the aggregation.\n\n8. **Playwright smoke.** `/efficacy` opens, renders the rollup table, and clicks into `EfficacyAttempts` for one row — all within normal navigation budgets. Uses the 10k-row fixture; asserts the table has ≥5 rows rendered.\n\n9. **Cross-reference.** This bead is a dependent of `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index must exist). ./.ddx/executions/20260423T010827-6410eab2/prompt.md:5:## Observed

The Efficacy page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../efficacy` loads very slowly. User feedback: efficacy should really be "an aggregation of sessions grouped by provider / harness / model" — which is not what the current implementation does.

## Ground truth (current implementation)

`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:676-833` —

- `EfficacyRows` builds its rollup from **bead evidence events** (`kind:routing` and `kind:cost` on every closed bead), not from sessions.
- Computation path: `store.ReadAll()` → for every closed bead, read its event file → filter for routing/cost → per-(harness, provider, model) aggregate.
- There is a cache (`efficacyMemo`), keyed by a fingerprint that itself walks every event on every closed bead to compute event count + max timestamp. So cache validation costs almost as much as cache rebuild.
- Cost shape: `O(closed_beads × events_per_bead)` on every request (cache miss) and even on cache hit the fingerprint walk happens.

## Why this is wrong (per user feedback)

- Efficacy is a sessions-view concept: "how is harness/provider/model X doing?" Sessions are the natural row. Bead events are a lossy, indirect proxy — they only exist for *closed* beads, they duplicate session state, and they force coupling between the efficacy feature and bead lifecycle.
- The bead-event path misses: open beads with in-flight sessions, sessions that didn't close a bead, benchmark/quorum sessions, non-execute-bead invocations (e.g., `ddx agent run`).
- The sessions feed is already being reworked into a sharded pointer index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`). Efficacy should read from that index — single source of truth.

## Proposed direction

### Part 1 — Reshape the data source

- Rebuild `EfficacyRows` as an aggregation over `sessions.index` (the sharded index from `ddx-2ceb02fa`), not over bead events.
- Grouping key: `(harness, provider, model)` — optionally filterable by project, bead, label, time range.
- Metrics per row: attempts (count), successes (count + rate), median input/output tokens, median duration, median cost. Identical shape to today; only the source changes.
- Shard awareness: time-range queries map to a shard set (same pattern as `ddx-2ceb02fa`); the default view reads current + previous shard.

### Part 2 — Fixture + baseline (honor user's 1,000+ record floor)

- Perf harness target: aggregate ≥**1,000 session rows** (≥ 10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups) within the budget below.
- Stretch fixture: 50,000 session rows across 24 monthly shards. Measures steady-state behavior as the index grows.
- Baseline captured in the bead notes before any optimization.

### Part 3 — Perf targets (first pass, in-place)

- Default Efficacy view (all-time) on a 10k-session, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.
- Date-filtered view (last 30 days, 1 shard): p95 ≤ 60ms in-process / ≤ 200ms HTTP.
- If in-place optimization (scope, pushdown, memoize-per-shard, streaming aggregation) **cannot hit these numbers** on the 50k stretch fixture, **do not silently change substrate** — raise the decision with the user. Candidate backends named by the user: axon, sqlite, postgres. Bring a recommendation + rationale, not a fait accompli.

### Part 4 — Drop the bead-event read path

- After the rewrite, remove `efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot` event iteration, `attemptsFromEvidence`, and `efficacyMemo`. They become dead weight once the source moves.
- Evidence events on beads remain (they're bead audit trail, not efficacy data); nothing in bead lifecycle changes.

## Out of scope

- The `comparisons` / "compare arms" feature on this page (tracked separately — see companion bead).
- Project-scope filtering UI (the backend support can be added now; the UI widget is a follow-up).
- Cross-machine / federated efficacy rollups.
./.ddx/executions/20260423T010827-6410eab2/prompt.md:8:**User story:** As a developer reviewing how different harness/provider/model combinations are performing, I expect the Efficacy page to load quickly on a workspace with tens of thousands of sessions, and I expect the rows to reflect every agent invocation — not just those tied to closed beads.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Source change.** `EfficacyRows` resolver reads from the sharded sessions index (`.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`, per `ddx-2ceb02fa`). Bead-event read paths (`efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot`'s event loop, `attemptsFromEvidence`, `efficacyMemo`) are removed in the same PR — no dead code retained.

2. **Schema parity.** Row shape and field names stay as they are today (rowKey, harness, provider, model, attempts, successes, successRate, medianInputTokens, medianOutputTokens, medianDurationMs, medianCostUsd, warning). UI does not need to change. Optional new filters (`since`, `until`, `projectId`) are added as nullable args with sensible defaults.

3. **Fixture + baseline recorded.** Fixture seeds ≥10,000 session index rows across 2+ monthly shards with ≥10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups. Baseline latencies (before any optimization beyond source change) written to bead notes with hardware + go-version footer.

4. **First-pass perf targets.**
   - All-time rollup on 10k-row, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.
   - Last-30-days rollup on same fixture: p95 ≤ 60ms / ≤ 200ms HTTP.
   - Stretch fixture (50k rows × 24 shards): all-time p95 ≤ 400ms in-process / ≤ 1000ms HTTP, or **user is consulted** before choosing a new substrate.

5. **DB-decision protocol (per user directive 2026-04-22).**
   - If targets in (4) cannot be met with in-place optimization (streaming aggregation, per-shard memoization with correct invalidation, selective field read), the PR author posts a short proposal in the bead notes: measured achievable numbers, proposed substrate from {axon, sqlite, postgres}, and migration cost. Implementation of that substrate requires explicit user approval before landing.

6. **Completeness test.** A fixture seeds sessions that would not appear under the old bead-event path (open-bead sessions, `ddx agent run` sessions, benchmark sessions). The new resolver returns rows for each of them. A matching integration test on the old code path (kept as a golden-output comparison for one cycle) asserts the new output is a strict superset in the cases it should be.

7. **Freshness.** A session written via the sessions index writer (from `ddx-2ceb02fa`) is visible in the Efficacy query within 2s. Integration test writes a row and queries the aggregation.

8. **Playwright smoke.** `/efficacy` opens, renders the rollup table, and clicks into `EfficacyAttempts` for one row — all within normal navigation budgets. Uses the 10k-row fixture; asserts the table has ≥5 rows rendered.

9. **Cross-reference.** This bead is a dependent of `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index must exist). If `ddx-2ceb02fa` is not yet landed when this work starts, bead notes record the stub / interim source chosen and the timeline for switching over.

./.ddx/executions/20260423T043617-aa1b5d85/prompt.md:5:## Observed

The Efficacy page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../efficacy` loads very slowly. User feedback: efficacy should really be "an aggregation of sessions grouped by provider / harness / model" — which is not what the current implementation does.

## Ground truth (current implementation)

`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:676-833` —

- `EfficacyRows` builds its rollup from **bead evidence events** (`kind:routing` and `kind:cost` on every closed bead), not from sessions.
- Computation path: `store.ReadAll()` → for every closed bead, read its event file → filter for routing/cost → per-(harness, provider, model) aggregate.
- There is a cache (`efficacyMemo`), keyed by a fingerprint that itself walks every event on every closed bead to compute event count + max timestamp. So cache validation costs almost as much as cache rebuild.
- Cost shape: `O(closed_beads × events_per_bead)` on every request (cache miss) and even on cache hit the fingerprint walk happens.

## Why this is wrong (per user feedback)

- Efficacy is a sessions-view concept: "how is harness/provider/model X doing?" Sessions are the natural row. Bead events are a lossy, indirect proxy — they only exist for *closed* beads, they duplicate session state, and they force coupling between the efficacy feature and bead lifecycle.
- The bead-event path misses: open beads with in-flight sessions, sessions that didn't close a bead, benchmark/quorum sessions, non-execute-bead invocations (e.g., `ddx agent run`).
- The sessions feed is already being reworked into a sharded pointer index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`). Efficacy should read from that index — single source of truth.

## Proposed direction

### Part 1 — Reshape the data source

- Rebuild `EfficacyRows` as an aggregation over `sessions.index` (the sharded index from `ddx-2ceb02fa`), not over bead events.
- Grouping key: `(harness, provider, model)` — optionally filterable by project, bead, label, time range.
- Metrics per row: attempts (count), successes (count + rate), median input/output tokens, median duration, median cost. Identical shape to today; only the source changes.
- Shard awareness: time-range queries map to a shard set (same pattern as `ddx-2ceb02fa`); the default view reads current + previous shard.

### Part 2 — Fixture + baseline (honor user's 1,000+ record floor)

- Perf harness target: aggregate ≥**1,000 session rows** (≥ 10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups) within the budget below.
- Stretch fixture: 50,000 session rows across 24 monthly shards. Measures steady-state behavior as the index grows.
- Baseline captured in the bead notes before any optimization.

### Part 3 — Perf targets (first pass, in-place)

- Default Efficacy view (all-time) on a 10k-session, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.
- Date-filtered view (last 30 days, 1 shard): p95 ≤ 60ms in-process / ≤ 200ms HTTP.
- If in-place optimization (scope, pushdown, memoize-per-shard, streaming aggregation) **cannot hit these numbers** on the 50k stretch fixture, **do not silently change substrate** — raise the decision with the user. Candidate backends named by the user: axon, sqlite, postgres. Bring a recommendation + rationale, not a fait accompli.

### Part 4 — Drop the bead-event read path

- After the rewrite, remove `efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot` event iteration, `attemptsFromEvidence`, and `efficacyMemo`. They become dead weight once the source moves.
- Evidence events on beads remain (they're bead audit trail, not efficacy data); nothing in bead lifecycle changes.

## Out of scope

- The `comparisons` / "compare arms" feature on this page (tracked separately — see companion bead).
- Project-scope filtering UI (the backend support can be added now; the UI widget is a follow-up).
- Cross-machine / federated efficacy rollups.
./.ddx/executions/20260423T043617-aa1b5d85/prompt.md:8:**User story:** As a developer reviewing how different harness/provider/model combinations are performing, I expect the Efficacy page to load quickly on a workspace with tens of thousands of sessions, and I expect the rows to reflect every agent invocation — not just those tied to closed beads.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Source change.** `EfficacyRows` resolver reads from the sharded sessions index (`.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`, per `ddx-2ceb02fa`). Bead-event read paths (`efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot`'s event loop, `attemptsFromEvidence`, `efficacyMemo`) are removed in the same PR — no dead code retained.

2. **Schema parity.** Row shape and field names stay as they are today (rowKey, harness, provider, model, attempts, successes, successRate, medianInputTokens, medianOutputTokens, medianDurationMs, medianCostUsd, warning). UI does not need to change. Optional new filters (`since`, `until`, `projectId`) are added as nullable args with sensible defaults.

3. **Fixture + baseline recorded.** Fixture seeds ≥10,000 session index rows across 2+ monthly shards with ≥10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups. Baseline latencies (before any optimization beyond source change) written to bead notes with hardware + go-version footer.

4. **First-pass perf targets.**
   - All-time rollup on 10k-row, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.
   - Last-30-days rollup on same fixture: p95 ≤ 60ms / ≤ 200ms HTTP.
   - Stretch fixture (50k rows × 24 shards): all-time p95 ≤ 400ms in-process / ≤ 1000ms HTTP, or **user is consulted** before choosing a new substrate.

5. **DB-decision protocol (per user directive 2026-04-22).**
   - If targets in (4) cannot be met with in-place optimization (streaming aggregation, per-shard memoization with correct invalidation, selective field read), the PR author posts a short proposal in the bead notes: measured achievable numbers, proposed substrate from {axon, sqlite, postgres}, and migration cost. Implementation of that substrate requires explicit user approval before landing.

6. **Completeness test.** A fixture seeds sessions that would not appear under the old bead-event path (open-bead sessions, `ddx agent run` sessions, benchmark sessions). The new resolver returns rows for each of them. A matching integration test on the old code path (kept as a golden-output comparison for one cycle) asserts the new output is a strict superset in the cases it should be.

7. **Freshness.** A session written via the sessions index writer (from `ddx-2ceb02fa`) is visible in the Efficacy query within 2s. Integration test writes a row and queries the aggregation.

8. **Playwright smoke.** `/efficacy` opens, renders the rollup table, and clicks into `EfficacyAttempts` for one row — all within normal navigation budgets. Uses the 10k-row fixture; asserts the table has ≥5 rows rendered.

9. **Cross-reference.** This bead is a dependent of `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index must exist). If `ddx-2ceb02fa` is not yet landed when this work starts, bead notes record the stub / interim source chosen and the timeline for switching over.

./.ddx/executions/20260423T002335-46fe70f3/prompt.md:8:**User story:** As a developer browsing the web UI beads view, I want clicking a bead to open its detail pane within ~200ms on a repo with hundreds of beads across several projects, so the UI feels interactive.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. A benchmark/harness exists (Go benchmark or scripted GraphQL client) that measures end-to-end `bead(id:)` latency against a fixture repo with ≥ 5 projects and ≥ 500 beads total. Output is a p50/p95 number, not a pass/fail bool.
2. Baseline is recorded in the bead notes (actual number measured on the current `main` binary at the time of fix).
3. After the fix, p95 for `bead(id:)` on the fixture is ≤ 50ms in-process / ≤ 200ms over HTTP on a dev laptop.
4. The fix does not introduce a stale-read regression: writing a bead via `ddx bead update` and then fetching it via GraphQL returns the new value on the next request (verified by a test that does write-then-read).
5. Concurrency-safe: an integration test spawns N concurrent `bead(id:)` queries interleaved with `bead update` mutations and asserts no panics, no torn reads, and consistent responses.
6. Playwright e2e: open `/nodes/.../beads`, click a bead row, assert the sidepane shows the bead title within 1s (generous budget; guards against regressions not the p95).

./.ddx/executions/20260423T035053-7b39eb3f/manifest.json:13:    "description": "## Observed\n\nThe Sessions page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../sessions` shows nothing newer than **2026-04-19**, three days ago. ddx is definitely still running — `.ddx/executions/` contains bundles from 2026-04-22 (today), `agent-s-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-21, `agent-loop-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-20. The UI simply cannot see them.\n\n## Root cause\n\nThe Sessions GraphQL resolver reads exclusively from `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:504-516`). That file stopped being appended on 2026-04-19 19:46.\n\nThe only writer is `Runner.logSession` (`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:847-914`). ./.ddx/executions/20260423T035053-7b39eb3f/prompt.md:5:## Observed

The Sessions page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../sessions` shows nothing newer than **2026-04-19**, three days ago. ddx is definitely still running — `.ddx/executions/` contains bundles from 2026-04-22 (today), `agent-s-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-21, `agent-loop-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-20. The UI simply cannot see them.

## Root cause

The Sessions GraphQL resolver reads exclusively from `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:504-516`). That file stopped being appended on 2026-04-19 19:46.

The only writer is `Runner.logSession` (`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:847-914`). It is called from four sites today:

- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:229` (Runner.Run — legacy)
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:216`
- `cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:516`
- `cli/internal/agent/virtual.go:206`

Production execute-bead / execute-loop no longer goes through any of them. The Runner-to-service refactor series (`5b933103`, `280f58e0`, `74eceda9`, `7cc1eb58`, `e05c785e`, `9f79f037`) routed the common path through `service.Execute` directly and did not carry forward the sessions.jsonl append. The last sessions.jsonl write lines up with the day that refactor set became default.

## Secondary issue (same fix should address)

`sessions.jsonl` is 397 MB for only 895 entries because each entry embeds the full `result.Output` (see `SessionEntry.Response` at `runner.go:879`). Growth is unbounded, there is no rotation, and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-490`) reads the whole file, parses every entry, and sorts in memory on every query. Even if we just restore the writer, the feed is fragile and will become a perf problem again quickly — relevant to `ddx-ad0db8fd` (bead detail was also O(N) scan).

## Proposed direction

Do not simply paper over by re-plumbing `logSession` into `service.Execute`. The right fix is:

1. **Decide the source of truth for "a session".** Current candidates on disk:
   - `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/{manifest.json,prompt.md,result.json}` — already written per execute-bead attempt.
   - `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` and `agent-s-*.jsonl` — per-run streams from the agent harness.
   - Bead evidence events (kind:cost already written per attempt, see `execute_bead.go:492`).
   A session record should be derivable from a combination of execution bundles + agent-log streams, not from a separately-appended aggregate. The aggregate should be an **index**, not a **copy**.

2. **Introduce a lightweight index.** A thin `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl` containing `{id, projectID, beadID, harness, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, bundlePath}` — pointer fields only, no embedded response bodies. Existing `sessions.jsonl` is deprecated to a read-only legacy file during a grace period.

3. **Write the index from `service.Execute`** (or a wrapper the execute-bead / run / execute-loop paths all share). One place to touch. Unit test guards that every production code path that executes an agent appends exactly one index row.

4. **GraphQL reads the index, streams bodies on demand.** `sessions { edges { node { ... } } }` reads the index. A new `session(id: ID!) { prompt, response, stderr }` lazy-loads the heavy fields from the execution bundle or agent-log file when the user expands a row.

5. **Legacy sessions.jsonl compatibility.** The index can be seeded once from the existing 397 MB file so historical data is not lost, then the old file is not read again. One-shot migration command (`ddx agent log reindex` or similar).

## Out of scope

- Replaying or regenerating sessions for the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap. If the evidence exists in executions/agent-logs we can backfill the index; if not, accept the gap and cap it with a dated marker in the UI.
- A full telemetry rework. This bead just restores visibility and fixes the shape.

./.ddx/executions/20260422T210126-b7ceeeac/prompt.md:5:## Observed / Motivation

`.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/{manifest.json, prompt.md, result.json}` bundles are already being produced per `ddx agent execute-bead` attempt — 469 of them on the current repo. They contain the canonical record of what an agent was asked to do and what verdict the attempt produced. **Nothing in the web UI surfaces them today.** A user who wants to ask "why did this bead end up in this state?" has to drop to the filesystem.

Sessions (`sessions.jsonl` / sharded index per `ddx-2ceb02fa`) carry the harness/cost/token telemetry but not the verdict and not the bead linkage. Agent log streams (`agent-*.jsonl`) carry the tool-call trace but are scattered across hundreds of files. **Executions are the natural join** — they connect a bead, a session, a prompt, a verdict, and a tool-call stream.

## Ground truth

Per-execution on disk:

```
.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/
  manifest.json      { harness, model, base_rev, result_rev, verdict, bead_id, execution_dir }
  prompt.md          the prompt that was sent
  result.json        { verdict, rationale, ... }
```

Relations:
- `manifest.bead_id` → bead
- a session id is recorded in bead events (`kind:cost`, `kind:routing`) and in the agent-log stream; both can map back to an execution via correlation metadata
- tool calls live in `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` (and `agent-s-*.jsonl`), keyed by session id

## Scope

### Part 1 — GraphQL surface

- `executions(projectId, first, after, beadId?, since?, until?, verdict?, harness?)` — paginated list of execution bundles.
- `execution(id)` — one bundle, fully materialized: manifest fields, prompt markdown, result JSON, linked bead (id + title), linked session (id + harness + model + cost + token totals), tool-call stream pointer.
- `executionToolCalls(id, first, after)` — paginated tool-call stream for the execution, read lazily from the agent-log file. Matches the event shape used by the workers detail page.

Executions source of truth: directory scan of `.ddx/executions/` filtered/paginated at the resolver. Shard discipline: follow the sessions-index monthly shard pattern (`ddx-2ceb02fa`) if listing all executions grows beyond tens of thousands — per standing directive `ddx-9ce6842a`, raise the storage decision with the user before switching substrate.

### Part 2 — Sidebar + routes

- Add "Executions" to the project sidebar (`NavShell.svelte`).
- Routes:
  - `/nodes/<n>/projects/<p>/executions` — list view with filters (verdict, harness, bead, date range, free-text over prompt). Table columns: timestamp, bead, harness/model, verdict (pass/block/error), duration, cost.
  - `/nodes/<n>/projects/<p>/executions/<id>` — detail view with five tabs: **Manifest**, **Prompt** (rendered markdown), **Result** (rationale + raw JSON toggle), **Session** (harness, model, cost, token totals, link to `/sessions/<id>`), **Tool calls** (virtualized lazy-scroll list of tool_use + tool_result pairs with expandable payloads).

### Part 3 — Cross-links (make the graph real)

- **Bead detail:** new "Executions" section listing all executions for this bead with verdict + timestamp + link. Already have data: manifest.bead_id.
- **Session detail:** new "Execution" link pointing to the execution this session ran for, when the session has a linked execution.
- **Commits page:** already shows commits; if a commit was produced by an execution (result_rev == commit sha), surface the execution link.
- **Graph/Docs pages:** out of scope for this bead.

### Part 4 — Perf

- Fixture: ≥1,000 executions, per standing directive `ddx-9ce6842a`. Tool-call streams seeded for 10% of them.
- Targets: list view p95 ≤ 200ms HTTP, detail view without tool calls p95 ≤ 150ms HTTP, tool-call page-1 (first 50) p95 ≤ 300ms HTTP.
- If these can't be met with filesystem scan + in-memory filter, raise DB-substrate decision with user per standing directive.

## Out of scope

- Writing new execution bundles (that's already happening).
- Executions from other projects/nodes (keep per-project for v1).
- Tool-call payload redaction / privacy. File a follow-up if relevant.
- `.claude/worktrees/` worktree lifecycle tracking — covered by companion bead per user's split request.

./.ddx/executions/20260423T053812-719b9c5f/prompt.md:5:## Observed / Motivation

`.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/{manifest.json, prompt.md, result.json}` bundles are already being produced per `ddx agent execute-bead` attempt — 469 of them on the current repo. They contain the canonical record of what an agent was asked to do and what verdict the attempt produced. **Nothing in the web UI surfaces them today.** A user who wants to ask "why did this bead end up in this state?" has to drop to the filesystem.

Sessions (`sessions.jsonl` / sharded index per `ddx-2ceb02fa`) carry the harness/cost/token telemetry but not the verdict and not the bead linkage. Agent log streams (`agent-*.jsonl`) carry the tool-call trace but are scattered across hundreds of files. **Executions are the natural join** — they connect a bead, a session, a prompt, a verdict, and a tool-call stream.

## Ground truth

Per-execution on disk:

```
.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/
  manifest.json      { harness, model, base_rev, result_rev, verdict, bead_id, execution_dir }
  prompt.md          the prompt that was sent
  result.json        { verdict, rationale, ... }
```

Relations:
- `manifest.bead_id` → bead
- a session id is recorded in bead events (`kind:cost`, `kind:routing`) and in the agent-log stream; both can map back to an execution via correlation metadata
- tool calls live in `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` (and `agent-s-*.jsonl`), keyed by session id

## Scope

### Part 1 — GraphQL surface

- `executions(projectId, first, after, beadId?, since?, until?, verdict?, harness?)` — paginated list of execution bundles.
- `execution(id)` — one bundle, fully materialized: manifest fields, prompt markdown, result JSON, linked bead (id + title), linked session (id + harness + model + cost + token totals), tool-call stream pointer.
- `executionToolCalls(id, first, after)` — paginated tool-call stream for the execution, read lazily from the agent-log file. Matches the event shape used by the workers detail page.

Executions source of truth: directory scan of `.ddx/executions/` filtered/paginated at the resolver. Shard discipline: follow the sessions-index monthly shard pattern (`ddx-2ceb02fa`) if listing all executions grows beyond tens of thousands — per standing directive `ddx-9ce6842a`, raise the storage decision with the user before switching substrate.

### Part 2 — Sidebar + routes

- Add "Executions" to the project sidebar (`NavShell.svelte`).
- Routes:
  - `/nodes/<n>/projects/<p>/executions` — list view with filters (verdict, harness, bead, date range, free-text over prompt). Table columns: timestamp, bead, harness/model, verdict (pass/block/error), duration, cost.
  - `/nodes/<n>/projects/<p>/executions/<id>` — detail view with five tabs: **Manifest**, **Prompt** (rendered markdown), **Result** (rationale + raw JSON toggle), **Session** (harness, model, cost, token totals, link to `/sessions/<id>`), **Tool calls** (virtualized lazy-scroll list of tool_use + tool_result pairs with expandable payloads).

### Part 3 — Cross-links (make the graph real)

- **Bead detail:** new "Executions" section listing all executions for this bead with verdict + timestamp + link. Already have data: manifest.bead_id.
- **Session detail:** new "Execution" link pointing to the execution this session ran for, when the session has a linked execution.
- **Commits page:** already shows commits; if a commit was produced by an execution (result_rev == commit sha), surface the execution link.
- **Graph/Docs pages:** out of scope for this bead.

### Part 4 — Perf

- Fixture: ≥1,000 executions, per standing directive `ddx-9ce6842a`. Tool-call streams seeded for 10% of them.
- Targets: list view p95 ≤ 200ms HTTP, detail view without tool calls p95 ≤ 150ms HTTP, tool-call page-1 (first 50) p95 ≤ 300ms HTTP.
- If these can't be met with filesystem scan + in-memory filter, raise DB-substrate decision with user per standing directive.

## Out of scope

- Writing new execution bundles (that's already happening).
- Executions from other projects/nodes (keep per-project for v1).
- Tool-call payload redaction / privacy. File a follow-up if relevant.
- `.claude/worktrees/` worktree lifecycle tracking — covered by companion bead per user's split request.
